title: Adonis
summary: An ancient Greek Gangrel living on an island in the river.
text:
# Adonis

## Overview

Adonis is described by [[Gregory]] as an ancient Greek Gangrel living on a river island near Prague. Gregory says Adonis has the regal aspect of an eagle and was a speaker of his clan in Rome before the Republic.

## Notes

- The river island may be protected from or resistant to [[Shagra]]'s pervasive influence.

title: Adonis's Island Refuge
summary: An ancient Gangrel named Adonis lives on a river island that may be shielded from Shagra's touch.
text:
# Adonis's Island Refuge

## Overview

[[Gregory]] says he knows an ancient Greek Gangrel named [[Adonis]] who lives on an island in the river. The implication is that running water interferes with Shagra's pervasive influence, making the island a possible refuge or blind spot.

## Appears In

- [[Game 7]]

text:
# Adrastos von Orlok

![Adrastos von Orlok](/wiki/media/Adrastos-von-Orlok.png)

## Overview

Adrastos von Orlok imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Elders, Vampire). Vampire.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Vampire
- **Foundry folder:** NPC's / Elders
- **Foundry name note:** Tori Prim
- **Original Foundry name:** Adrastos von Orlok (Tori Prim)
- **Advantages:** Willpower 5; Blood Pool 0/15

## Background

Adrastos von Orlok, an 8th-generation Toreador, was embraced 1102 in the heart of Prague's noble circles. Born to an aristocratic family, he was known for his exceptional beauty and his patronage of the arts. His embrace came at the hands of a Toreador elder who admired his charm and cultural influence. This elder, seeing in Adrastos the perfect combination of ambition and refinement, brought him into the fold of the Toreador Clan, forever binding him to the immortal world. After his embrace, Adrastos quickly adapted to his new life, using his Toreador abilities to continue his rise in society, even as a vampire, building a powerful reputation among Prague's elite.

Residing within the Old Town district of Prague, Adrastos maintains a luxurious haven in a grand estate, surrounded by beauty and splendor. His home, filled with fine art, sculptures, and lavish decorations, is not only a reflection of his Toreador nature but also a place of political maneuvering. Nestled in the center of Prague's cultural and political hub, Adrastos is perfectly positioned to influence both the mortal and vampiric spheres of power. His estate is also where he secretly conducts his manipulations, using the shadows of its beautiful halls to control those who enter, all while playing the role of a cultured noble.

Beneath his elegant exterior lies a ruthless schemer who has been manipulating a small group of Salubri. Adrastos pretends to offer them protection from the Tremere, who hunt the Salubri to extinction. Using his Toreador charm, he convinces them they are safe under his wing. However, in secret, Adrastos enslaves them through the Blood Bond, using his vitae to ensure their loyalty while subtly breaking their will. He takes advantage of their trust, turning them into tools for his own advancement, all the while pretending to shield them from the Tremere's wrath.

The relationship between Adrastos and the Tremere is one of calculated balance. Publicly, he maintains a neutral or even friendly relationship with the clan, ensuring that his dealings with the Salubri remain hidden. He plays both sides of the conflict, using his knowledge of the Salubri's whereabouts to occasionally feed information to the Tremere, thus ensuring his continued survival and gaining small political favors. However, his true loyalty is to no one but himself. He understands that the Tremere's power in Prague is significant, but he views them as both a threat and a resource to be manipulated for his own gain.

Adrastos' ultimate goal is to dominate Prague's vampiric society and rise to greater power. He sees the Salubri as valuable pawns in this ambition--not only as leverage against the Tremere but also for their unique abilities. The ancient knowledge of the Salubri, particularly their healing Discipline, **Valeren**, fascinates Adrastos, and he uses it for his personal benefit. While he has no interest in the altruistic aspects of their powers, he seeks to harness their techniques for his own purposes, including the ability to dominate and manipulate others more effectively.

Despite his meticulous planning, Adrastos' manipulations have their dangers. His vanity as a Toreador makes him vulnerable to flattery and indulgence, and his constant betrayal of others breeds mistrust among his peers. Even those bound to him by blood are at risk of turning against him should they ever discover the truth of his schemes. This makes his position precarious, particularly if the Tremere or the Salubri realize they have been deceived. Adrastos thrives in the web of lies he has spun, but it is a delicate balance that could collapse with one wrong move.

## Attributes

| Trait | Rating |
|---|---:|
| Strength | 2 |
| Dexterity | 3 |
| Stamina | 1 |
| Charisma | 4 |
| Manipulation | 4 |
| Appearance | 2 |
| Perception | 2 |
| Intelligence | 4 |
| Wits | 4 |

## Abilities

**Talents:** Alertness 1, Awareness 2, Empathy 4, Intimidation 4, Leadership 2, Subterfuge 3

**Skills:** Etiquette 3, Performance 1

**Knowledges:** Academics 1, Enigmas 2, Law 2, Occult 1, Politics 3

## Disciplines and powers

- **Auspex** (0)
  The most discerning vampires see in the dark, hear whispers on the wind and the crash of rats' feet stomping a hundred yards away. They feel the tendrils of fog and know how close warm bodies are. Supernatural levels of sensitivity and awareness blossom further as the vampire develops his gifts. Eventually, he can see the colors of the souls around him, read minds, and pierce the illusions made by other monsters.For more on using Auspex to pierce Obfuscate and othermental illusions, see the sidebar See the Unseen (p. 195).Sensory details are of the utmost importance when using Auspex. Storytellers should always add additional and useful sensory details with increasingly successful rolls.
- **Celerity** (0)
  Characters add their Celerity rating to their Dexterity-based dice pools, including their Initiativeratings. Add her Celerity rating to the number of yards or meters she moves in a turn. Additionally, they may reflexively spend one point ofblood to ignore difficulty penalties for multiple actions up to their Celerity dots for the turn. For example, a character with Celerity 3 makes three actions before suffering +1 difficulty for multiple actions. This does not mitigate the dice pool reduction, however. A numberof these additional actions equal to half their Celerity (round up) may be attack actions. See p. 322 for rules on multiple actions. Lastly, by spending one blood point, she may multi-ply her movement speed by 1 plus her Celerity dots. Incombat, this lasts one turn. Otherwise, it lasts the scene.
- **Presence** (0)
  Anyone can resist Presence for one scene by spending a Willpower point and then succeeding on a Willpower roll (difficulty 8). The affected individual must keep spending Willpower points until he is no longer in the presence ofthe vampire (or, in the case of Summon, until the effectwears off). Vampires three or more Generations lower than the wielder need only spend a single Willpower to ignore the Presence for an entire night. In this case, no Willpower roll is needed.
- **Heighten Senses** (1)
  This power costs nothing, is reflexive, and requires no rolls to use. When activated, any roll related to the sense's use is decreased in difficulty by the character's Auspex dots. A character may utilize one sense at a time, or all of them in any combination she likes.
  
  This power does not allow the character to see inperfect darkness as with Eyes of the Beast(see p. 240).
- **Read the Soul** (2)
  The soul-gazer must actively and attentively observe her subject, allowing the sense of the soul to washover her. At the moment she chooses to interpret the state of the soul, the character's player rolls Perception+ Empathy (difficulty 8), with each success granting thecharacter more pieces of information about their subject.Consider the chart on page 196 for ideas as to what information the Storyteller might communicate. A failure indicates that the character was unable to interpret the current state of the soul. A botch indicates misinformation or a wrong interpretation. Soul-gazing is not a science.The chart on the right consists of colors and their suggested interpretations. Interpretations through other senses are also possible. Players and Storytellers should be encouraged to create charts for their characters, unique in its sensory details.
- **Awe** (1)
  The player chooses what impression she wants to make. The impression should be simple, something that can be summed up in one or two words. Honest, competent, or dangerous are good examples.The player then spends a blood point and rolls Charisma + Performance (difficulty 7). The number of successes rolled determines how many people are affected, as noted on the chart below. If there are more people present than the character can influence, Awe affects those with lower Willpower ratings first. The power stays in effect for the remainder of the scene or until the character chooses to drop it.
  
  Those affected can use Willpower points to overcome the effect, but must continue spending Willpower every scene for as long as they remain in the same area as the vampire. As soon as an individual spends a number of Willpower points equal to the successes rolled, he shakes off Awe completely and remains unaffected for the rest of the night. The character gains additional dice equal to her dots in Presence to any action that would foster or take advantage of the chosen reaction against an affected individual.
- **Dread Gaze** (2)
  The player rolls Charisma + Intimidation (difficulty equal to the victim's Wits + Courage). Success indicates the victim is cowed, while failure means the target is startled but not terrified by the sight. Three or more successes means he runs away in abject fear; victims who have nowhere to run claw at the walls, hoping to dig a way out rather than face the vampire. Moreover, each success subtracts one from the target's action dice pools for the next turn. The character may attempt Dread Gazeonce per turn against a single target, though she may also perform it as an extended action, adding her successes in order to subjugate the target completely. Once the target loses enough dice that he cannot perform any action, he's so shaken and terrified that he curls up on the ground and weeps. Failure during the extended action means the attempt falters. The character loses all her collected successes and can start over next turn, while the victim may act normally again.
- **Entrancement** (3)
  The player spends a blood point and rolls Appearance + Empathy (difficulty equal to the target's current Willpower points); the number of successes determines how long the subject is Entranced, as per the chart on page 237. (Subjects can still spend Willpower to temporarily resist, like any other Presence power.) The Storyteller may wish to make the roll instead, since the character is never certain of the strength of her hold on the victim. The vampire may try to keep the subject under her thrall with further blood and further rolls.
- **Summon** (4)
  The player spends a blood point and rolls Charisma + Subterfuge. The base difficulty is 5; this increases to difficulty 7 if the vampire only met the subject briefly. If the character used Presence successfully onthe target in the past, this difficulty drops to 4, but if theattempt was unsuccessful, the difficulty rises to 8. The vampire can allocate successes either to make the subject make haste or to prolong the duration of the Summoning.
  
  You can also spend extra successes to prolong the Summoning. If you summon a mortal, one success can be spent on prolonging the Summoning by one day. For vampires, two successes need to be spent to prolong the Summoning by one night. Both mortals and vampires alike will seek rest and shelter when they feel that it is needed.
- **Majesty** (5)
  No roll is required on the part of the vampire, but she must spend a Willpower point. A subject must make a Courage roll (difficulty equal to the vampire's Charisma + Intimidation, to a maximum of 9) if he wishesto be rude or simply contrary to the vampire. Success allows the individual to act normally for the moment, although he feels the weight of the vampire's displeasure crushing down on him. A subject who fails theroll aborts his intended action and even goes to absurd engths to humble himself before the vampire, no matter who else is watching. The effects of Majesty last one scene.
- **Kingmaker** (6)
  The player chooses a target within line of sight and earshot of the vampire. The player then spends a blood point and rolls Manipulation + Empathy. The normal difficulty is 7, but if the vampire and target know each other well, the difficulty is reduced to 5. A success lets the vampire add as many bonus dice as she had dots in Presence to all the target's Social rolls. Every 2 successes also lets the vampire reduce the difficulty on the target's Social rolls. A failure means that the vampire can no longer assist the target. The power remains in effect for a scene, or until the vampire decided to no longer uphold it.
- **Background: Domain 2** (0)
- **Background: Generation 4** (0)
- **Background: Influence 2** (0)
- **Background: Resources 3** (0)
- **Background: Status 3** (0)

## Traits

- **Legerdemain** (0)
- **Archery** (0)
- **Commerce** (0)
- **Ride** (0)
- **Hearth Wisdom** (0)
- **Seneschal** (0)
- **Theology** (0)

## Source

- Foundry world archive: aoe.zip
- Actor ID: Ue3ca7pC7GouxIxx
- Actor folder: NPC's / Elders

## Game 3-4 notes

- In [[Game 3]], a figure at [[Public Elysium]] is addressed in the transcript as Andreas or Adrastos von Orlok / Master Orlok.
- The coterie finds him unimpressed during the Elysium sequence after Sabina's abduction. The exact transcript name should be reviewed.

## Game 5-8 notes

- In [[Game 5]], Adrastos explains [[Tremere as Prague Power Brokers]].
- In [[Game 8]], the coterie discusses him as a possible elder target in the [[Elder Targets Plot]].

## Chronicle 1 finale notes

- Destroyed during the [[Orlok Assassination]] at the [[Three Keys Tavern]].
- His death helps trigger the [[Night of Chaos]] and leaves Toreador rivals competing for position.

title: Adrian
summary: A Tremere figure, probably the Prague regent, connected to a retroactive Embrace permission request.
text:
# Adrian

## Overview

Adrian is a Tremere figure discussed at [[Public Elysium]]. The transcript suggests he may be the Tremere regent of Prague.

## Appears In

- [[Game 3]] - named in relation to a boon spent for permission for an Embrace.

## Notes

- The permission appears to have been requested after the fact.
- This makes the coterie more suspicious that [[Sabina Premysl]] was taken into Clan Tremere, though that remains unconfirmed.

title: Alessandro di Ravello

text:
# Alessandro di Ravello

![Alessandro di Ravello](/wiki/media/Alessandro-di-Ravello.png)

## Overview

Alessandro di Ravello imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Elders, Vampire). Vampire.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Vampire
- **Foundry folder:** NPC's / Elders
- **Foundry name note:** Lasombra
- **Original Foundry name:** Alessandro di Ravello (Lasombra)
- **Advantages:** Willpower 5; Blood Pool 0/30

## Background

Alessandro di Ravello, a 6th-generation Lasombra, was embraced in 920 AD in southern Italy during a time when the Lasombra Clan was deeply entwined with the Catholic Church. Before his embrace, Alessandro was a priest with strong ties to the Church, known for his political acumen and personal charisma. His sire, an influential Lasombra elder, recognized his potential to manipulate the Church for the clan's benefit. Alessandro thrived after his embrace, wielding both mortal and vampiric influence within the Church's hierarchy. For centuries, he became a shadowy force behind bishops, cardinals, and even popes, guiding Christian policy to align with his own ambitions and securing his place among the Catholic Lasombra.

Alessandro's control over the Church was built on subtlety and precision. He believed that by influencing the spiritual center of the mortal world, he could ensure his clan's dominance in both religious and secular affairs. Through his elder mastery of dominate, he played a long game, bending priests and religious leaders to his will while remaining hidden behind the scenes. His position was one of immense power, not just within mortal society, but within the Lasombra Clan as well, where he was a respected elder among those who valued their deep connections to Catholicism.

However, Alessandro's influence was challenged when one of his most promising childer (and motal grandnephew) , **Giovanni di Ravello**, began to rise within the Church and vampiric circles. Giovanni, like his sire, was ambitious and cunning, but he saw opportunities that Alessandro did not. While Alessandro remained focused on the Church's traditional power structures, Giovanni aligned himself with more progressive elements within the clan and even developed ties to the rising Tremere and Ventrue factions. Over time, Giovanni began to outmaneuver his sire, gaining favor among the Lasombra elite by embracing new methods and alliances that Alessandro disdained. Their conflict came to a head when Giovanni orchestrated a coup within the Church hierarchy, outflanking Alessandro by manipulating several key cardinals into aligning with his vision, effectively cutting his sire out of the inner circles of both the Church and the clan.

Betrayed and humiliated by his own childe, Alessandro's influence crumbled. His Lasombra peers, once loyal, began to side with Giovanni, who promised them a stronger foothold in the Church's evolving political landscape. Isolated, Alessandro was forced to retreat from his power base in Italy. His loss was more than political; it was personal. Giovanni's betrayal cut deep, not just because of the loss of control, but because his childe had used Alessandro's own tactics against him. The once-great manipulator had been outplayed by his progeny, who now stood as a more favored figure within the clan's Catholic circles.

Disillusioned and seeking revenge, Alessandro fled to Prague, far from the Church's epicenter. In the relatively untamed outskirts of Christianity, he found solace in the rising **Hussite movement**, a Christian reform movement that opposed the corruption of the Catholic Church. The Hussites' radical ideas and rebellion against the Papacy mirrored Alessandro's own growing disdain for the institution that had once been the source of his power. Viewing the Hussites as a tool for revenge against the Catholic Lasombra who had abandoned him, Alessandro began secretly supporting the movement, offering resources and guidance to its leaders to weaken the Church's authority and, by extension, the influence of his rivals within the clan.

Alessandro's involvement with the Hussites is driven by a personal vendetta, not ideology. He sees the movement as a means to destabilize the Catholic Church and undermine the Catholic Lasombra faction, including his former childe Giovanni. By supporting a rebellion against the very institution he once controlled, Alessandro seeks to sow chaos in the ranks of those who betrayed him. He operates from the shadows, using his considerable powers to influence the movement's leaders while maintaining a low profile, allowing him to strike indirectly at his enemies in the Church and Lasombra Clan alike.

Now residing in a fortified manor on the outskirts of Prague, Alessandro has rebuilt a small but loyal network of followers, including disillusioned Lasombra and mortal agents. His haven is a stark contrast to the opulence he once enjoyed in Italy, reflecting his fall from grace and the darker, more vengeful path he has chosen. From this remote seat of power, Alessandro continues to manipulate the Hussite movement, using it as a weapon to erode the Church's influence in Bohemia and strike back at the Catholic faction that now sees him as a pariah. His thirst for revenge, combined with centuries of political experience, makes him a dangerous player in Prague's supernatural and mortal conflicts.

Despite his fall from favor, Alessandro remains a cunning and dangerous elder. The betrayal by Giovanni has left him more ruthless and calculating, willing to use any means necessary to regain some semblance of control and influence. His centuries of manipulating both the Church and his clan have taught him that power can shift unexpectedly, and he is determined to be the one pulling the strings in the shadows, no matter the cost. Now, with the Hussite movement as his weapon, Alessandro watches and waits, planning his next move in a centuries-old game of revenge and dominance.

This has also led him to support the current prince and with those succeses has been named his right hand of the court. (shenechal)

## Attributes

| Trait | Rating |
|---|---:|
| Strength | 2 |
| Dexterity | 4 |
| Stamina | 1 |
| Charisma | 2 |
| Manipulation | 5 |
| Appearance | 1 |
| Perception | 3 |
| Intelligence | 3 |
| Wits | 5 |

## Abilities

**Talents:** Alertness 2, Awareness 2, Empathy 3, Intimidation 4, Leadership 3, Subterfuge 2

**Skills:** Etiquette 4, Melee 1

**Knowledges:** Academics 1, Enigmas 1, Medicine 1, Occult 4

## Disciplines and powers

- **Dominate** (0)
  Cainites with Dominate embody every legend about vampire hypnotism. With a simple glance, a master of Dominate breaks wills, enslaves minds, and robs both identity and memory from her victims. While Dominate is among the fiercest weapons in a vampire's arsenal, it possesses three inherent weaknesses. First, one cannot Dominate another Cainite of thicker blood, which is to say, characters cannot use Dominate on characters of lower Generations. Second, Dominate requires eye contact, with a brief moment where the vampire'sgaze can catch a glimpse of her victim's soul. Charactersavoiding the vampire's gaze can roll Willpower, difficulty equal to the vampire's Manipulation + Intimidation. Levy a -1 to the difficulty if he tries to hide his eyes, -2 if they're obscured, or -3 if his eyes are fully covered or not at all present (ripped out, for example). Finally, the vampire must be able to communicate her wishes in a way her victim understands. If they do not share a language, she may use gestures or imagery for simple commands. Dominate difficulties are equal to the victim's current Willpower points unless otherwise noted.
- **Obtenebration** (0)
- **Potence** (0)
  For each dot of Potence the vampire possesses, add one die to all Strength-based dice pools. Spend one blood point reflexively to turn those dice into automatic successes for the turn. In hand to hand combat, this means automatic successes on the damage roll.
- **Command** (1)
  Roll Manipulation + Intimidation, with adifficulty equal to the victim's current Willpower points. The more successes achieved on this roll, the better the effect takes hold. A single success may result in the target having only a hazy impression of the command, or the command may activate infrequently, while three or more successes indicate that the command's roots probe deeper and more frequently into the subject's mind. Attempting to force a victim to perform an action that would be contrary to his Nature results in an immediate failure. Giving conflicting or indirectly harmful orders to a victim (such as commanding atarget to sleep while in the middle of a duel) adds +1 to +3 to the difficulty, depending on the severity ofthe order. A command with a difficulty of 10 or more is impossible.
- **Subjugation** (2)
  Roll Manipulation + Leadership. Thenumber of successes achieved indicates how well the instructions will hold. One success indicates a weak impression, while five or more successes indicate that your very words have been carved into the target's thoughts. This ability still cannot be used to force a target to directly harm himself or to defy his Nature. Adding a new command using Subjugation before the old one expires requires a comparison of successes betweennew and old. The command with the higher number of successes wins. In the event of a tie, the new command overrides the old.
- **The Forgetful Mind** (3)
  Roll Wits + Subterfuge. Consult the table below to determine the result.
  
  1 success Remove a single memory; lasts as long as one day.
  
  2 successes Permanently remove, but not alter, one memory.
  
  3 successes Make slight alterations to the subject's memory.
  
  4 successes Alter or remove entire scene from the subject's memory.
  
  5 successes Wholly reconstruct a period of the subject's life.
- **Conditioning** (4)
  As an extended action, roll Charisma +Leadership. The total number of successes needed is equal to 5 times the target's Self-Control or Instinct. A subject can shake off Conditioning if he is separated entirely from his master for weeks equal to the subject's permanent Willpower. Any contact in the mean time reasserts her dominance instantly. Provided he avoids the vampire for the given period of time, the subject retains his former individuality. A Conditioned character can be Dominated without eye contact. The vampire can use other Dominate powers on him at -2 difficulty. Other characters attempting to Dominate him use his Willpower or his master's, whichever is higher. He can spend a point of Willpower to reinterpret another's Dominate command to be contrary to his Nature.
- **Possession** (5)
  he vampire and the target have a clashof wills. The vampire rolls Charisma + Intimidation and the subject rolls Willpower, each at difficulty 7. The net successes reduce the target's Willpower. When the target's Willpower is reduced to 0, the vampire invades her target's mind. Roll Manipulation + Intimidation and consult the chart below to determine the strength of her grip on the target's body. If the Cainite's original body is destroyed, she may stay in her host body indefinitely, though she remains trapped in the weak prison of flesh.At each sunrise, she must roll Courage at difficulty 8 or be forcefully ejected from the host. If she fails this roll, her soul falls into the astral plane and is permanently lost. While walking about in a mortal's shell, the vampire cannot be "re-Embraced." Any attempt to do so sends her directly to the Final Death.
- **Chain the Psyche** (6)
  Spend a blood point and roll Manipulation + Intimidation against a difficulty of the target's Stamina + Empathy. Each success is a turn the target spends writhing, screaming, and weeping, mechanically unable to take any other action. Each such turn causes one level of unsoakable bashing damage.
- **Shadow Play** (1)
  Spend one blood point to add one die to a character's Intimidation and Stealth dice pools for the remainder of the scene. While this power is active, characters may also move, change, and animate shadows, which can include separating them from their original source. This can include moving shadows in such a way as to allow the character to see better, which reduces any penalty incurred by darkness by the amount of the character's Obtenebration rating. Mortals find this power terrifying to behold and must pass a Courage check of 7. If they fail, their Social dice pools are reduced by one when they see this power in use.
- **Nocturne** (2)
  To use this ability, roll Manipulation + Occult with a difficulty of 7. Success results in a cloud of impenetrable blackness 10 feet (or three meters) in diameter anywhere within 50 yards/meters of the character. Each additional success may add ten feet/three meters to diameter of the cloud, at the caster's discretion. The caster may choose to affect an area not in his line of sight, but at a difficulty of 9, and they must spend one point of blood. All Perception pools are reduced by five dice and the difficulties of all rolls from characters within the cloud are increased by two. Characters with supernatural methods of enhancing Perception, such as Heightened Senses (Auspex 1) or the Merit Darksight take only a two dice penalty to their Perception. Additionally, the cloud is a breach of the Abyss into the material world; let loose into the world of the living, it will attempt to drain the life from any beings caught in it. Characters inside the cloud have their Stamina pools reduced by two. For mortals, if this reduces their Stamina pool to zero or lower, they suffocate.
- **Arms of Ahriman** (3)
  Using this ability requires a roll of Manipulation + Occult, difficulty 7. Each success allows a character to create one tentacle six feet in length that must originate in a source of shadow within 20 feet/ 7 meters of the caster, with Strength and Dexterity ratings equal to the caster's Obtenebration rating. If the caster desires, they may spend blood to increase Strength and Dexterity at the cost of one blood per point increased; they may also increase the length by spending one blood per six feet/ two meters of length per tentacle.
  
  Each tentacle has four health levels, does not take wound penalties, and soaks nonaggravated damage by rolling Stamina + Fortitude equal to the caster's rating. Aggravated damage may not be soaked. Tentacles may attack characters by using Dexterity to attack for Strength + 1 points of bashing damage. Controlling the tentacles requires a moderate amount of concentration on the part of the caster. The tentacles receive one independent action; they act in concert as the caster directs.
- **Obedience** (1)
  As a permanent upgrade to her abilities, the vampire no longer needs to make eye contact with those she wishes to subjugate. All this requires is momentary physical contact where in skin must touch skin.
- **Flaw: Vengeful** (0)
  Revenge drives you. Its hot,constant presence motivates your every move. Work out with the Storyteller what wrongdoing has pushed you into such a state. Whenever you encounter the source of your ire, you are compelled to pursue it, but you may spend aWillpower point to ignore this urge.
- **Merit: Multi-Lingual** (0)
  Through patience, study, and/or immersion, you are able to speak another language or languages with native fluency. This represents a full command over the language. For each dot of Intelligence your character possesses, you may know one other language. You may also use this Merit to reflect secret codes, cants, jargon, or sign language as well. Additional languages increase the cost of this Merit by one point per language.
- **Flaw: Prey Exclusion - Clergy** (0)
  For whatever reason you choose, you refuse to feed upon a certain type of person. If, by some unfortunate circumstance, you prey upon such a person, you frenzy immediately and must make a Degeneration check. Ventrue may only take this Flaw for a subset of their particular feeding stock. For example, aVentrue might feed from "virgin men," but will not feed from Jewish virgin men.
- **Flaw: Rivalry 2** (0)
  Someone hates you so much that they cannot bear to leave you alone. The scale of this Flaw is similar to that of the Enemy Flaw (see above), but your Rival is not interested in seeing you dead. Rather, a Rival desires only to make your unlife as troublesome and hellish as possible. At lower point value, the Rivalmay also be a friend who arrives at precisely the worst moment to steal your thunder or make you embarrassed. At higher levels, your Rival loses sleep planning how to make your life miserable.
- **Flaw: Enemy 2** (0)
  In your travels, you have earnedthe enmity of a person or organization. The severity andscope of this hatred is reflected by the rating of the Flaw. At1 point, your enemy is an aggrieved peer or the irritation ofan organization. You may be undercut, inconvenienced, orframed for petty crimes, but nothing so severe as attempts on your unlife. At 3 points, your enemy is your superior, orthe anger of an important group. They make regular effortsto thwart your plans, threaten your allies, and undermineyour standing with your clan and Road. At this level, yourenemy may consider granting you the Final Death. Workthis out with your Storyteller. At 5 points, your enemy is anelder or an entire organization, such as the ruling family ofyour nation or the Catholic Church. Your death is not out of the question. None of your associates are safe. Everywhereyou go, you can expect your enemy to be waiting. Whathave you done to make someone so powerful so angry?
- **Merit: Lucky 3** (0)
  Fortune smiles upon you. The variable point costs reflect just how much you have been blessed by luck. At 1 point, the trifles of everyday life rarely seem to affect you. At 3 points, you are a gambling den's nightmare. At 5 points, you live a truly charmed life,misfortune seemingly passing you by at every turn. Foreach point invested in this Merit, you gain one reroll perday. You may reroll any roll except a roll to resist falling from your Road, but you must take the second result.
- **Presence** (0); V20 193
  Presence is the Discipline of emotional manipulation. Vampires with this power can inspire passionate fervor or unreasoning terror in mortals and Kindred alike. In addition, unlike most Disciplines, some of Presence's powers can be used on entire crowds at one time. Presence can transcend race, religion, gender, class, and (most importantly) supernatural nature. As such, this subtle power is one of the most useful Disciplines a vampire can possess.
  
  Anyone can resist Presence for one scene by spending a Willpower point and succeeding on a Willpower roll (difficulty 8), but the affected individual must keep spending points until he is no longer in the presence of the vampire (or, in the case of Summon, until the effect wears off). Vampires three or more Generations lower than the wielder need only spend a single Willpower to ignore the Presence for an entire night and need not roll Willpower to do so.
  
  The major drawback of Presence is that it controls only the emotions. It causes others to feel a certain way toward the vampire, but does not give her outright control over them. While people weigh strongly the orders that the vampire declares, their minds are still their own. Suicidal or ridiculous directives don't sound any more sensible just because the person giving them is unusually fascinating. Still, inspired eloquence or significant wealth used in combination with this Discipline can enable the vampire to urge others along a desired course.
  
  The Brujah, Followers of Set, Toreador, and Ventrue Clans are all adept in this Discipline. The Ventrue are arguably the most skilled with its application, however, due to their ability to use Presence and Dominate in efficient combination.
- **●●●● Summon** (4); V20 195
  The player spends a blood point and rolls Charisma + Subterfuge. The base difficulty is 5; this increases to difficulty 7 if the subject was met only briefly. If the character used Presence successfully on the target in the past, this difficulty drops to 4, but if the attempt was unsuccessful, the difficulty rises to 8.
  
  The number of successes indicates the subject's speed and attitude in responding:
  
  **Successes**
  **Result**
  
  Botch
  Subject cannot be Summoned by that vampire for the rest of the story.
  
  Failure
  Subject cannot be Summoned by that vampire for the rest of the night.
  
  1 success
  Subject approaches slowly and hesitantly.
  
  2 successes
  Subject approaches reluctantly and is easily thwarted by obstacles.
  
  3 successes
  Subject approaches with reasonable speed.
  
  4 successes
  Subject comes with haste, overcoming any obstacles in his way.
  
  5 successes
  Subject rushes to the vampire, doing anything to get to her.
- **●●● Entrancement** (3); V20 195
  The player spends a blood point and rolls Appearance + Empathy (difficulty equal to the target's current Willpower points); the number of successes determines how long the subject is Entranced, as per the chart below. (Subjects can still spend Willpower to temporarily resist, like any other Presence power.) The Storyteller may wish to make the roll instead, since the character is never certain of the strength of her hold on the victim. The vampire may try to keep the subject under her thrall, but can do so only after the initial Entrancement wears off. Attempting this power while Entrancement is already in operation has no effect.
  
  **Successes**
  **Result**
  
  Botch
  Subject cannot be entranced for the rest of the story.
  
  Failure
  Subject cannot be entranced for the rest of the night.
  
  1 success
  One hour
  
  2 successes
  One day
  
  3 successes
  One week
  
  4 successes
  One month
  
  5 successes
  One year
- **●● Dread Gaze** (2); V20 194
  The player rolls Charisma + Intimidation (difficulty equal to the victim's Wits + Courage). Success indicates the victim is cowed, while failure means the target is startled but not terrified by the sight. Three or more successes means he runs away in abject fear; victims who have nowhere to run claw at the walls, hoping to dig a way out rather than face the vampire. Moreover, each success subtracts one from the target's action dice pools next turn.
  
  The character may attempt Dread Gaze once per turn against a single target, though she may also perform it as an extended action, adding her successes in order to subjugate the target completely. Once the target loses enough dice that he cannot perform any action, he's so shaken and terrified that he curls up on the ground and weeps. Failure during the extended action means the attempt falters. The character loses all her collected successes and can start over next turn, while the victim may act normally again.
  
  A botch at any time indicates the target is not at all impressed -- perhaps even finding the vampire's antics comical -- and remains immune to any further uses of Presence by the character for the rest of the story.
- **● Awe** (1); V20 193
  The player spends a blood point and rolls Charisma + Performance (difficulty 7). The number of successes rolled determines how many people are affected, as noted on the chart below. If there are more people present than the character can influence, Awe affects those with lower Willpower ratings first. The power stays in effect for the remainder of the scene or until the character chooses to drop it.
  
  **Successes**
  **Result**
  
  1 Successes
  One person
  
  2 Successes
  Two people
  
  3 Successes
  Six people
  
  4 Successes
  20 people
  
  5 Successes
  Everyone in the vampire's immediate vicinity (an entire auditorium, a mob)
  
  Those affected can use Willpower points to overcome the effect, but must continue spending Willpower every scene for as long as they remain in the same area as the vampire. As soon as an individual spends a number of Willpower points equal to the successes rolled, he shakes off the Awe completely and remains unaffected for the rest of the night.

## Traits

- **Legerdemain** (0)
- **Archery** (0)
- **Commerce** (0)
- **Ride** (0)
- **Hearth Wisdom** (0)
- **Seneschal** (0)
- **Theology** (0)

- Foundry world archive: aoe.zip
- Actor ID: QhK2h487eaC6Y2cJ

## Session notes

- [[Game 1]] - summons the coterie to [[Millie's Tavern]], announces [[Radomir Pavlovich]]'s destruction, and gives the group authority to investigate on behalf of the elders.

## Game 5-8 notes

- In [[Game 8]], [[Father Zemislav]] begins spreading the [[Alessandro Hussite Extremist Rumor]] to weaken Alessandro politically.
- Alessandro is discussed as a difficult but significant elder target in the [[Elder Targets Plot]].
playerText:
# Alessandro di Ravello

![Alessandro di Ravello](/wiki/media/Alessandro-di-Ravello.png)

## Overview

Alessandro di Ravello imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Elders, Vampire). Vampire.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Vampire
- **Foundry folder:** NPC's / Elders
- **Foundry name note:** Lasombra
- **Original Foundry name:** Alessandro di Ravello (Lasombra)
- **Advantages:** Willpower 5; Blood Pool 0/30

## Background

Alessandro di Ravello, a 6th-generation Lasombra, was embraced in 920 AD in southern Italy during a time when the Lasombra Clan was deeply entwined with the Catholic Church. Before his embrace, Alessandro was a priest with strong ties to the Church, known for his political acumen and personal charisma. His sire, an influential Lasombra elder, recognized his potential to manipulate the Church for the clan's benefit. Alessandro thrived after his embrace, wielding both mortal and vampiric influence within the Church's hierarchy. For centuries, he became a shadowy force behind bishops, cardinals, and even popes, guiding Christian policy to align with his own ambitions and securing his place among the Catholic Lasombra.

Alessandro's control over the Church was built on subtlety and precision. He believed that by influencing the spiritual center of the mortal world, he could ensure his clan's dominance in both religious and secular affairs. Through his elder mastery of dominate, he played a long game, bending priests and religious leaders to his will while remaining hidden behind the scenes. His position was one of immense power, not just within mortal society, but within the Lasombra Clan as well, where he was a respected elder among those who valued their deep connections to Catholicism.

However, Alessandro's influence was challenged when one of his most promising childer (and motal grandnephew) , **Giovanni di Ravello**, began to rise within the Church and vampiric circles. Giovanni, like his sire, was ambitious and cunning, but he saw opportunities that Alessandro did not. While Alessandro remained focused on the Church's traditional power structures, Giovanni aligned himself with more progressive elements within the clan and even developed ties to the rising Tremere and Ventrue factions. Over time, Giovanni began to outmaneuver his sire, gaining favor among the Lasombra elite by embracing new methods and alliances that Alessandro disdained. Their conflict came to a head when Giovanni orchestrated a coup within the Church hierarchy, outflanking Alessandro by manipulating several key cardinals into aligning with his vision, effectively cutting his sire out of the inner circles of both the Church and the clan.

Betrayed and humiliated by his own childe, Alessandro's influence crumbled. His Lasombra peers, once loyal, began to side with Giovanni, who promised them a stronger foothold in the Church's evolving political landscape. Isolated, Alessandro was forced to retreat from his power base in Italy. His loss was more than political; it was personal. Giovanni's betrayal cut deep, not just because of the loss of control, but because his childe had used Alessandro's own tactics against him. The once-great manipulator had been outplayed by his progeny, who now stood as a more favored figure within the clan's Catholic circles.

Disillusioned and seeking revenge, Alessandro fled to Prague, far from the Church's epicenter. In the relatively untamed outskirts of Christianity, he found solace in the rising **Hussite movement**, a Christian reform movement that opposed the corruption of the Catholic Church. The Hussites' radical ideas and rebellion against the Papacy mirrored Alessandro's own growing disdain for the institution that had once been the source of his power. Viewing the Hussites as a tool for revenge against the Catholic Lasombra who had abandoned him, Alessandro began secretly supporting the movement, offering resources and guidance to its leaders to weaken the Church's authority and, by extension, the influence of his rivals within the clan.

Alessandro's involvement with the Hussites is driven by a personal vendetta, not ideology. He sees the movement as a means to destabilize the Catholic Church and undermine the Catholic Lasombra faction, including his former childe Giovanni. By supporting a rebellion against the very institution he once controlled, Alessandro seeks to sow chaos in the ranks of those who betrayed him. He operates from the shadows, using his considerable powers to influence the movement's leaders while maintaining a low profile, allowing him to strike indirectly at his enemies in the Church and Lasombra Clan alike.

Now residing in a fortified manor on the outskirts of Prague, Alessandro has rebuilt a small but loyal network of followers, including disillusioned Lasombra and mortal agents. His haven is a stark contrast to the opulence he once enjoyed in Italy, reflecting his fall from grace and the darker, more vengeful path he has chosen. From this remote seat of power, Alessandro continues to manipulate the Hussite movement, using it as a weapon to erode the Church's influence in Bohemia and strike back at the Catholic faction that now sees him as a pariah. His thirst for revenge, combined with centuries of political experience, makes him a dangerous player in Prague's supernatural and mortal conflicts.

Despite his fall from favor, Alessandro remains a cunning and dangerous elder. The betrayal by Giovanni has left him more ruthless and calculating, willing to use any means necessary to regain some semblance of control and influence. His centuries of manipulating both the Church and his clan have taught him that power can shift unexpectedly, and he is determined to be the one pulling the strings in the shadows, no matter the cost. Now, with the Hussite movement as his weapon, Alessandro watches and waits, planning his next move in a centuries-old game of revenge and dominance.

This has also led him to support the current prince and with those succeses has been named his right hand of the court. (shenechal)

- **Conditioning** (4)
  As an extended action, roll Charisma +Leadership. The total number of successes needed is equal to 5 times the target's Self-Control or Instinct. A subject can shake off Conditioning if he is separated entirely from his master for weeks equal to the subject's permanent Willpower. Any contact in the mean time reasserts her dominance instantly. Provided he avoids the vampire for the given period of time, the subject retains his former individuality. A Conditioned character can be Dominated without eye contact. The vampire can use other Dominate powers on him at -2 difficulty. Other characters attempting to Dominate him use his Willpower or his master's, whichever is higher. He can spend a point of Willpower to reinterpret another's Dominate command to be contrary to his Nature.
- **Possession** (5)
  he vampire and the target have a clashof wills. The vampire rolls Charisma + Intimidation and the subject rolls Willpower, each at difficulty 7. The net successes reduce the target's Willpower. When the target's Willpower is reduced to 0, the vampire invades her target's mind. Roll Manipulation + Intimidation and consult the chart below to determine the strength of her grip on the target's body. If the Cainite's original body is destroyed, she may stay in her host body indefinitely, though she remains trapped in the weak prison of flesh.At each sunrise, she must roll Courage at difficulty 8 or be forcefully ejected from the host. If she fails this roll, her soul falls into the astral plane and is permanently lost. While walking about in a mortal's shell, the vampire cannot be "re-Embraced." Any attempt to do so sends her directly to the Final Death.
- **Chain the Psyche** (6)
  Spend a blood point and roll Manipulation + Intimidation against a difficulty of the target's Stamina + Empathy. Each success is a turn the target spends writhing, screaming, and weeping, mechanically unable to take any other action. Each such turn causes one level of unsoakable bashing damage.
- **Shadow Play** (1)
  Spend one blood point to add one die to a character's Intimidation and Stealth dice pools for the remainder of the scene. While this power is active, characters may also move, change, and animate shadows, which can include separating them from their original source. This can include moving shadows in such a way as to allow the character to see better, which reduces any penalty incurred by darkness by the amount of the character's Obtenebration rating. Mortals find this power terrifying to behold and must pass a Courage check of 7. If they fail, their Social dice pools are reduced by one when they see this power in use.
- **Nocturne** (2)
  To use this ability, roll Manipulation + Occult with a difficulty of 7. Success results in a cloud of impenetrable blackness 10 feet (or three meters) in diameter anywhere within 50 yards/meters of the character. Each additional success may add ten feet/three meters to diameter of the cloud, at the caster's discretion. The caster may choose to affect an area not in his line of sight, but at a difficulty of 9, and they must spend one point of blood. All Perception pools are reduced by five dice and the difficulties of all rolls from characters within the cloud are increased by two. Characters with supernatural methods of enhancing Perception, such as Heightened Senses (Auspex 1) or the Merit Darksight take only a two dice penalty to their Perception. Additionally, the cloud is a breach of the Abyss into the material world; let loose into the world of the living, it will attempt to drain the life from any beings caught in it. Characters inside the cloud have their Stamina pools reduced by two. For mortals, if this reduces their Stamina pool to zero or lower, they suffocate.
- **Arms of Ahriman** (3)
  Using this ability requires a roll of Manipulation + Occult, difficulty 7. Each success allows a character to create one tentacle six feet in length that must originate in a source of shadow within 20 feet/ 7 meters of the caster, with Strength and Dexterity ratings equal to the caster's Obtenebration rating. If the caster desires, they may spend blood to increase Strength and Dexterity at the cost of one blood per point increased; they may also increase the length by spending one blood per six feet/ two meters of length per tentacle.
  
  Each tentacle has four health levels, does not take wound penalties, and soaks nonaggravated damage by rolling Stamina + Fortitude equal to the caster's rating. Aggravated damage may not be soaked. Tentacles may attack characters by using Dexterity to attack for Strength + 1 points of bashing damage. Controlling the tentacles requires a moderate amount of concentration on the part of the caster. The tentacles receive one independent action; they act in concert as the caster directs.
- **Obedience** (1)
  As a permanent upgrade to her abilities, the vampire no longer needs to make eye contact with those she wishes to subjugate. All this requires is momentary physical contact where in skin must touch skin.
- **Flaw: Vengeful** (0)
  Revenge drives you. Its hot,constant presence motivates your every move. Work out with the Storyteller what wrongdoing has pushed you into such a state. Whenever you encounter the source of your ire, you are compelled to pursue it, but you may spend aWillpower point to ignore this urge.
- **Merit: Multi-Lingual** (0)
  Through patience, study, and/or immersion, you are able to speak another language or languages with native fluency. This represents a full command over the language. For each dot of Intelligence your character possesses, you may know one other language. You may also use this Merit to reflect secret codes, cants, jargon, or sign language as well. Additional languages increase the cost of this Merit by one point per language.
- **Flaw: Prey Exclusion - Clergy** (0)
  For whatever reason you choose, you refuse to feed upon a certain type of person. If, by some unfortunate circumstance, you prey upon such a person, you frenzy immediately and must make a Degeneration check. Ventrue may only take this Flaw for a subset of their particular feeding stock. For example, aVentrue might feed from "virgin men," but will not feed from Jewish virgin men.
- **Flaw: Rivalry 2** (0)
  Someone hates you so much that they cannot bear to leave you alone. The scale of this Flaw is similar to that of the Enemy Flaw (see above), but your Rival is not interested in seeing you dead. Rather, a Rival desires only to make your unlife as troublesome and hellish as possible. At lower point value, the Rivalmay also be a friend who arrives at precisely the worst moment to steal your thunder or make you embarrassed. At higher levels, your Rival loses sleep planning how to make your life miserable.
- **Flaw: Enemy 2** (0)
  In your travels, you have earnedthe enmity of a person or organization. The severity andscope of this hatred is reflected by the rating of the Flaw. At1 point, your enemy is an aggrieved peer or the irritation ofan organization. You may be undercut, inconvenienced, orframed for petty crimes, but nothing so severe as attempts on your unlife. At 3 points, your enemy is your superior, orthe anger of an important group. They make regular effortsto thwart your plans, threaten your allies, and undermineyour standing with your clan and Road. At this level, yourenemy may consider granting you the Final Death. Workthis out with your Storyteller. At 5 points, your enemy is anelder or an entire organization, such as the ruling family ofyour nation or the Catholic Church. Your death is not out of the question. None of your associates are safe. Everywhereyou go, you can expect your enemy to be waiting. Whathave you done to make someone so powerful so angry?
- **Merit: Lucky 3** (0)
  Fortune smiles upon you. The variable point costs reflect just how much you have been blessed by luck. At 1 point, the trifles of everyday life rarely seem to affect you. At 3 points, you are a gambling den's nightmare. At 5 points, you live a truly charmed life,misfortune seemingly passing you by at every turn. Foreach point invested in this Merit, you gain one reroll perday. You may reroll any roll except a roll to resist falling from your Road, but you must take the second result.
- **Presence** (0); V20 193
  Presence is the Discipline of emotional manipulation. Vampires with this power can inspire passionate fervor or unreasoning terror in mortals and Kindred alike. In addition, unlike most Disciplines, some of Presence's powers can be used on entire crowds at one time. Presence can transcend race, religion, gender, class, and (most importantly) supernatural nature. As such, this subtle power is one of the most useful Disciplines a vampire can possess.
  
  Anyone can resist Presence for one scene by spending a Willpower point and succeeding on a Willpower roll (difficulty 8), but the affected individual must keep spending points until he is no longer in the presence of the vampire (or, in the case of Summon, until the effect wears off). Vampires three or more Generations lower than the wielder need only spend a single Willpower to ignore the Presence for an entire night and need not roll Willpower to do so.
  
  The major drawback of Presence is that it controls only the emotions. It causes others to feel a certain way toward the vampire, but does not give her outright control over them. While people weigh strongly the orders that the vampire declares, their minds are still their own. Suicidal or ridiculous directives don't sound any more sensible just because the person giving them is unusually fascinating. Still, inspired eloquence or significant wealth used in combination with this Discipline can enable the vampire to urge others along a desired course.
  
  The Brujah, Followers of Set, Toreador, and Ventrue Clans are all adept in this Discipline. The Ventrue are arguably the most skilled with its application, however, due to their ability to use Presence and Dominate in efficient combination.
- **●●●● Summon** (4); V20 195
  The player spends a blood point and rolls Charisma + Subterfuge. The base difficulty is 5; this increases to difficulty 7 if the subject was met only briefly. If the character used Presence successfully on the target in the past, this difficulty drops to 4, but if the attempt was unsuccessful, the difficulty rises to 8.
  
  The number of successes indicates the subject's speed and attitude in responding:
  
  **Successes**
  **Result**
  
  Botch
  Subject cannot be Summoned by that vampire for the rest of the story.
  
  Failure
  Subject cannot be Summoned by that vampire for the rest of the night.
  
  1 success
  Subject approaches slowly and hesitantly.
  
  2 successes
  Subject approaches reluctantly and is easily thwarted by obstacles.
  
  3 successes
  Subject approaches with reasonable speed.
  
  4 successes
  Subject comes with haste, overcoming any obstacles in his way.
  
  5 successes
  Subject rushes to the vampire, doing anything to get to her.
- **●●● Entrancement** (3); V20 195
  The player spends a blood point and rolls Appearance + Empathy (difficulty equal to the target's current Willpower points); the number of successes determines how long the subject is Entranced, as per the chart below. (Subjects can still spend Willpower to temporarily resist, like any other Presence power.) The Storyteller may wish to make the roll instead, since the character is never certain of the strength of her hold on the victim. The vampire may try to keep the subject under her thrall, but can do so only after the initial Entrancement wears off. Attempting this power while Entrancement is already in operation has no effect.
  
  **Successes**
  **Result**
  
  Botch
  Subject cannot be entranced for the rest of the story.
  
  Failure
  Subject cannot be entranced for the rest of the night.
  
  1 success
  One hour
  
  2 successes
  One day
  
  3 successes
  One week
  
  4 successes
  One month
  
  5 successes
  One year
- **●● Dread Gaze** (2); V20 194
  The player rolls Charisma + Intimidation (difficulty equal to the victim's Wits + Courage). Success indicates the victim is cowed, while failure means the target is startled but not terrified by the sight. Three or more successes means he runs away in abject fear; victims who have nowhere to run claw at the walls, hoping to dig a way out rather than face the vampire. Moreover, each success subtracts one from the target's action dice pools next turn.
  
  The character may attempt Dread Gaze once per turn against a single target, though she may also perform it as an extended action, adding her successes in order to subjugate the target completely. Once the target loses enough dice that he cannot perform any action, he's so shaken and terrified that he curls up on the ground and weeps. Failure during the extended action means the attempt falters. The character loses all her collected successes and can start over next turn, while the victim may act normally again.
  
  A botch at any time indicates the target is not at all impressed -- perhaps even finding the vampire's antics comical -- and remains immune to any further uses of Presence by the character for the rest of the story.
- **● Awe** (1); V20 193
  The player spends a blood point and rolls Charisma + Performance (difficulty 7). The number of successes rolled determines how many people are affected, as noted on the chart below. If there are more people present than the character can influence, Awe affects those with lower Willpower ratings first. The power stays in effect for the remainder of the scene or until the character chooses to drop it.
  
  **Successes**
  **Result**
  
  1 Successes
  One person
  
  2 Successes
  Two people
  
  3 Successes
  Six people
  
  4 Successes
  20 people
  
  5 Successes
  Everyone in the vampire's immediate vicinity (an entire auditorium, a mob)
  
  Those affected can use Willpower points to overcome the effect, but must continue spending Willpower every scene for as long as they remain in the same area as the vampire. As soon as an individual spends a number of Willpower points equal to the successes rolled, he shakes off the Awe completely and remains unaffected for the rest of the night.

## Traits

- **Legerdemain** (0)
- **Archery** (0)
- **Commerce** (0)
- **Ride** (0)
- **Hearth Wisdom** (0)
- **Seneschal** (0)
- **Theology** (0)

## Source

- Foundry world archive: aoe.zip
- Actor ID: QhK2h487eaC6Y2cJ
- Actor folder: NPC's / Elders

## Session notes

- [[Game 1]] - summons the coterie to [[Millie's Tavern]], announces [[Radomir Pavlovich]]'s destruction, and gives the group authority to investigate on behalf of the elders.

## Game 5-8 notes

- In [[Game 8]], [[Father Zemislav]] begins spreading the [[Alessandro Hussite Extremist Rumor]] to weaken Alessandro politically.
- Alessandro is discussed as a difficult but significant elder target in the [[Elder Targets Plot]].

text:
# Alessandro Hussite Extremist Rumor

## Overview

In [[Game 8]], [[Father Zemislav]] spreads a rumor that the failed burial miracle was a plot by Hussite extremists. He then begins connecting his sire [[Alessandro di Ravello]] to the extremist faction.

## Risk

Zemislav is willing to risk the Masquerade to compromise Alessandro, but the immediate roll suggests mortals do not realize Alessandro is a vampire.

## Related

- [[Elder Targets Plot]]

text:
# Ardan of Golden Lane

![Ardan of Golden Lane](/wiki/media/Ardan-of-Golden-Lane.png)

## Overview

Ardan of Golden Lane imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Chantry, Vampire). Vampire.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Vampire
- **Foundry folder:** NPC's / Chantry
- **Advantages:** Blood Pool 0/10

## Background

Born in the heart of Prague to a humble blacksmith, **Arden** was raised amidst the clang of hammers and the heat of the forge. His father, a skilled craftsman, had hoped Arden would follow in his footsteps. However, from a young age, Arden displayed a curiosity that went far beyond shaping metal. He became fascinated by alchemy--an art he believed could reveal the deeper secrets of life, the universe, and immortality. By the time he turned 17, his obsession with the pursuit of knowledge had drawn the attention of the **Order of Hermes**, a secret society dedicated to the mystical arts. Arden eagerly joined their ranks, leaving behind his life as a blacksmith's son.

As an apprentice within the **Hermetic Order of Tremere**, Arden's intellectual prowess quickly set him apart. He devoured the arcane texts, obsessed with uncovering the philosopher's stone and other alchemical wonders. During his time within the order, Arden began hearing rumors of a bold, experimental shift within the Tremere ranks. The mages, disillusioned with their waning powers and the rise of the vampiric Tzimisce, had discovered a way to convert themselves into vampires--undying alchemical creatures. While many mages recoiled from the idea, terrified of losing their magical avatars, Arden was enthralled. The promise of immortality, free from the constraints of paradox, was too enticing for his alchemical mind to resist.

When the opportunity arose to undergo the transformation, Arden eagerly volunteered, becoming one of the first to undergo the ritual. Unlike other apprentices, who grieved the loss of their connection to their avatars, Arden felt liberated. His new state as a vampire was, to him, the ultimate alchemical evolution--an immortal being unbound by the limitations of humanity. Arden's enthusiasm and unwavering support for the goals of Clan Tremere propelled him rapidly through the ranks. His cunning, combined with his fervor for advancing the clan's cause, soon caught the eye of his sire, **Celestyn**, who saw in Arden the potential for leadership.

In recognition of his talents and loyalty, Arden was eventually granted the prestigious title of **Regent**, tasked with overseeing the Tremere's influence in Prague. With his newfound power, Arden sought to merge his past and present, rebuilding his father's old blacksmith shop into a front for the public face of his chantry. The once-humble forge was transformed into a **shop of curiosities**, filled with arcane trinkets and mystical relics, attracting scholars, nobles, and mystics alike. To the outside world, the shop was operated by a supposed descendant of Arden's, but in reality, it served as a hub for Tremere influence in the heart of the city.

Behind the veil of this public shop, Arden operated a secret Tremere chantry, where he continued his pursuit of alchemical mastery. Here, he delved into blood magic and vampiric rituals, crafting a reputation as one of the most gifted Tremere alchemists in the region. The chantry under his command became a vital stronghold for the clan's efforts in Eastern Europe, providing a sanctuary for Tremere scholars and ensuring the clan's foothold in the region remained secure.

Though dedicated to his clan, Arden never forgot his personal ambitions. He maintained his obsession with discovering the true nature of immortality, seeking out rare ingredients and texts from across the continent, always on the hunt for the elusive philosopher's stone. His drive for knowledge and power made him both a valuable asset and a dangerous rival within the Tremere hierarchy. He often manipulated other kindred and mortals alike to further his experiments, earning a reputation as a schemer whose loyalty to the clan was matched only by his ambition.

Now, centuries into his unlife, **Arden of Golden Lane** stands as one of the most influential Tremere in Prague. His shop of curiosities continues to serve as a front for his experiments and a gathering place for those seeking arcane knowledge. Beneath its unassuming exterior, the Tremere chantry hums with activity as Arden and his fellow warlocks continue to push the boundaries of blood magic and alchemical transformation. Hidden in plain sight, Arden manipulates the city's power players and ensures that the Tremere's influence remains strong, even as he continues his personal quest for ultimate alchemical enlightenment.

## Roleplaying notes

Clan
[Tremere](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Tremere_(VTM))

Sire
[Celestyn](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Celestyn)

Nature
Judge

Demeanor
Director

Generation
7th

Embrace
1107 CE

Apparent Age
Late 20s

Physical
Strength 2, Dexterity 3, Stamina 3

Social
Charisma 4, Manipulation 3, Appearance 3

Mental
Perception 4, Intelligence 5, Wits 4

Talents
Alertness 3, Brawl 1, Dodge 3, Empathy 2, Intimidation 2, Intuition 2, Leadership 3, Subterfuge 5

Skills
Crafts 2, Etiquette 3, Herbalism 3, Melee 3, Performance 2, Ride 1, Security 3, Stealth 3

Knowledges
Academics 4, Enigmas 2, Investigation 3, Law 4, Linguistics 3 (German, Hebrew, Hungarian, Romanian), Medicine 2, Occult 4, Politics 4

Disciplines
[Auspex](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Auspex_(VTM)) 5, [Dominate](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Dominate_(VTM)) 3, [Necromancy](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Necromancy_(VTM)) 2, [Obfuscate](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Obfuscate_(VTM)) 3, [Presence](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Presence_(VTM)) 3, [Thaumaturgy](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Thaumaturgy_(VTM)) 6

Thaumaturgical Paths
[Alchemy](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Thaumaturgy_(VTM)#Alchemy) 5, [Path of Blood](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Thaumaturgy_(VTM)#Path_of_Blood) 5, [Focused Mind](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Thaumaturgy_(VTM)#Focused_Mind) 4, [Elemental Mastery](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Thaumaturgy_(VTM)#Elemental_Mastery) 3, [Hearth Path](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Thaumaturgy_(VTM)#Hearth_Path) 3, [Path of Conjuring](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Thaumaturgy_(VTM)#Path_of_Conjuring) 2, [Oneiromancy](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Thaumaturgy_(VTM)#Oneiromancy) 1

Necromantic Paths
[Sepulchre Path](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Necromancy_(VTM)#Sepulchre_Path) 2

Backgrounds
Clan Prestige ([Tremere](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Tremere_(VTM))) 3, Contacts 3, Resources 4, Retainers 2

Virtues
Conscience 3, Self-Control 5, Courage 4

Morality
[Humanity](https://whitewolf.fandom.com/wiki/Humanity_(VTM)) 6

Willpower
7

| Manipulation | 1 |

| Intelligence | 1 |
| Wits | 1 |

- **Archery** (0)
- **Commerce** (0)
- **Ride** (0)

title: Ari
summary: A wounded mortal servant of Prince Josef who escorts the coterie into the Jewish Quarter.
text:
# Ari

## Overview

Ari is a mortal servant of [[Prince Josef Zvi]]. He escorts the coterie to the Prince's private audience in [[Game 5]].

## Notes

- He is recovering from a leg injury caused by a crowd leaving the mock burial.
- His aura appears unusually clear and crisp.
- The coterie suspects some kind of possession or supernatural alteration.
- He uses a hand-wave compulsion to pass the Jewish Quarter gate.

title: Awakened Spirit Objects
summary: Spirit-bound items in Sabina's cottage.
text:
# Awakened Spirit Objects

## Confirmed facts

- At [[Sabina Premysl's Cottage]], the barrier between the physical and spirit worlds is extremely thin.
- Theodosia perceives nature spirits and is overwhelmed.
- The house contains many objects that were experimented on to awaken or capture spirits.
- Furniture and household objects respond subtly; chairs move into place as guests sit.
- Sabina keeps failures in the basement.

## Related concepts

- The transcript compares temporary awakened objects to talens and stronger multi-use objects to fetishes.
- These are spirit/nature phenomena, not wraiths or the dead.

title: Blood Bond and the Crown
summary: Jakub frames the blood bond as the chain created by the Crown or Crone in Noddist myth.
text:
# Blood Bond and the Crown

## Overview

At the [[Red Calf]], Jakub describes the blood bond as a chain associated with the Crown or Crone from Noddist myth. He says Cain broke the bond after a year and a day, and that younger vampires remain trapped in versions of those chains.

## Confirmed Table Lore

- Jakub presents the blood bond as a tool of vampiric hierarchy and enslavement.
- He connects liberation from the bond to recent upheavals among Lasombra, Tzimisce, and Assamite/Banu Haqim actors.
- This is delivered as Jakub's doctrine and should be treated as his framing, not neutral cosmic fact.

## Related

- [[Fireflower and Proto-Vauldere]]
- [[Jakub's Liberation Offer]]
- [[Lasombra Founder's Death]]

title: Boat That Sailed on Land
summary: Greg's elemental working that moves a boat across earth as if over water.
text:
# Boat That Sailed on Land

## Overview

In [[Game 13]], [[Greg the Tremere]] rips out a dying crusader's heart, lets it turn to dust over the boat, and causes the vessel to float over the ground as if the earth were water.

## Significance

- The working demonstrates Greg's extreme Tremere power.
- It allows the carriage and boat to continue travel after the ambush.
- It foreshadows the stranger forms of land magic encountered in [[Game 14]].

## Related

- [[Greg the Tremere]]
- [[Land-Spirit Crossing]]

title: Borislav Premysl
summary: Borislav Premysl imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Revenants, Mortal). Mortal.
text:
# Borislav Premysl

![Borislav Premysl](/wiki/media/Borislav-Premysl.jpg)

## Overview

Borislav Premysl imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Revenants, Mortal). Mortal.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Mortal
- **Foundry folder:** NPC's / Reventants
- **Advantages:** Blood Pool 0/10

## Appearance

Borislav is a burly, malformed man with disproportionately large limbs, a hunched posture, and thick, calloused skin. His facial features are distorted, with a permanently broken nose and an exaggerated mouth lined with sharp, malformed fangs. His eyes are an opaque, eerie white, and he exudes an aura of feral intensity.

## Background

Borislav's role within the Premysl family is that of an enforcer and tormentor. While Dragan is the family's honorable blade, Borislav takes on the work requiring ruthlessness and intimidation. He relishes his connection to the Tzimisce and sees his deformities as marks of honor. Borislav was responsible for carrying out much of the violence in the family's campaigns against Christians and outsiders, including the attacks on the Convent of the Knights of the Red Cross following Ahzra's death.

## Roleplaying notes

Borislav thrives on violence and believes the Premysl family should rule Prague's underworld with fear.  Borislav also despises those who sympathize with humans, believing that only the strong have the right to survive. He is, however, fiercely loyal to the family and particularly to Dragan, who he sees as a leader worthy of the Methuselah's service. Borislav's ultimate goal is to see Prague's Kindred recognize the Premysl family's power, bowing to their rule in the Methuselah's name.

- [[Game 2]] - named by Dragan/Sabina as part of the Premysl household.

## Chronicle 1 finale notes

- Confronts [[Istvan Vlaszy]] at [[Dragan Premysl's House]], accusing him of abandoning his sister and turning toward the enemies of his masters.

title: Bronze Fleshcrafted Vampire Statue
summary: Living vampire statue removed from Radomir's haven.
text:
# Bronze Fleshcrafted Vampire Statue

## Overview

A bronze-like statue shaped from a living vampire, found in a trunk or room at [[Radomir Pavlovich's Haven]]. The figure's arms and legs curve into tentacle-like forms, and its head is flattened into a platform. The transcript states it is still alive or in torpor-like condition, with micro-movements detectable.

## Important events

- Found in [[Game 1]].
- Father Zemislav covers it with a sheet and removes it from the haven.
- A demonic or abyssal manifestation confronts him while he is moving it.

## Open questions

- Who was this vampire before fleshcrafting?
- Why was it kept at Radomir's haven?
- What happens if it is restored or awakened?

title: Burned Salubri with the Third Eye
summary: A young vampire in chainmail with a third eye is found burned in public.
text:
# Burned Salubri with the Third Eye

## Overview

In [[Game 7]], the coterie finds a burned vampire in chainmail near a church. Examination reveals fangs and a third eye, pointing to a Salubri.

## Interpretation

- The vampire was likely young, since the body did not immediately ash away completely.
- [[Dragan Premysl]] later says one Salubri warrior was compelled into daylight during the attack on the Tremere Chantry.

## Related

- [[Salubri Chantry Raid]]

text:
# Butcher Shop of the Impossible River

## Overview

The butcher shop is the apparent source of [[Impossible River]]. [[Eliska von Vlasak]] buys meat there that later appears to be the heart of her dead husband.

## Investigation

- The river seems to flow from a chute or basement access.
- Something appears to have been pushed down the chute within the last week or so.
- Deeper investigation leads toward the [[Tremere Public Chantry]].

title: Cain's Northern Tomb
summary: The northern tomb or basalt throne sought by the Setites.
text:
# Cain's Northern Tomb

## Overview

The [[Norse Setite Anteaters]] tell the coterie of a northern tomb that legend identifies as Cain's own resting place, or at least a place of ancient vampiric power.

## Details

- The tomb is associated with mazes, contraptions, and places where sunlight never reaches.
- It may contain a basalt throne.
- Those who enter are said not to return.
- The Setites hope [[The Librarian]] or [[The Architect]] might help bypass the tomb's mysteries.

## Uncertain

- The transcript does not confirm whether this is truly Cain's tomb.

## Related

- [[Norse Setite Anteaters]]
- [[Chronicle 1 Epilogue]]

title: Cappadocian Cover-Up Offer
summary: A Cappadocian churchman offers rewards if the coterie hides Radomir's crimes, then threatens Istvan through Mira's ear.
text:
# Cappadocian Cover-Up Offer

## Overview

In Game 4, [[Garinol]], a Cappadocian churchman or abbot, tells the coterie that their mandate is to find Radomir's killer, not expose Radomir's crimes. He offers payment if they protect the court from scandal.

## Offered Rewards

- A vision-bearing [[Gehenna Tapestry]].
- A manse outside the domain useful as a safehouse or interrogation site.
- Prestation equivalent to major boons.

## Threat

When pushed, he produces an ear said to belong to [[Mira Vlaszy]]. Mira later says she is alive and that the ear had been handed over earlier as part of Radomir's Tremere arrangements.


## Appears In

- [[Game 4]]

title: Carrion Footprints at the River
summary: Footprints of a corpse-smelling watcher near the Salubri cave exit.
text:
# Carrion Footprints at the River

## Overview

After leaving the [[Hidden Salubri Cave]], the coterie finds tracks near the riverbank.

## Clue Details

- The tracks appear to belong to an adult male.
- The scent is carrion-like or corpse-like.
- The figure seems to have come from the water, followed or watched the group, and not gone as deep into the caves.
- The coterie suspects [[Garinol]], but the transcript does not confirm the identity.

## Related

- [[Garinol Intelligence]]
- [[Game 9]]

title: Castle Catacombs Audience
summary: The Librarian arranges a meeting with Prince Josef in the castle catacombs.
text:
# Castle Catacombs Audience

## Overview

After the coterie shares what they have learned, [[The Librarian]] says [[Prince Josef Zvi]] may listen if they can show that his agents are driving Prague toward war rather than protecting it.

## Conditions

- The coterie should bring evidence that agents or "dogs" of the Prince are escalating conflict.
- Crusader-linked forces outside Prague may be a useful lever.
- The meeting is to be arranged in the [[Castle Catacombs]] the following night.

## Payment

[[Katherine Karalova]] gives the Librarian the [[Book of Koldun Exorcery]] as payment for the boon or meeting.

## Appears In

- [[Game 4]]

A jail in the castle area. In Game 1/Game 2, the coterie learns that a suspect in the arson attempt against [[Radomir Pavlovich's Haven]] was captured and taken here.

## Open questions

- Whether the suspect actually started the fire is uncertain.
- The fire occurred after the coterie left the haven and is separate from Father Zemislav's statue incident.

title: Chronicle 1
summary: Reader-facing front page for the first Ashes of Ages chronicle.
text:
# Chronicle 1

Ashes of Ages Chronicle 1 follows a Prague coterie from a murder investigation in 1433 into the early fire of the Anarch Revolt, the birth of their pack, and their long flight north toward [[Stockholm]].

## Start Here

- [[Chronicle 1 Summary]] - readable prose overview of the whole chronicle.
- [[Timeline]] - chronological reference from Game 1 through the epilogue.
- [[Chronicle 1 End State]] - where everyone and everything stands when Chronicle 1 closes.
- [[Open Threads for Chronicle 2]] - unresolved hooks carried into Stockholm.
- [[Known Uncertainties]] - spelling, missing transcript, and in-character uncertainty notes.

## Main Cast

- [[Dalibor]]
- [[Eliska von Vlasak]]
- [[Father Zemislav]]
- [[Istvan Vlaszy]]
- [[Jardani Jovonovich]]
- [[Katherine Karálová]]
- [[Theodosia Byzantios]]

## Major Arcs

# Chronicle 1

Ashes of Ages Chronicle 1 follows a Prague coterie from a murder investigation in 1433 into the early fire of the Anarch Revolt, the birth of their pack, and their long flight north toward [[Stockholm]].

## Start Here
