playerText:
# Katherine Karálová

![Katherine Karálová](/wiki/media/Katherine-Karalova.jpeg)

## Overview

Katherine Karálová imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Player Characters, Vampire). Vampire | Nature: Defender | Demeanor: Pedagogue | Concept: Evergreen Scholar.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Vampire
- **Foundry folder:** Players
- **Foundry name note:** Laura
- **Original Foundry name:** Katherine Karálová (Laura)
- **Nature:** Defender
- **Demeanor:** Pedagogue
- **Concept:** Evergreen Scholar
- **Advantages:** Willpower 7; Blood Pool 15/20; Path of Community (Humanity) 6

## Appearance

Sir Pumpernickel, a mouse born in a crust of bread, has been ghouled as a messenger.

Owl retainer, to be named.

Rudolph was a contact, but I've ghouled him to keep him alive.

## Background

Klára Karálová was born in 1324 to a wealthy and influential merchant family in the Kingdom of Bohemia. Expected to marry well and uphold the family's reputation, she felt suffocated by the trappings of noble society. Her solace was found in the vast library of her family estate, where she could escape into books, feeding an insatiable curiosity for knowledge. When the University of Prague was founded in 1348, Klára saw a glimmer of hope--a place where learning was valued above all else. Defying the strict social norms of the time, she disguised herself as a man and enrolled in the university. Her deception was short-lived; she was discovered and expelled from formal study. However, her passion for learning had not gone unnoticed. The Headwoman offered her a humble position as a maid, allowing her access to the halls of academia by day, while granting her the freedom to read by night.

One evening, as she quietly emptied the ashes of a fireplace in one of the many shadowy corners of the university, a figure emerged from the dark--a mysterious librarian who had long watched her dedication in silence. This librarian was no ordinary scholar but a Nosferatu vampire, a keeper of ancient knowledge hidden away from mortal eyes. Impressed by Klára's relentless hunger for wisdom, he Embraced her, offering her eternity in exchange for her loyalty and devotion to learning. From that moment on, Klára became "The Girl Who Would Learn," apprenticed to the ancient Librarian. Deep within his underground warren, she immersed herself in forgotten tomes and haunted the lectures and libraries of Prague, unseen and unknown. As the decades passed, her knowledge grew, but her thirst for more remains as endless as the night.

## Gear

[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1bvrBFfSyI8eyph26821NXEGVG0HZR_wEWdkdv2bdS5Q/edit?gid=0#gid=0](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1bvrBFfSyI8eyph26821NXEGVG0HZR_wEWdkdv2bdS5Q/edit?gid=0#gid=0)

## Attributes

| Trait | Rating |
|---|---:|
| Strength | 2 |
| Dexterity | 2 |
| Stamina | 2 |
| Charisma | 3 |
| Manipulation (Conjuror of Facts) | 4 |
| Perception | 3 |
| Intelligence (Book Knowledge) | 4 |
| Wits | 3 |

## Abilities

**Talents:** Alertness 2, Awareness 2, Empathy 1, Intimidation 1, Subterfuge 3

**Skills:** Animal Ken 2, Performance (Acting) 3, Stealth 2

**Knowledges:** Academics (Philosophy) 4, Enigmas 3, Investigation 3, Occult 2

## Experiences

- **Gained experience**
  Game 1 - 11/05/2024
- **Gained experience**
  Game 2 - 11/18/2024
- **Gained experience**
  Game 14 - 04/21/2025

## Features, backgrounds, merits, and flaws

- **Death by Stake** (5)
  Like the vampires of legend, a wooden stake through your heart can destroy you immediately. This Flaw is worth two points if the stake must be cut from a specific type of wood, such as ash or rowan, to inflict Final Death. If any wooden stake can kill you, this Flaw is worth five points. Vampires with this Flaw do not know their vulnerability.
- **Rat in a Cage** (2)
  Anytime you are penned in or physically restrained (such as by a cage or with handcuffs), you suffer acute anxiety. The difficulties of any rolls made under such circumstances are increased by two.
- **Phobia: Wolves** (2)
  You feel an irrational fear of something. Spiders, Snakes, Crowds, and heights are examples of common phobias. You should do a Courage roll whenever you encounter the object of your phobia. The difficulty of the test is determined by the Storyteller and if you fail, you must move away from that object.
- **Anosmia** (2)
  Your life in the sewers has removed your sense of smell and taste. This means you are unperturbed by even the worst stench or most disgusting flavor. However, it also means you cannot ever succeed at any Perception rolls that rely on taste or smell. It also does not make you immune in any way to gas attacks or poisons; you just won't be able to tell they are there.
- **Darksight** (2)
  Your character sees in the darkness the way others see in light. Even in the complete absence of light, she sees perfectly. She suffers no penalties for darkness. Additionally, she catches glimpses of the otherworldly. She sees ghosts as if they were material, but only while in the dark. More than a candle's light negates this ability.
- **Lingua Franca** (1)
  You are able to speak a mixed language made of common tongues that most people you encounter will understand. You may not be able to hold philosophical discussions, but you can navigate your way through conversations in most places and will be able to communicate sufficiently in dire situations.
- **Literacy** (1)
  You are a learned scholar, able to both read and write. The Merit assumes that you can read and write with fluency in your native tongue, as well as Latin and any languages you may have gained through the Multi-Lingual Merit. In some chronicles, literacy can be assumed. In these cases, Illiteracy becomes a 1-point Flaw.
- **Multi-Lingua** (2)
  Through patience, study, and/or immersion, you are able to speak another language or languages with native fluency. This represents a full command over the language. For each dot of Intelligence your character possesses, you may know one other language. You may also use this Merit to reflect secret codes, cants, jargon, or sign language as well. Additional languages increase the cost of this Merit by one point per language.
- **Contacts** (2)
- **Herd** (1)
  You have built a group of mortals from whom you can feed without fear. In addition to providing nourishment, your Herd may be useful for minor tasks. However, unlike Allies or Retainers, they are typically not very controllable or skilled. Your Herd rating adds dice to your rolls for hunting. Players may purchase pooled Herd with Background points.
  
  - Three vessels
  
  - Seven vessels
  
  - 15 vessels
  
  - 30 vessels
  
  - 60 vessels
- **Resources** (2)
- **Generation (7th Gen)** (5)
- **Sebík Pražský** (3)

## Melee Weapons

- **Stake**; V20 280
  May paralyze a vampire if driven through the heart. The attacker must target the heart (difficulty 9) and score three damage successes.
- **Knife**; V20 280
- **Bite**; V20 276
  This maneuver is available only to vampires (or other supernatural creatures with sharp teeth, such as werewolves). A bite maneuver is a “combat” bite, intended to cause damage rather than drain blood. Bite damage is aggravated. To use a bite attack, the vampire must first perform a successful clinch, hold, or tackle maneuver (see below). On the turn following the successful attack, the player may declare the bite attempt and make a roll using the modifiers below.
  
  Alternatively, a player can declare her vampire’s bite to be a “Kiss” attack. A Kiss is resolved in the same way as a normal bite, but inflicts no health levels of damage. Upon connecting with a Kiss, the vampire may begin to drain the victim’s blood at the normal rate, and the victim is typically helpless to resist (see p. 269 for specifics). Following the Kiss, a vampire may, if she chooses, lick the puncture wound of the Kiss closed, thereby removing any evidence that she has fed.
- **Bite (Kiss)**; V20 276
  This maneuver is available only to vampires (or other supernatural creatures with sharp teeth, such as werewolves). A bite maneuver is a “combat” bite, intended to cause damage rather than drain blood. Bite damage is aggravated. To use a bite attack, the vampire must first perform a successful clinch, hold, or tackle maneuver (see below). On the turn following the successful attack, the player may declare the bite attempt and make a roll using the modifiers below.
  
  Alternatively, a player can declare her vampire’s bite to be a “Kiss” attack. A Kiss is resolved in the same way as a normal bite, but inflicts no health levels of damage. Upon connecting with a Kiss, the vampire may begin to drain the victim’s blood at the normal rate, and the victim is typically helpless to resist (see p. 269 for specifics). Following the Kiss, a vampire may, if she chooses, lick the puncture wound of the Kiss closed, thereby removing any evidence that she has fed.
- **Hold**; V20 276
  Hold: This attack inflicts no damage, as the intent is to immobilize rather than injure the subject. On a successful roll, the attacker holds the target until the subject’s next action. At that time, both combatants roll resisted Strength + Brawl actions; the subject remains immobilized (able to take no other action) until she rolls more successes than the attacker does.
  
  Traits: Strength + Brawl
  Difficulty: Normal
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: None
- **Kick**; V20 276
  Kicks range from simple front kicks to aerial spins. The base attack is at +1 difficulty and inflicts the attacker’s Strength +1 in damage. These ratings may be modified further at the Storyteller’s discretion, increasing in damage and/or difficulty as the maneuver increases in complexity.
  
  Ability: Dexterity + Brawl
  Difficulty: +1
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: Strength +1
- **Strike**; V20 276
  The attacker lashes out with a fist. The base attack is a standard action and inflicts the character’s Strength in damage. The Storyteller may adjust the difficulty and/or damage depending on the type of punch: hook, jab, haymaker, karate strike.
  
  Traits: Dexterity + Brawl
  Difficulty: Normal
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: Strength
- **Clinch**; V20 276
  Clinch: On a successful attack roll, the attacker goes into a clinch with the target. In the first turn, the attacker may roll Strength damage. In each subsequent turn, combatants act on their orders in the initiative. A combatant can inflict Strength damage automatically or attempt to escape the clinch. No other actions are allowed until one combatant breaks free. To escape a clinch, make a resisted Strength + Brawl roll against the opponent. If the escaping character has more successes, she breaks free; if not, the characters continue to grapple in the next turn.
  
  Traits: Strength + Brawl
  Difficulty: Normal
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: Strength

## Disciplines and powers

- **Obfuscate** (0); VDA 224
  To many common men and women living in the 13th century, the Devil lurks in every shadow and hides behind every corner. Evil is ever-present and a threat that can rarely be seen with your eyes.
  In the case of those Cainites who have mastered Obfuscate, this paranoia is justified. While evil may be debatable, Obfuscate makes it so that a gifted vampire could be lurking in the darkness ready to strike or flee at any time. The monster must be close at hand to accomplish this, no more than five meters per dots of Wits + Stealth from their would-be victims.
  Most mortals, even those trained in Awareness, cannot pierce the monster's deception. Some unique individuals may be able to see straight through these lies; children, horses, dogs, some cats, and very few religious visionaries are unaffected by Obfuscate, but this is all at Storyteller discretion.
  Cainite with the Discipline of Auspex may be able to pierce a Cainite's Obfuscate. For more, see Auspex on p. 195.
  Most Obfuscate powers last a scene once activated,unless willed away by the Cainite. They take no particular concentration to uphold once raised.
- **● The Silence of Death** (1); VDA 225
  There is no roll. The power creates a zone of absolute silence around the character, with a maximum radius of 7 meters. No sound occurs inside this zone, though sounds originating outside the area of effect may be heard by anyone in it. Additionally, if the player spends two blood points and the character spends five turns in concentration in a room or enclosure, the silence remains in it after the character leaves the room and lasts until she wills it to end. A character can't create more than one zone of silence, so if she wishes to use Silence of Death on herself (or curse another room), the zone in the first room fades automatically.
- **●● Unseen Presence** (2); VDA 225
  There is no roll necessary to conjure an Unseen Presence. Should a Cainite draw attention to herself by sneak attack, blunder, or speech, accidental attention will call for a Wits + Stealth roll, difficulty from 5 to 9, based on the amount of noise or calamity the Cainite has caused. Willful attempts to violate the secrecy of Unseen Presence while still maintaining it may call for a high difficulty and multiple successes. For example, whispering to an enemy without giving away her location would require three or so successes at a difficulty of 7.
  Even when seen or unseen, a secondary use of this power allows a Cainite to conceal small items already on her person, or with a successful Manipulation + Subterfuge from 5 to 9, based on the amount of observation she can grasp and conceal an easily moved item.
- **●●● Mask of a Thousand Faces** (3); VDA 225
  To create this guise, the player rolls Manipulation + Performance at a difficulty 7. To mimic the face of another, the vampire must have a good look at them.
  The Storyteller might raise the difficulty on the roll if the vampire has only briefly seen the person they wish to mimic. How successful the disguise is depends on the chart at the top of the page.
  Should a vampire wish to create a face much more appealing than her own, she must spend a point of blood for every dot of difference from her Appearance to that
  of the disguise.
  While fooling acquaintances may require only a look at the target and clever crafting of the mask, fooling someone intimately connected to the target is another matter. That requires actual familiarity with the target, and perhaps other rolls to keep the illusion going.
  
  **Successes**
  **Result**
  
  1 Success
  The vampire retains the same height and build, with a few slight alterations to his basic features. Nosferatu can appear as normal, albeit ugly, mortals.
  
  2 Successes
  He looks unlike himself; people don't easily recognize him or agree about his appearance.
  
  3 Successes
  He looks the way he wants to appear.
  
  4 Successes
  Complete transformation, including gestures, mannerisms, appearance, and voice.
  
  5 Successes
  Profound alteration (appear as the opposite sex, a vastly different age, or an extreme change of size).
- **Animalism** (0); V20 128
  The Beast resides within all creatures, from scuttling cockroaches to scabrous rats up through untamed wolves and even powerful Kindred elders. Animalism allows the vampire to amplify his intensely primordial nature. He can not only communicate with animals, but can also force his will upon them, directing such beasts to do as he commands. As the vampire grows in power, he can even control the Beast within mortals and other supernaturals.
  
  Beasts grow distinctly agitated in the presence of a vampire who lacks this Discipline or the Skill of Animal Ken, often to the point of attacking or running from the vampire. In contrast, vampires possessing Animalism exude a dominant vibe to lower creatures, which attracts them.
  
  Animalism is commonly found with vampires of the Gangrel and Nosferatu Clans. Manipulation and Charisma are important for the use of Animalism powers; the stronger the vampire's personality, the more influence he has over animals.
- **● Whispers to the Wild** (1); VDA 191
  No roll is necessary to talk with an animal. However, the vampire must make eye contact to initiate  Whispers to the Wild. Once she's established the power, she does not need to maintain eye contact for the current scene. Commanding an animal requires a Manipulation + Animal Ken roll. The difficulty depends on the creature; more complex creatures, particularly predators, are more prone to the vampire's whims. Taking the animal's shape with Protean or Animalism or speaking to the animal using familiar sounds makes such commands easier. Highly dangerous commands are much more difficult. Use the following chart to determine the difficulty.
  
  Predator Mammal (Wolves, cates, Bats)
  5
  
  Other mammals and preadatory birds (rats, owls)
  6
  
  Other birds and reptiles (doves, snakes)
  7
  
  No Current eye contact
  +1
  
  Using Animal Sounds
  -1
  
  Currently Taking the animal's shape
  -2
  
  Dangerous Command
  +1
  
  Deadly Command
  +2
  
  While animals may do dangerous or even potentially deadly tasks for the Cainite, they will not commit directly suicidal tasks, nor will they violate their base biological natures. Cowardly animals tasked with guard duty will not engage and combat an invader, for example. They might run to their master and report the threat, however.
  Successes determine the animal's dedication to the cause. A single success will ensure the animal's compliance for the current scene. Three successes will guarantee a regular task for a week or more. Five successes will command indefinite loyalty.
- **●●●● Vanish from the Mind's Eye** (4); V20 186
  The player rolls Charisma + Stealth; the difficulty equals the target's Wits + Alertness (use the highest total in the group if the character disappears in front of a crowd). With three or fewer successes, the character fades but does not vanish, becoming an indistinct, ghostlike figure. With more than three, he disappears completely. If the player scores more successes than an observer's Willpower rating, that person forgets that the vampire was there in the first place.
  
  Tracking the character accurately while he appears ghostlike requires a Perception + Alertness roll (difficulty 8). A successful roll means the individual can interact normally with the vampire (although the Kindred looks like a profoundly disturbing ghostly shape). A failed roll results in a +2 difficulty modifier (maximum 10) when attempting to act upon, or interact with, the vampire. The Storyteller may call for new observation checks if the vampire moves to an environment in which he's difficult to see (heads into shadows, crosses behind an obstacle, proceeds through a crowd). When fully invisible, the vampire is handled as described under Unseen Presence, above.
  
  A person subject to the vanishing makes a Wits + Courage roll (mortals at difficulty 9, vampires at difficulty 5). A successful roll means the individual reacts immediately (although after the vampire performs his action for that turn); failure means the person stands uncomprehending for two turns while her mind tries to make sense of what she just experienced.
- **● Zillah's Litany** (1); V20 226
  If the subject has any blood bonds or Vinculi to other vampires, this power reveals them to the caster. Although the caster may not know the vampires in question, this power does reveal the names and gives rough psychic impressions of the individuals in question.
- **The Path of the Father's Vengeance** (0); V20 226
  This path, based loosely on a powerful thaumaturge's interpretations of the_ Book of Nod_, devotes itself to delivering justice to the race of Cainites. Each power supposedly has some precedent in the parables of the ancient book, and focuses on teaching the lessons of Caine via the power of blood magic. Use of this path is hotly debated in the Sabbat, as some consider it tantamount to claiming to hold Caine's right over all vampires oneself. Camarilla vampires don't have the same knowledge of the_ Book of Nod_ that the Sabbat do, but the path is not entirely unheard of in Tremere chantries.
  
  The power of this path comes not only from the magic of blood, but also incantation of verses from the _Book of Nod_. For any of these powers to take effect, the caster must speak the actual condemnation. For example, to invoke the third-level power, the caster must state plainly to his target that she may eat only ashes. The subject must generally be able to hear the caster for these powers to take effect, though writing them and showing them to the subject will do.
  
  These powers apply to vampires only. They do not affect mortals, ghouls, or any other supernatural creatures.

title: Katherine's Hidden Trunk
summary: The waterproof trunk of books Katherine throws into the river after Orlok's death.
text:
# Katherine's Hidden Trunk

## Overview

After the [[Orlok Assassination]], [[Katherine Karálová]] returns to [[The Librarian's Library]], silences the room, packs important books into a waterproof trunk, and throws it into the river.

## Contents

The transcript does not list the exact titles. The trunk is described as containing important books or knowledge Katherine does not want to leave openly in the library.

## Later Discussion

In [[Game 13]], Katherine considers returning to recover the trunk before fleeing Prague. The group decides the box is likely stable enough to leave for later, especially because Rudolph or the maker seems to have built it well.

## Related

- [[The Librarian]]
- [[Stake the Librarian Offer]]
- [[Game 10]]
- [[Game 13]]

text:
# Known Uncertainties

## Confirmed Corrections

- Micah and Vykos are the same person. Use [[Micah Vykos]].
- Garinol is the cleaned final name, even where raw transcript text mishears it.
- Jakub of Thessaloniki is the corrected name; raw transcript text may say Jacob.
- Sabina's Tremere Embrace happened off screen.

## Source Gaps

- No raw Game 12 transcript was provided. [[Game 12 Missing Transcript Summary]] is based only on the opening recap of [[Game 13]].

## Human Review Still Useful

- [[Colleen Norgobane]] spelling may need confirmation from player notes.
- [[Greg the Tremere]] gives only "Greg" in character. Any stronger identification should remain marked as out-of-character unless later source material confirms it.
- Some raw transcripts preserve speech-to-text errors; cleaned reference pages should be preferred for names and summaries.

text:
# Labyrinth Chasm

## Overview

In the deep cave near the hidden Salubri, [[Theodosia Byzantios]] and [[Father Zemislav]] investigate a chasm with no meaningful shroud between the living world and the Labyrinth.

## Confirmed Effects

- Theodosia glimpses something like the upper crust of Hell or Oblivion.
- The experience damages her Path and leaves her eyes black-on-black.
- Zemislav serves as a dark guide through the experience.

## Interpretation

The transcript treats the chasm as genuinely supernatural and dangerous. It does not fully explain why it is there or whether it is tied to Salubri defenses, Tzimisce land-magic, or older underworld wounds.

## Related

- [[Hidden Salubri Cave]]
- [[Theodosia's Tzimisce Infection]]
- [[Game 9]]

title: Land-Spirit Crossing
summary: The moment the pack crosses into land that welcomes Istvan instead of enslaving him.
text:
# Land-Spirit Crossing

## Overview

In [[Game 14]], while portaging the Setite boat, [[Istvan Vlaszy]] hears a voice welcome him. It resembles the pressure of a Tzimisce Methuselah only at first; the transcript clarifies it is something older and more natural.

## Details

- The soil feels different, fine, or silty.
- The weight on Istvan lifts rather than clamps down.
- The phenomenon is not undead.
- The Tzimisce seem to have copied, exploited, or enslaved this type of land-spirit bond elsewhere.

## Related

- [[Lithuanian Hearth Pact]]
- [[Devara]]
- [[Shagra's Bloodline]]

title: Land Spirits and Tzimisce Dominion
summary: Hub for land-spirits, Tzimisce control, Shagra, and the Lithuanian hearth pact.
text:
# Land Spirits And Tzimisce Dominion

## Overview

Chronicle 1 repeatedly ties vampiric power to land: Tzimisce soil, revenant service, Shagra's influence, and the spiritual geography beneath Prague. [[Game 14]] reframes that pattern by showing [[Devara]] and the [[Lithuanian Hearth Pact]], where vampires marry the land rather than enslaving it.

## Key Pages

- [[Shagra's Bloodline]]
- [[Land-Spirit Crossing]]
- [[Lithuanian Hearth Pact]]
- [[Devara]]
- [[Hanged Men Blood Hospitality]]
- [[Spirit Communion Ritual]]
- [[Book of Koldun Exorcery]]
- [[Theodosia's Tzimisce Infection]]

## Working Distinction

- Tzimisce dominion: land and spirits forced into service.
- Hearth pact: land treated as spouse, partner, and host.
- Shagra: an ancient force that may exploit or corrupt the land-bound channel.

title: Lasombra Founder's Death
summary: Recent rumors and table clarification connect Lasombra upheaval to the destruction of the Lasombra founder.
text:
# Lasombra Founder's Death

## Overview

Game 3 and Game 4 discuss a recent event among the Lasombra: a group involving Lasombra rebels, Assamite/Banu Haqim aid, and Tzimisce ritual knowledge broke elder bonds and killed a foundational Lasombra figure.

## Confirmed From Transcript

- Father Zemislav connects this to a vision involving day, night, and a black sun.
- Jacob says the Keepers have been liberated, though they still keep slaves of their own.
- Out-of-character clarification identifies this as the death of the Lasombra Antediluvian/founder and links it to Gratiano and allied assassins.

## Caution

Some of this page is table clarification rather than clean in-character testimony.

## Related

- [[Fireflower and Proto-Vauldere]]
- [[Jakub of Thessaloniki (Zakariya ibn Masud al-Muntazir)]]

title: Late Market
summary: Market that remains active after dark.
text:
# Late Market

## Overview

A late market marked on the Prague map as one of the places still plausibly open at night. The coterie uses it as a meeting point after Game 1.

## Important events

- Contacts are consulted here in [[Game 1]].
- The coterie regroups here in [[Game 2]].
- Characters buy tools, hunt, and exchange information before following the Istvan lead.

title: Lithuanian Hearth Pact
summary: The coven tradition that marries vampires to land instead of enslaving it.
text:
# Lithuanian Hearth Pact

## Overview

The Lithuanian Hearth Pact is the working name for [[Devara]]'s coven and its relationship with local land-spirits.

## Beliefs And Practices

- The land owns itself.
- Devara and her coven marry or unite with the land rather than ruling it as prince or king.
- Their protections are mutual bonds with spirits, not domination.
- They consider Tzimisce treatment of land-spirits to be enslavement.
- They say every land has a spirit unless that spirit has been destroyed, which is rare.

## Locations

- A grove with a central tree and multiple hanged men.
- Nearby lands in Lithuania; Devara mentions other covens a day's journey east and four days northwest.

## Related

- [[Devara]]
- [[Land-Spirit Crossing]]
- [[Hanged Men Blood Hospitality]]

title: Malina
summary: A notorious local werewolf and Jardani's lover.
text:
# Malina

## Overview

Malina is a werewolf recognized by description as a notorious figure of the region. She ambushes [[Jardani Jovonovich]] in Prague, transforms into war form, but does not try to kill him.

## Relationships

- [[Jardani Jovonovich]] - lover.

## Notes

- Her presence inside Prague is considered alarming because werewolves normally avoid cities.
- Jardani initially tries to conceal her identity from the group.

## Chronicle 1 finale notes

- Steals or hides [[Istvan Vlaszy]]'s shoes and leaves [[Malina's Rune Sword]] in place of his weapon.

title: Malina's Rune Sword
summary: The hostile rune-carved sword Malina leaves for Istvan.
text:
# Malina's Rune Sword

## Overview

After [[Malina]] steals [[Istvan Vlaszy]]'s shoes, Istvan wakes to find his sword replaced by a near-identical rune-carved weapon.

## Details

- The runes are in the language of Malina's people.
- The sword tries to move on its own when partially drawn.
- It appears hostile to vampires and to the coterie.
- During the Orlok attack it slides toward Istvan's allies, apparently misunderstanding who holds or threatens whom.

## Found By / Connected To

- [[Istvan Vlaszy]]
- [[Malina]]
- [[Orlok Assassination]]

## Uncertain

- The sword's exact maker, spirit, and rules are not fully identified in the transcript.

title: Malina's Test
summary: Malina ambushes Jardani in Prague in war form but does not try to kill him.
text:
# Malina's Test

## Overview

At the [[Night Market]] in [[Game 7]], a woman lures [[Jardani Jovonovich]] aside, attacks, and transforms into a war-form werewolf. She does not bite, claw, or try to kill him, instead seeming to test boundaries.

## Confirmed Later

- The werewolf is [[Malina]], Jardani's lover.
- Istvan identifies the description as a notorious werewolf of the region.
- Her presence in Prague is considered deeply dangerous and unnatural for a werewolf.

title: Marzanna
summary: Slavic winter/death figure mentioned in Radomir's books.
text:
# Marzanna

## Confirmed facts

- A book in [[Radomir Pavlovich's Haven]] describes a person claiming to be the channel of Marzanna, named as a Slavic god of winter and death.
- The channel claimed that burning them would only make them stronger and that cold winds would kill crops.
- Characters with survival knowledge know crops in the area have become harder to grow for the last seven to ten years.

## Unconfirmed

- The transcript does not prove that Marzanna is responsible for crop failures. It presents the connection as an ominous clue.

text:
# Micah Vykos

## Overview

Micah Vykos is a metamorphosis-minded Tzimisce Anarch leader present in the [[Fireflower Anarch Camp]]. Micah and Vykos are the same person.

## Appears In

- [[Game 11]]
- [[Game 12 Missing Transcript Summary]]
- [[Game 13]] recap

## Confirmed Details

- Participates in discussions about [[Istvan Vlaszy]]'s Embrace.
- Speaks about the "Hydra" of elder power and the difficulty of killing it before enough heads are removed.
- Says the Prince knows the Shagra creature and that the creature believes it enslaved him, but may be wrong.
- Says the Tremere have built a weapon to strike an ancient's psyche and that the weapon may need to be forced into use.

## Related

- [[Tremere Weapon Against Shagra]]
- [[Tremere Plot Against Shagra]]
- [[Night of Chaos]]
- [[Anarchs]]

title: Mira Vlaszy
summary: Mira Vlaszy imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Revenants, Mortal). Mortal.
text:
# Mira Vlaszy

![Mira Vlaszy](/wiki/media/Mira-Vlaszy.jpg)

## Overview

Mira Vlaszy imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Revenants, Mortal). Mortal.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Mortal
- **Foundry folder:** NPC's / Reventants
- **Advantages:** Blood Pool 0/10

## Appearance

Mira is slender and poised, with sharp, intelligent eyes and a quiet, observant demeanor. Her face bears the marks of her Slavic lineage, with fair skin and striking, cold blue eyes. Mira is rarely seen in armor, preferring the unassuming attire of a courtier to better blend into the background. Her presence is subtle yet magnetic, often drawing attention with minimal effort--a testament to her refined use of the Presence discipline.

## Background

Mira was trained not only in war but in the intricate web of Tzimisce court intrigue, serving as a covert messenger, spy, and diplomat. Her sense of duty is as unbreakable as any Vlaszy knight's, but her talents lie in the shadows rather than the battlefield. Despite her lack of physical prowess, Mira's quiet resilience and devotion earned her a place of trust, especially among the more secretive Tzimisce lords.

Mira developed her Presence and Animalism abilities for subtle manipulation and reconnaissance. She is particularly skilled at using small animals, such as crows and mice, as her eyes and ears in distant places. Her calm, unshakable loyalty to her master is unquestionable, but her interactions with others are often marked by a degree of skepticism. She is aware that few share her unwavering commitment to honor.

## Roleplaying notes

Mira seeks to uphold her family's legacy by any means necessary, valuing duty over personal ambition. She is deeply loyal to the Tzimisce and despises betrayal or disloyalty. Though devoted, she harbors private doubts about the wisdom of serving only one master, given the fracturing alliances within Tzimisce society. In the back of her mind, Mira contemplates the possibility of securing an independent foothold for her family, preserving their name should the Tzimisce elders falter.

## Attributes

| Trait | Rating |
|---|---:|
| Strength | 1 |
| Dexterity | 1 |
| Stamina | 1 |
| Charisma | 1 |
| Manipulation | 1 |
| Appearance | 1 |
| Perception | 1 |
| Intelligence | 1 |
| Wits | 1 |

## Traits

- **Legerdemain** (0)
- **Archery** (0)
- **Commerce** (0)
- **Ride** (0)
- **Hearth Wisdom** (0)
- **Seneschal** (0)
- **Theology** (0)

## Source

- Foundry world archive: aoe.zip
- Actor ID: gFg7AtfqV0Uik6X1
- Actor folder: NPC's / Reventants

## Game 3-4 notes

- In [[Game 4]], Mira is found alive after a Cappadocian negotiator uses an ear said to be hers as leverage.
- She says the ear had been given earlier as part of Radomir's Tremere arrangements.
- She confirms delivering bodies to the [[Golden Lane Shop]] and explains parts of Radomir's communication network.

Related: [[Mira Vlaszy's Testimony]]

title: Mira Vlaszy's Ear
summary: An ear used as leverage against Istvan, later revealed to have been surrendered earlier.
text:
# Mira Vlaszy's Ear

## Overview

The Cappadocian negotiator produces an ear he says belongs to [[Mira Vlaszy]] in order to threaten [[Istvan Vlaszy]]. Mira is later found alive with both ears.

## Explanation

Mira says the ear was handed over earlier as part of [[Radomir Pavlovich]]'s Tremere arrangements, likely to pass blood wards or prove access.

## Related

- [[Cappadocian Cover-Up Offer]]
- [[Mira Vlaszy's Testimony]]

title: Mira Vlaszy's Testimony
summary: Mira explains her role in Radomir's Tremere dealings and why Sabina may still matter.
text:
# Mira Vlaszy's Testimony

## Overview

[[Mira Vlaszy]] tells the coterie that she acted as one of [[Radomir Pavlovich]]'s inside-Prague messengers. She delivered bodies to the [[Golden Lane Shop]] and confirms that her ear had previously been given under Radomir's arrangements, not freshly cut by the Cappadocian negotiator.

## Key Points

- Mira worked Radomir's court communications inside Prague.
- [[Istvan Vlaszy]] handled outside-city communications.
- Mira believes the Prince may not have known the source of Radomir's Tremere knowledge.
- She believes [[Sabina Premysl]] may have been taken because the knowledge she carried was valuable.
- She links Radomir's work to efforts around [[Prince Joseph and Antediluvian Influence]].

## Appears In

- [[Game 4]]

title: Necromancer Countermeasures
summary: Library research records ways mortal necromancers have threatened vampiric necromancers.
text:
# Necromancer Countermeasures

## Overview

At [[The Librarian's Library]], [[Istvan Vlaszy]] researches necromancers. The notes include an account of Adolfo Giovanni killing his Cappadocian sire by filling zombies with hard liquor and igniting trails, as well as references to mortal necromancers who can paralyze vampiric necromancers during casting.

## Usefulness

This is practical research rather than a resolved plot point. It suggests that mortal necromancers may be dangerous to Cainite necromancers and are often hunted in Christian lands.

## Appears In

- [[Game 4]]

title: Night Market
summary: Recurring Prague meeting place for the coterie and several supernatural encounters.
text:
# Night Market

## Overview

The Night Market is a recurring gathering point for the coterie.

## Game 5-8 Events

- The coterie gathers there before the Prince's escort arrives.
- [[Theodosia Byzantios]] comes there after her Methuselah crisis.
- [[Malina]] ambushes [[Jardani Jovonovich]] near the market.
- A burned Salubri body is found nearby.

title: Night of Chaos
summary: The elder-killing crisis in Prague after Orlok's assassination.
text:
# Night of Chaos

## Overview

The Night of Chaos is the period after the coterie's attacks and Anarch plots leave multiple elders dead and Prague's Kindred power structure unstable.

## Confirmed Effects

- [[Adrastos von Orlok]] is destroyed.
- Several key elders are reported dead.
- Lasombra leadership is in chaos.
- Cappadocians are reportedly attacked by their own childer.
- Toreador rivals maneuver for power after Orlok's fall.
- The Anarchs prepare the [[Gunpowder Diversion at Elder Elysium]].

## Related

- [[Orlok Assassination]]
- [[Fireflower Anarch Camp]]
- [[Game 11]]
- [[Game 13]]

title: Niktuku Attack in Estonia
summary: The epilogue attack that drives the pack out of Estonia.
text:
# Niktuku Attack in Estonia

## Overview

During the Chronicle 1 epilogue, the pack spends time in Estonia before a Niktuku attack strikes the vampire warrens near sunrise.

## Details

- [[Katherine Karálová]] saves herself and helps the pack escape by collapsing a section of the warren between them and the Niktuku.
- [[Istvan Vlaszy]] and [[Father Zemislav]] help break out into a sunlit area.
- The pack escapes by caravan during the day while burning under the sun's pressure.

## Consequences

- The attack ends the Estonia period and sends the pack toward St. Petersburg.

## Related

- [[Chronicle 1 Epilogue]]

title: Norse Setite Anteaters
summary: Heretical Setites who fled north where there were no snakes and adopted northern sorceries.
text:
# Norse Setite Anteaters

## Overview

The Norse Setite Anteaters are a northern heretical Setite band encountered in the [[Fireflower Anarch Camp]]. Their ancestors fled Egypt and Europe into lands cold enough that no snakes lived there.

## Details

- Their name is given as "the anteaters."
- They are Setites, but their magic is intertwined with northern spirits, sleeping dragons, and cold lands.
- [[Svigar Derkinson]] says they were sent south by an enslaving leader to trade northern artifacts.
- After receiving the Fireflower, they intend to return north and overthrow their own masters.
- They offer the coterie passage on the [[Setite Ship North]].

## Members

- [[Svigar Derkinson]]
- [[Ursa]]
- [[Colleen Norgobane]]
- [[Thor Skydre]]

## Related

- [[Cain's Northern Tomb]]
- [[Game 13]]

title: Northern Road and Stockholm
summary: Hub for the escape from Prague, northern allies, epilogue route, and Chronicle 2 setup.
text:
# Northern Road And Stockholm

## Overview

After the [[Night of Chaos]], the pack leaves Prague with northern Setites, meets Lithuanian hearth witches, survives a long epilogue, and reaches [[Stockholm]].

## Route Pages

- [[Setite Ship North]]
- [[Redcap Ambush on the Road]]
- [[Greg the Tremere]]
- [[Boat That Sailed on Land]]
- [[Land-Spirit Crossing]]
- [[Lithuanian Hearth Pact]]
- [[Niktuku Attack in Estonia]]
- [[St. Petersburg Sabbat Work]]
- [[Helsinki and the Camarilla News]]
- [[Stockholm]]

## Destination

Stockholm is where Chronicle 2 begins. The pack arrives older, fully Sabbat, and carrying knowledge of Fireflower, land-spirits, Tremere hubris, Cain's tomb rumors, and the consequences of Prague.

title: Open Threads for Chronicle 2
summary: Unresolved hooks carried from Chronicle 1 into Stockholm.
text:
# Open Threads for Chronicle 2

## Personal Threads

- [[Theodosia Byzantios]] still carries the consequences of [[Theodosia's Tzimisce Infection]] and the [[Labyrinth Chasm]].
- [[Katherine Karálová]] left [[Katherine's Hidden Trunk]] in the river near Prague.
- [[Istvan Vlaszy]] has Banu Haqim guidance, the legacy of revenant servitude, and the status of [[Unconquered Banu Haqim]].
- The pack must settle final roles after the time jump. See [[Pack Roles After Prague]].

## Political Threads

- The Camarilla exists by the time the pack reaches the north.
- The Sabbat identity is now explicit for the pack.
- [[Stockholm]] is the next court to infiltrate or reshape.
- The [[Norse Setite Anteaters]] and other northern converts may become allies, rivals, or converts.

## Mythic Threads

- [[Cain's Northern Tomb]] remains unresolved.
- [[Land Spirits and Tzimisce Dominion]] becomes a major frame for future conflicts.
- [[Tremere Origin Confession]] and [[Greg the Tremere]] leave open what the Tremere antitribu future may mean.

title: Orlok Assassination
summary: The Three Keys attack that destroys Count Adrastos von Orlok.
text:
# Orlok Assassination

## Overview

In [[Game 10]], the coterie attacks [[Adrastos von Orlok]] at the [[Three Keys Tavern]] with hidden Salubri aid.

## Event Summary

- [[Dalibor]] enters the gambling room where Orlok is playing dominoes.
- Orlok uses Majesty and exposes [[Istvan Vlaszy]] as tied to Tzimisce interests.
- A Tzimisce defender or construct in the room becomes blood or a living table-like horror.
- The Salubri ally lights a quarterstaff and intentionally rides frenzy to resist supernatural control.
- The Salubri's opened third eye makes melee wounds aggravated.
- [[Father Zemislav]] uses shadow tentacles after darkness returns.
- Orlok is destroyed, but a psychic observer escapes.

## Consequences

- The attack helps trigger the [[Night of Chaos]].
- The coterie flees to the [[Fireflower Anarch Camp]].

## Related

- [[Adrastos von Orlok]]
- [[Salubri War Names]]
- [[Three Keys Tavern]]

title: Pack Roles After Prague
summary: The emerging Sabbat pack roles after the time jump.
text:
# Pack Roles After Prague

## Overview

At the end of [[Game 14]], the group clarifies that the coterie is now a Sabbat pack rather than merely an Anarch-adjacent coterie.

## Discussed Roles

- [[Father Zemislav]] - pack priest.
- [[Istvan Vlaszy]] - likely pack leader, especially after becoming an ancilla Banu Haqim.
- [[Katherine Karalova]] - possible abbot because her Nosferatu instincts could make the haven warren-like and secure.
- [[Theodosia Byzantios]] - possible abbot or seer figure, though her instability and Tzimisce taint complicate leadership.

## Notes

The transcript leaves final role assignment partly open for later play.

## Related

- [[First Fireflower Valderie]]
- [[Stockholm]]

text:
# Pierre de la Salle

![Pierre de la Salle](/wiki/media/Pierre-de-la-Salle.png)

## Overview

Pierre de la Salle imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Chantry, Vampire). Vampire | Nature: Fanatic | Demeanor: Conformist | Concept: Templar Knight.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Vampire
- **Foundry folder:** NPC's / Chantry
- **Foundry name note:** Ali
- **Original Foundry name:** Pierre de la Salle (Ali)
- **Nature:** Fanatic
- **Demeanor:** Conformist
- **Concept:** Templar Knight
- **Advantages:** Willpower 10; Blood Pool 20/20; Path of Christ 5

## Gear

5 freebies spent on Mentor

5 freebies spent on Willpower

2 freebies sepnt on self-conrol

2 frebies spent on concisnece

1 freebies spent on status

## Attributes

| Trait | Rating |
|---|---:|
| Strength | 3 |
| Dexterity (Quick) | 4 |
| Stamina | 3 |
| Charisma | 2 |
| Manipulation | 2 |
| Appearance | 2 |
| Perception | 2 |
| Intelligence | 3 |
| Wits | 3 |

## Abilities

**Talents:** Alertness 1, Athletics 1, Intimidation 1, Leadership 1, Subterfuge 1

**Skills:** Animal Ken 3, Etiquette 3, Melee 3, Performance (Stirring Speeches) 1

**Knowledges:** Academics (Sciences) 1, Enigmas 1, Law (Cannon) 1, Occult 3, Politics 1

## Armors

- **Standard Shield**
- **Class 5 (Knight's Armor)**; VDA 350
  Armor adds its rating to the character’s soak dice pool against bashing damage, lethal damage, and aggravated damage from fangs and claws. It does not protect against fire or sunlight. However, armor also subtracts a number of dice from dice pools related to bodily coordination and agility (most Dexterity-based dice pools). This is reflected in the penalty listing. Attackers may make targeting rolls to hit unprotected portions of a defender and thus ignore the armor (Storyteller assigns difficulty penalty — typically +1 or +2).

## Features, backgrounds, merits, and flaws

- **Generation** (5)
- **Mentor** (5)
- **Mage Blood** (5)
- **Bound by the Concil** (3)
- **Blush of Health** (2)
- **Literacy** (1)
- **Multi-Lingual** (2)
- **Code of Honor** (2)
- **Status(Clan)** (1)
- **Bound to the Clan** (3)
  You have done more than just drink from the blood
  of the elders. Whether as punishment or by choice, you
  have become fully blood bound to Clan Tremere. You
  cannot act against the Clan, and find everything you do
  works in the service of the Tremere. You might not like it,
  but you can't help yourself doing it. Of course, now you
  are bound to the Clan, you cannot be bound to another
  individual. Many older Tremere have this Flaw, as it has
  been common in the past to completely blood bind new
  Tremere.
- **Ardan of Golden Lane** (3)

## Melee Weapons

- **Spear**
- **Lance**

## Disciplines and powers

- **Thaumaturgy** (0)
- **Creatio Ignis (Creation of Fire)** (0); VDA 297
  One success is enough to conjure a flame for illumination and to release it at any target within ten yards. Every additional success gives the flame a further ten yards of range, up to the limit of the thaumaturge's line of sight.
  To hurl the fire at a target in combat, roll Perception + Alertness to hit and apply damage according to the power's level. For complete rules on fire, see page 356; for rules on Rötschreck, see page 358. Thaumaturgic fire causes
  aggravated damage.
  
  Botches: A botch when using Creatio Ignis means that the conjured flame is not under the thaumaturge's control. It immediately spills from the hand to a flammable object within ten feet or, if no such object is present, causes the caster one level of aggravated damage before
  extinguishing itself.
  
  • Candle (difficulty 3 to soak, one health level of aggravated damage/turn)
  
  •• Palm of flame (difficulty 4 to soak, one health level of aggravated damage/turn)
  
  ••• Campfire (difficulty 5 to soak, two health levels of aggravated damage/turn)
  
  •••• Bonfire (difficulty 7 to soak, two health levels of aggravated damage/turn)
  
  ••••• Inferno (difficulty 9 to soak, three health levels of aggravated damage/turn)
- **●●●●● The Lure of Flames** (0); V20 218
  The number of successes determines how accurately the vampire places the flame in his desired location (declared before the roll is made). One success is all that is necessary to conjure a flame in one's hand, while five successes place a flame anywhere in the Kindred's line of sight. Less successes mean that the flame appears somewhere at the Storyteller's discretion -- as a rough rule of thumb, the thaumaturge can accurately place a flame within 10 yards or meters of themselves per success.
  
  Individual descriptions are not provided for each level of this path -- fire is fire, after all (including potentially causing frenzy in other vampires witnessing it). The chart below describes the path level required to generate a specific amount of flame. To soak the damage at all, a vampire must have the Fortitude Discipline. Fire under the caster's control does not harm the vampire or cause him to frenzy, but fires started as a result of the unnatural flame affect the thaumaturge normally.
  
  • Candle (difficulty 3 to soak, one health level of aggravated damage/turn)
  
  •• Palm of flame (difficulty 4 to soak, one health level of aggravated damage/turn)
  
  ••• Campfire (difficulty 5 to soak, two health levels of aggravated damage/turn)
  
  •••• Bonfire (difficulty 7 to soak, two health levels of aggravated damage/turn)
  
  ••••• Inferno (difficulty 9 to soak, three health levels of aggravated damage/turn)
- **Iter Pernix (Swift Journey)** (0); VDA 297
  Choose a number of targets and roll for successes. Each success allows the targets to move along roads at a speed of thirty miles per hour/forty-five km per hour for one hour without need of rest or any negative after-effects. It has no effect on movement speeds in combat, only for long-distance travel. Botches: Instead of speeding up travel, a botch when invoking Iter Pernix causes mounts to become difficult to handle, slowing movement to half speed
  for the next hour if they are connected to wagons or possibly to wander off-course if they are not.
  
  • One being (a mount or walking human)
  •• Up to six mounts or one laden wagon
  ••• Up to twelve mounts or four supply wagons
  •••• Up to 24 mounts or eight supply wagons or
  one siege engine
  ••••• Up to 48 mounts or sixteen supply wagons or
  four siege engines
- **Movement of the Mind** (0); V20 220
  The number of successes indicates the duration of the caster's control over the object (or subject). Each success allows one turn of manipulation, though the Kindred may attempt to maintain control after this time by making a new roll (she need not spend additional blood to maintain control). If the roll is successful, control is maintained. If a thaumaturge loses or relaxes control over an object and later manipulates it again, her player must spend another blood point, as a new attempt is being made. Five or more successes on the initial roll means the vampire can control the object for duration of the scene.
  
  If this power is used to manipulate a living being, the subject may attempt to resist. In this case, the caster and the subject make opposed Willpower rolls each turn the control is exercised.
  
  Like The Lure of Flames, individual power levels are not provided for this path -- consult the chart below to see how much weight a thaumaturge may control. Once a Kindred reaches a rating of 3, she may levitate herself and "fly" at approximately running speed, no matter how much she weighs, though the weight restrictions apply if she manipulates other objects or subjects. Once a Kindred achieves 4, she may "throw" objects at a Strength equal to her level of mastery of this path.
  
  • One pound/one-half kilogram
  •• 20 pounds/10 kilograms
  ••• 200 pounds/100 kilograms
  •••• 500 pounds/250 kilograms
  ••••• 1000 pounds/500 kilograms
- **●●●●● Movement of the Mind** (0); V20 220
  The number of successes indicates the duration of the caster's control over the object (or subject). Each success allows one turn of manipulation, though the Kindred may attempt to maintain control after this time by making a new roll (she need not spend additional blood to maintain control). If the roll is successful, control is maintained. If a thaumaturge loses or relaxes control over an object and later manipulates it again, her player must spend another blood point, as a new attempt is being made. Five or more successes on the initial roll means the vampire can control the object for duration of the scene.

- **Class 5 (Knight's Armor)**; VDA 350
  Armor adds its rating to the character’s soak dice pool against bashing damage, lethal damage, and aggravated damage from fangs and claws. It does not protect against fire or sunlight. However, armor also subtracts a number of dice from dice pools related to bodily coordination and agility (most Dexterity-based dice pools). This is reflected in the penalty listing.   You have done more than just drink from the blood
  of the elders. Whether as punishment or by choice, you
  have become fully blood bound to Clan Tremere. You
  cannot act against the Clan, and find everything you do
  works in the service of the Tremere. You might not like it,
  but you can't help yourself doing it. Of course, now you
  are bound to the Clan, you cannot be bound to another
  individual. Many older Tremere have this Flaw, as it has

  Tremere.

  To hurl the fire at a target in combat, roll Perception + Alertness to hit and apply damage according to the power's level. For complete rules on fire, see page 356; for rules on Rötschreck, see page 358. Thaumaturgic fire causes
  aggravated damage.
  
  Botches: A botch when using Creatio Ignis means that the conjured flame is not under the thaumaturge's control. It immediately spills from the hand to a flammable object within ten feet or, if no such object is present, causes the caster one level of aggravated damage before
  extinguishing itself.

  The number of successes determines how accurately the vampire places the flame in his desired location (declared before the roll is made). One success is all that is necessary to conjure a flame in one's hand, while five successes place a flame anywhere in the Kindred's line of sight. Less successes mean that the flame appears somewhere at the Storyteller's discretion -- as a rough rule of thumb, the thaumaturge can accurately place a flame within 10 yards or meters of themselves per success.

title: Prague
summary: Campaign city and political pressure cooker.
text:
# Prague

## Overview

The first two sessions take place in Prague in 1433, during the Hussite crisis. Mortal politics, curfew, crusader pressure, and Kindred elder politics all shape movement through the city.

## District notes

- The castle district lies across the river and includes wealthy makers and high-status places.
- The western part of Old Town is the [[Jewish Quarter]].
- The map marks Elysium-like locations with skulls, the university with a book, late markets with coins, and known Tremere space with a tower.

## Appears in

- [[Game 1]]
- [[Game 2]]

title: Prague Curfew and Kindred Travel
summary: Curfew and movement constraints in 1433 Prague.
text:
# Prague Curfew and Kindred Travel

## Confirmed facts

- Curfew begins around market close.
- Characters can attempt to move openly with excuses, avoid the town guard, use rooftops, arrange escorts, or lean on contacts.
- [[Jardani Jovonovich]] has Hussite/town guard contacts who can help the coterie move through the city.
- The [[Jewish Quarter]] is not locked like many European Jewish quarters, but Jews seen outside the gates without leaving the city can be arrested.

## Game use

Travel choices matter because the coterie repeatedly moves across the city at night while carrying contraband, following revenant leads, or avoiding attention.
