title: Prince Josef Zvi
summary: Prince Josef Zvi imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Elders, Vampire). Vampire.
text:
# Prince Josef Zvi

![Prince Josef Zvi](/wiki/media/Prince-Josef-Zvi.jpg)

## Overview

Prince Josef Zvi imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Elders, Vampire). Vampire.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Vampire
- **Foundry folder:** NPC's / Elders
- **Foundry name note:** Nos
- **Original Foundry name:** Prince Josef Zvi (Nos)
- **Advantages:** Willpower 4; Blood Pool 0/15

## Background

**Josef, the Shadow of the Ghetto**

Josef was born in the Jewish Quarter of Prague, a community whose resilience shaped his early life. The son of a respected rabbi, he was expected to follow in his father's footsteps, devoting himself to religious study and scholarship. At twelve, however, tragedy struck: his father fell ill and died, leaving Josef the family's sole provider. His scholarly dreams abandoned, Josef apprenticed with a butcher to support his household, exchanging holy texts for cleavers and livestock, yet he kept his father's teachings close.

Life was harsh, and violence against Prague's Jewish community all too common. One fateful night, Josef himself fell victim to brutality when a gang of local boys attacked him. His head was crushed, his nose shattered, and his jaw dislocated in the frenzied assault. As he lay dying, an enigmatic figure approached: Yehuda, a Nosferatu who had long watched over the Quarter's people from the shadows. With Josef's life fading, Yehuda offered him a second chance--a Cainite Embrace. When Yehuda asked if Josef desired revenge, Josef denied it; when asked if he would defend others from such brutality, Josef squeezed his hand in silent acceptance.

Yehuda guided Josef through the early nights of Cainite existence, teaching him the ways of the Nosferatu. But soon, Yehuda departed east, leaving Josef to watch over his people alone. In his mentor's absence, Josef took up the mantle of protector. Brutal acts against the Jews were met with swift retaliation from the Nosferatu avenger, who claimed the ghetto as his de facto domain. Other Cainites largely ignored the Jewish Quarter, granting Josef the freedom to defend it as his own, an unspoken guardian who preserved its peace in shadow.

However, Josef bore a dark curse: a craving for the blood of children. Unable to resist, he fed exclusively from Christian and pagan youth. Though he took extreme care to minimize harm, deaths inevitably occurred, leaving him haunted by guilt. This shame was a secret he guarded fiercely, aware of the great risk it posed to his cause and his conscience.

Josef's hold over the Jewish Quarter soon drew the ire of Prague's ruling Prince, Ventrue elder Rudolf Brandl, who sought to exploit the ghetto as a feeding ground for his allies. Brandl's demands grew, culminating in an ultimatum: open the Quarter to the prince's brood or face destruction. But Josef refused, unwilling to betray those he had sworn to protect. The prince's minions came for him, and Josef's Final Death seemed certain--until he eluded capture, retreating into the labyrinthine catacombs beneath Prague Castle, Brandl's own haven.

Under cover of day, Josef slipped into Brandl's lair and, while the prince lay helplessly at rest, exacted a cold and calculated vengeance. The death of Prince Brandl sparked a power vacuum, and Josef seized the opportunity to consolidate his control. Gaining critical support from the Brujah elder Ecaterina the Wise and Garinol the Cappadocian, he secured Prague as a haven for the Jewish people and those oppressed across Europe. This alliance granted Josef enough strength to reign over Prague for centuries, transforming the city into a sanctuary.

## Attributes

| Trait | Rating |
|---|---:|
| Strength | 4 |
| Dexterity | 4 |
| Stamina | 3 |
| Charisma | 1 |
| Manipulation | 4 |
| Appearance | 1 |
| Perception | 2 |
| Intelligence | 5 |
| Wits | 2 |

## Abilities

**Talents:** Alertness 1, Athletics 1, Awareness 2, Brawl 4, Intimidation 1, Leadership 2, Subterfuge 3

**Skills:** Etiquette 2, Melee 3, Performance 4, Stealth 4, Survival 2

**Knowledges:** Academics 1, Investigation 1, Occult 3, Politics 2

## Disciplines and powers

- **Animalism** (0)
  The Cainite Beast is a primal, animalistic force. While most Cainites will never know their Beasts on a fun-damental, such a creature speaks to his own. The vampire with Animalism pushes that to extremes, commanding legions of beasts to do his bidding.Without the Animal Ken Skill or the AnimalismDiscipline, animals become uncomfortable in a vampire's presence. Some attack, some run, some cause commotion.Vampires with Animalism present a dominant air, attracting such creatures and cowing them.
- **Obfuscate** (0)
  To many common men and women living in the 13th century, the Devil lurks in every shadow and hides behind every corner. Evil is ever-present and a threat that canrarely be seen with your eyes.
  
  In the case of those Cainites who have mastered Obfuscate, this paranoia is justified. While evil may be debatable, Obfuscate makes it so that a gifted vampire could be lurking in the darkness ready to strike or flee at any time. The monster must be close at hand to accomplish this, no more than five meters per dots of Wits + Stealth from their would-be victims.
  
  Most mortals, even those trained in Awareness, cannot pierce the monster's deception. Some unique individuals may be able to see straight through these lies; children, horses, dogs, some cats, and very few religious visionaries are unaffected by Obfuscate, but this is all at Storyteller discretion.
  
  Cainites with the Discipline of Auspex may be able to pierce a Cainite's Obfuscate. For more, see Auspexonp. 195.
  
  Most Obfuscate powers last a scene once activated, unless willed away by the Cainite. They take no particular concentration to uphold once raised.
- **Potence** (0)
  For each dot of Potence the vampire possesses, add one die to all Strength-based dice pools. Spend one blood point reflexively to turn those dice into automatic successes for the turn. In hand to hand combat, this means automatic successes on the damage roll.
- **The Silence of Death** (1)
  There is no roll. The power creates a zone of absolute silence around the character, with a maximum radius of 7 meters. No sound occurs inside this zone, though sounds originating outside the area of effect maybe heard by anyone in it. Additionally, if the player spends two blood points and the character spends five turns inconcentration in a room or enclosure, the silence remainsin it after the character leaves the room and lasts until she wills it to end. A character can't create more than one zone of silence, so if she wishes to use Silence of Death on herself (or curse another room), the zone in the first room fades automatically.
- **Unseen Presence** (2)
  There is no roll necessary to conjure an Unseen Presence. Should a Cainite draw attention to herself by sneak attack, blunder, or speech, accidental attention will call for a Wits + Stealth roll, difficulty from 5 to 9, based on the amount of noise or calamity the Cainite has caused. Willful attempts to violate the secrecy of Unseen Presence while still maintaining it may call for a high difficulty and multiple successes. For example, whispering to an enemy without giving away her location would require three or so successes at a difficulty of 7. Even when seen or unseen, a secondary use of this power allows a Cainite to conceal small items already on her person, or with a successful Manipulation + Subterfuge from 5 to 9, based on the amount of observation she can grasp and conceal an easily moved item.
- **Mask of a Thousand Faces** (3)
  To create this guise, the player rolls Manipulation + Performance at a difficulty 7. To mimic the face of another, the vampire must have a good look at them. The Storyteller might raise the difficulty on the roll if the vampire has only briefly seen the person they wish to mimic. How successful the disguise is depends on the chart at the top of the page. Should a vampire wish to create a face much more appealing than her own, she must spend a point of blood for every dot of difference from her Appearance to that of the disguise. While fooling acquaintances may require only a look at the target and clever crafting of the mask, fooling someone intimately connected to the target is another matter. That requires actual familiarity with the target, and perhaps other rolls to keep the illusion going.
  
  1 success  The vampire's mass remains the same. She cannot be taller or shorter or much bulkier, but she may change enough features so as to be hard to recognize (though she might be mistaken for a blood relative). A Nosferatu, for example, can appear as a perfectly believable, albeit tragically hideous human.
  
  2 successes  Her appearance is now a complete mask, and she resembles herself in no way, should she wish. She could not be mistaken for her own blood relative even in strong lantern light. She can now change how others perceive her gender.
  
  3 successes  Her intention is made complete, and she wears the mask she intends to wear. Her face is her own canvas.
  
  4 successes  The illusion is now so complete that the victims will imagine the vampire to sound exactly as the mask suggests. If it is a specific person, the illusion forces the victims to imagine the vampire is performing the correctt witches and physical mannerisms of the person they are mimicking.
  
  5 successes  The transformation is complete. Even age and size no longer hold the vampire back. She can make her victims  see her as anyone she wants, just by willing it so.
- **Vanish From The Mind’s Eye** (4)
  The player rolls her character's Intelligence + Stealth against their victim's Wits + Alertness. If there is more than one victim, use the highest pool to resist. If the player rolls one to three successes, the vampire fades but does not vanish, appearing ghost-like and translucent. With more than three successes, the vampire vanishes from her victim's senses entirely. If the player achieves more successes than the victim, the victim forgets that the vampire was there to begin with, and tends to not reflecton any conflict in memory that might cause.
  
  It is possible to interact with a ghosted vampire, though difficult. A player tracking such a vampire rolls Perception + Alertness at a difficulty of 8. On a successful roll, the character can interact normally with the ghostlike vampire, though they are likely to be disturbed by hisethereal presence. On a failure, the character loses two dice on any roll to interact with the ghostlike vampire, from attacking them to following them to seducing them.
  
  After witnessing a vampire vanish before their eyes ,the player of any other character present must roll Wits + Courage. For humans, this is difficulty 9; for Cainites, difficulty 5. If the player succeeds, the character may act as expected in the situation. A failure on this roll means that the character is beside themselves and unable to act for two turns, unless that action is to flee.
- **Flaw 2: Ragged Bite** (0)
  Unlike your Cainite peers,you lack the ability to neatly seal up the wound caused by feeding. Instead, you leave torn, bleeding marks which have a chance of becoming infected. Your target must make a Stamina roll (difficulty the number of damage caused + 5) to resist infection. The impact of such a notable wound in conjunction with a diseased target is left to the discretion of the Storyteller. At the very least, this poses complications in hiding your identity as anything other than a monster.
- **Flaw 2: Territorial** (0)
  You have staked out a claim to a certain area of land and a certain group of mortals and defend it to the point of irrationality. If you catch another vampire hunting on your turf, check for frenzy immediately. Even if you do not frenzy, the thought alone is enough to make you violent. Note that without the necessary backgrounds, your "territory" means nothing to anyone other than you.
- **Flaw 2: Clan Flaw Ventrue** (0)
  Can only feed from Children.
- **Flaw 1: Prey Exclusion** (0)
  Only Feed on Pagan and Christian Children
- **Merit 1: Literacy** (0)
  You are a learned scholar, able to both read and write. The Merit assumes that you can read and write with fluency in your native tongue, as well as Latin and any languages you may have gained through the Multi-Lingual Merit. In some chronicles ,literacy can be assumed. In these cases, Illiteracy becomes a 1-point Flaw.
- **Merit 4: Sturdy** (0)
  Training, hardships, or natural ability have inured your body to pain and damage. While you are not necessarily a hulking brute, anyone who has seen you in action knows that you can take more damage than the average individual. You gain an additional Bruised health level and the Storyteller may award you with any appropriate bonuses when your innate toughness comes into play.
- **Background: Domain 5** (1)
- **Background: Generation 4** (1)
- **Background: Status 3** (1)
- **Background: Allies 2** (1)
- **Elemental Mastery** (0); V20 214
  This path allows a vampire limited control over and communion with inanimate objects. Elemental Mastery can only be used to affect the unliving -- a vampire could not cause a tree to walk by using Animate the Unmoving, for instance. Thaumaturges who seek mastery over living things generally study paths such as The Green Path (p. 215).
- **● Elemental Strength** (1); V20 214
  The player allocates a total of three temporary bonus dots between the character's Strength and Stamina. The number of successes on the roll to activate the power is the number of turns these dots remain. The player may spend a Willpower point to increase this duration by one turn. This power cannot be "stacked" -- one application must expire before the next can be made.
- **●●●●● Summon Elemental** (5); V20 215
  The character must be near some quantity of the classical element corresponding to the spirit he wishes to invoke. The spirit invoked may or may not actually follow the caster's instructions once summoned, but generally will at least pay rough attention to what it's being told to do. The number of successes gained on the Willpower roll determines the power level of the elemental.
  
  The elemental has three dots in all Physical and Mental Attributes. One dot may be added to one of the elemental's Physical Attributes for each success gained by the caster on the initial roll. The Storyteller should determine the elemental's Abilities, attacks, and damage, and any special powers it has related to its element.
  
  Once the elemental has been summoned, the thaumaturge must exert control over it. The more powerful the elemental, the more difficult a task this is. The player rolls Manipulation + Occult (difficulty of the number of successes scored on the casting roll + 4), and the number of successes determines the degree of control:
  
  **Successes**
  **Failure**
  
  Botch
  The elemental immediately attacks the thaumaturge.
  
  Failure
  The elemental goes free and may attack anyone or leave the scene at the Storyteller's discretion.
  
  1 Success
  The elemental does not attack its summoner.
  
  2 Successes
  The elemental behaves favorably toward the summoner and may perform a service in exchange for payment (determined by the Storyteller).
  
  3 Successes
  The elemental performs one service, within reason.
  
  4 Successes
  The elemental performs any one task for the caster that does not jeopardize its own existence.
  
  5 Successes
  The elemental performs any task that the caster sets for it, even one that may take several nights to complete or that places its existence at risk.
- **●●● Animate the Unmoving** (3); V20 214
  This power requires the expenditure of a Willpower point with less than four successes on the roll. Each use of this power animates one object no larger than human-sized; the caster may simultaneously control a number of animate objects equal to his Intelligence rating. Objects animated by this power stay animated as long as they are within the caster's line of sight or up to an hour, although the thaumaturge can take other actions during that time.
- **●●●● Elemental Form** (4); V20 214
  The number of successes determines how completely the character takes the shape she wishes to counterfeit. At least three successes are required for the character to use her senses or Disciplines while in her altered form. This power lasts for the remainder of the night, although the character may return to her normal form at will.
- **●● Wooden Tongues** (2); V20 214
  The number of successes dictates the amount and relevance of the information that the character receives. One success may yield a boulder's memory of a forest fire, while three may indicate that it remembers a shadowy figure running past, and five will cause the rock to relate a precise description of a local Gangrel.
- **● Elemental Strength** (1); V20 214
  The player allocates a total of three temporary bonus dots between the character's Strength and Stamina. The number of successes on the roll to activate the power is the number of turns these dots remain. The player may spend a Willpower point to increase this duration by one turn. This power cannot be "stacked" -- one application must expire before the next can be made.
- **●●● Animate the Unmoving** (3); V20 214
  This power requires the expenditure of a Willpower point with less than four successes on the roll. Each use of this power animates one object no larger than human-sized; the caster may simultaneously control a number of animate objects equal to his Intelligence rating. Objects animated by this power stay animated as long as they are within the caster's line of sight or up to an hour, although the thaumaturge can take other actions during that time.
- **●● Wooden Tongues** (2); V20 214
  The number of successes dictates the amount and relevance of the information that the character receives. One success may yield a boulder's memory of a forest fire, while three may indicate that it remembers a shadowy figure running past, and five will cause the rock to relate a precise description of a local Gangrel.
- **●●●●● Potence** (0); V20 192
  Each dot that the vampire has in Potence adds one die to all Strength-related dice rolls. Further, the player can spend one blood point and change his Potence dice into an equal number of automatic successes to all Strength-related rolls for the turn. In melee and brawling combat, successes from Potence (either rolled or automatic) are applied to the damage roll results.

## Traits

- **Legerdemain** (0)
- **Archery** (0)
- **Commerce** (0)
- **Ride** (0)
- **Hearth Wisdom** (0)
- **Seneschal** (0)
- **Theology** (0)

## Source

- Foundry world archive: aoe.zip
- Actor ID: RvLpsGlejNtVvokU
- Actor folder: NPC's / Elders

## Session notes

- [[Game 1]] - his authority frames the investigation; correspondence shows he refused Radomir's request to expel or enable attacks on Tremere domain.
- [[Game 1]] - the [[Jewish Quarter]] is under his particular protection.

## Game 3-4 notes

- Game 4 suggests Radomir's secret work may have fed attempts to help Prince Josef resist antediluvian pressure.
- [[The Librarian]] says Josef may listen if the coterie can show his own agents are escalating Prague toward war.
- A meeting is arranged in the [[Castle Catacombs]].

## Game 5-8 notes

- In [[Game 5]], Prince Josef receives the coterie privately at the [[Jewish Quarter Hidden Yeshiva]].
- He gives the [[Prince Josef's Abomination Commission]] and performs [[Prince Josef's Arm Ritual]] on [[Istvan Vlaszy]].
- His actions suggest he is seeking tools or knowledge against ancient vampiric influence, but he remains politically evasive about Tremere crimes.
playerText:
# Prince Josef Zvi

![Prince Josef Zvi](/wiki/media/Prince-Josef-Zvi.jpg)

## Overview

Prince Josef Zvi imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Elders, Vampire). Vampire.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Vampire
- **Foundry folder:** NPC's / Elders
- **Foundry name note:** Nos
- **Original Foundry name:** Prince Josef Zvi (Nos)
- **Advantages:** Willpower 4; Blood Pool 0/15

## Background

**Josef, the Shadow of the Ghetto**

Josef was born in the Jewish Quarter of Prague, a community whose resilience shaped his early life. The son of a respected rabbi, he was expected to follow in his father's footsteps, devoting himself to religious study and scholarship. At twelve, however, tragedy struck: his father fell ill and died, leaving Josef the family's sole provider. His scholarly dreams abandoned, Josef apprenticed with a butcher to support his household, exchanging holy texts for cleavers and livestock, yet he kept his father's teachings close.

Life was harsh, and violence against Prague's Jewish community all too common. One fateful night, Josef himself fell victim to brutality when a gang of local boys attacked him. His head was crushed, his nose shattered, and his jaw dislocated in the frenzied assault. As he lay dying, an enigmatic figure approached: Yehuda, a Nosferatu who had long watched over the Quarter's people from the shadows. With Josef's life fading, Yehuda offered him a second chance--a Cainite Embrace. When Yehuda asked if Josef desired revenge, Josef denied it; when asked if he would defend others from such brutality, Josef squeezed his hand in silent acceptance.

Yehuda guided Josef through the early nights of Cainite existence, teaching him the ways of the Nosferatu. But soon, Yehuda departed east, leaving Josef to watch over his people alone. In his mentor's absence, Josef took up the mantle of protector. Brutal acts against the Jews were met with swift retaliation from the Nosferatu avenger, who claimed the ghetto as his de facto domain. Other Cainites largely ignored the Jewish Quarter, granting Josef the freedom to defend it as his own, an unspoken guardian who preserved its peace in shadow.

However, Josef bore a dark curse: a craving for the blood of children. Unable to resist, he fed exclusively from Christian and pagan youth. Though he took extreme care to minimize harm, deaths inevitably occurred, leaving him haunted by guilt. This shame was a secret he guarded fiercely, aware of the great risk it posed to his cause and his conscience.

Josef's hold over the Jewish Quarter soon drew the ire of Prague's ruling Prince, Ventrue elder Rudolf Brandl, who sought to exploit the ghetto as a feeding ground for his allies. Brandl's demands grew, culminating in an ultimatum: open the Quarter to the prince's brood or face destruction. But Josef refused, unwilling to betray those he had sworn to protect. The prince's minions came for him, and Josef's Final Death seemed certain--until he eluded capture, retreating into the labyrinthine catacombs beneath Prague Castle, Brandl's own haven.

Under cover of day, Josef slipped into Brandl's lair and, while the prince lay helplessly at rest, exacted a cold and calculated vengeance. The death of Prince Brandl sparked a power vacuum, and Josef seized the opportunity to consolidate his control. Gaining critical support from the Brujah elder Ecaterina the Wise and Garinol the Cappadocian, he secured Prague as a haven for the Jewish people and those oppressed across Europe. This alliance granted Josef enough strength to reign over Prague for centuries, transforming the city into a sanctuary.

## Attributes

| Trait | Rating |
|---|---:|
| Strength | 4 |
| Dexterity | 4 |
| Stamina | 3 |
| Charisma | 1 |
| Manipulation | 4 |
| Appearance | 1 |
| Perception | 2 |
| Intelligence | 5 |
| Wits | 2 |

## Abilities

**Talents:** Alertness 1, Athletics 1, Awareness 2, Brawl 4, Intimidation 1, Leadership 2, Subterfuge 3

**Skills:** Etiquette 2, Melee 3, Performance 4, Stealth 4, Survival 2

**Knowledges:** Academics 1, Investigation 1, Occult 3, Politics 2

## Disciplines and powers

- **Animalism** (0)
  The Cainite Beast is a primal, animalistic force. While most Cainites will never know their Beasts on a fun-damental, such a creature speaks to his own. The vampire with Animalism pushes that to extremes, commanding legions of beasts to do his bidding.Without the Animal Ken Skill or the AnimalismDiscipline, animals become uncomfortable in a vampire's presence. Some attack, some run, some cause commotion.Vampires with Animalism present a dominant air, attracting such creatures and cowing them.
- **Obfuscate** (0)
  To many common men and women living in the 13th century, the Devil lurks in every shadow and hides behind every corner. Evil is ever-present and a threat that canrarely be seen with your eyes.
  
  In the case of those Cainites who have mastered Obfuscate, this paranoia is justified. While evil may be debatable, Obfuscate makes it so that a gifted vampire could be lurking in the darkness ready to strike or flee at any time. The monster must be close at hand to accomplish this, no more than five meters per dots of Wits + Stealth from their would-be victims.
  
  Most mortals, even those trained in Awareness, cannot pierce the monster's deception. Some unique individuals may be able to see straight through these lies; children, horses, dogs, some cats, and very few religious visionaries are unaffected by Obfuscate, but this is all at Storyteller discretion.
  
  Cainites with the Discipline of Auspex may be able to pierce a Cainite's Obfuscate. For more, see Auspexonp. 195.
  
  Most Obfuscate powers last a scene once activated, unless willed away by the Cainite. They take no particular concentration to uphold once raised.
- **Potence** (0)

Additionally, if the player spends two blood points and the character spends five turns inconcentration in a room or enclosure, the silence remainsin it after the character leaves the room and lasts until she wills it to end. A character can't create more than one zone of silence, so if she wishes to use Silence of Death on herself (or curse another room), the zone in the first room fades automatically.
- **Unseen Presence** (2)

Should a Cainite draw attention to herself by sneak attack, blunder, or speech, accidental attention will call for a Wits + Stealth roll, difficulty from 5 to 9, based on the amount of noise or calamity the Cainite has caused. Willful attempts to violate the secrecy of Unseen Presence while still maintaining it may call for a high difficulty and multiple successes. For example, whispering to an enemy without giving away her location would require three or so successes at a difficulty of 7. Even when seen or unseen, a secondary use of this power allows a Cainite to conceal small items already on her person, or with a successful Manipulation + Subterfuge from 5 to 9, based on the amount of observation she can grasp and conceal an easily moved item.

Your target must make a Stamina roll (difficulty the number of damage caused + 5) to resist infection. At the very least, this poses complications in hiding your identity as anything other than a monster.

title: Prince Josef's Abomination Commission
summary: Prince Josef asks the coterie to investigate whether Tremere-created abominations are a true threat to Prague.
text:
# Prince Josef's Abomination Commission

## Overview

During the private audience in [[Game 5]], [[Prince Josef Zvi]] accepts that [[Jakub of Thessaloniki (Zakariya ibn Masud al-Muntazir)]] killed [[Radomir Pavlovich]], then redirects the coterie toward the Tremere.

## Charge

- Determine whether Tremere abominations are mere trinkets, entertainments, experiments, or a threat to Prague.
- Keep the information contained enough to preserve court stability.
- Expect other factions to be pursuing the same information.

## Reward

The Prince implies renown or prestation may follow if the coterie brings useful proof.

## Appears In

- [[Game 5]]
playerText:
# Prince Josef's Abomination Commission

## Overview

During the private audience in [[Game 5]], [[Prince Josef Zvi]] accepts that [[Jakub of Thessaloniki (Zakariya ibn Masud al-Muntazir)]] killed [[Radomir Pavlovich]], then redirects the coterie toward the Tremere.

## Charge

- Determine whether Tremere abominations are mere trinkets, entertainments, experiments, or a threat to Prague.
- Keep the information contained enough to preserve court stability.
- Expect other factions to be pursuing the same information.

## Reward

The Prince implies renown or prestation may follow if the coterie brings useful proof.

## Appears In

- [[Game 5]]
links: Jakub of Thessaloniki (Zakariya ibn Masud al-Muntazir)

title: Prince Josef's Arm Ritual
summary: Prince Josef removes Istvan's arm and burns it into ruby-like crystals to expose ancient bloodline elements.
text:
# Prince Josef's Arm Ritual

## Overview

In [[Game 5]], [[Prince Josef Zvi]] demands [[Istvan Vlaszy]]'s right arm. He removes it, speaks in Aramaic, and burns the arm into tiny ruby-like crystals.

## Observed Result

- The ritual appears to extract or crystallize elements of Istvan's revenant blood.
- The coterie concludes that Josef has confirmed Istvan's true domitor or ancient bloodline connection.
- Istvan fears this exposes him to [[Shagra]] and may endanger his family.

## Uncertain

- The exact ritual tradition is not identified.
- Whether Josef learned something new or confirmed a suspicion is unclear.

title: Prince Joseph and Antediluvian Influence
summary: Radomir's work may have been tied to Prince Josef's fear of elder or antediluvian control.
text:
# Prince Joseph and Antediluvian Influence

## Overview

Game 4 reframes Radomir's secret Tremere dealings as possibly serving one of [[Prince Josef Zvi]]'s larger fears: that antediluvian or ancient vampiric influence was pressing on Prague and its ruler.

## Confirmed Details

- Mira says Radomir sought esoteric secrets that could help the Prince resist or understand antediluvian influence.
- The Librarian knows of the pressure on Prince Josef and the history behind his paranoia.
- The Prince may have benefited from Radomir's work without knowing its full criminal cost.

## Historical Context From The Librarian

- Josef once created or contacted a protector for Prague.
- That protector killed many vampires and disrupted rival networks.
- The previous prince tried to have Josef killed by day.
- Josef prepared for the attack and overthrew the old prince.

## Appears In

- [[Game 4]]

title: Radomir Pavlovich
summary: Destroyed Tzimisce elder whose murder launches the chronicle investigation.
text:
# Radomir Pavlovich

## Overview

Radomir Pavlovich was a Tzimisce elder in Prague and the representative/contact point for powerful Tzimisce voivodes outside the city. His destruction before Game 1 is the inciting crisis of the chronicle.

## Appears in

- [[Game 1]] - named by [[Alessandro di Ravello]] as the murdered elder.
- [[Game 2]] - his revenants and ritual research become the focus of the investigation.

## Relationships

- [[Sabina Premysl]] - used by Radomir as a vessel for spirit-communion research.
- [[Dragan Premysl]] - protector/enforcer loyal to Radomir.
- [[Istvan Vlaszy]] - courier for Radomir's packages.
- [[Borislav Premysl]] - revenant servant associated with his household.
- [[Prince Josef Zvi]] - corresponded with Radomir about Tremere domain.

## Associated locations

- [[Radomir Pavlovich's Haven]]
- [[Remote Tzimisce Castle]]

## Associated documents and clues

- [[Radomir's Missing Ritual Book]]
- [[Radomir's Tremere Correspondence]]
- [[Stone of Hearts Wandering]]
- [[Spirit Communion Ritual]]

## Notes

Only facts explicitly surfaced in the transcripts are included here. The identity of Radomir's killer remains unconfirmed.

## Game 3-4 notes

- [[Dragan Premysl]] and [[Mira Vlaszy]] reveal that Radomir traded Cainites or bodies to the Tremere for magical secrets.
- His work may have been connected to [[Prince Joseph and Antediluvian Influence]], but the crimes remain his own.
- Sabina's abduction may be tied to knowledge Radomir tried to transmit through spirits.

## Game 5-8 notes

- In [[Game 5]], the coterie reports to [[Prince Josef Zvi]] that [[Jakub of Thessaloniki (Zakariya ibn Masud al-Muntazir)]] killed Radomir.
- [[Graziano de Veronese]] says Radomir was investigating the Fireflower and the nature of blood bonds, which made him a target.

title: Radomir Pavlovich Investigation
summary: The open murder investigation driving the first two sessions.
text:
# Radomir Pavlovich Investigation

## Confirmed facts

- [[Radomir Pavlovich]] was a Tzimisce elder in Prague and a representative/contact point for Tzimisce voivodes outside the city.
- His destruction is serious enough that [[Alessandro di Ravello]] summons the coterie and gives them a mandate to investigate.
- The attack occurred at [[Radomir Pavlovich's Haven]].
- Radomir had multiple revenants under his control, including protectors and couriers/communications servants.
- [[Sabina Premysl]] says Radomir was occupied by a ritual involving spirits when he died, and believes the ritual was intended to lower his defenses.

## Leads

- [[Istvan Vlaszy]] carried packages for Radomir and knows of a courier route to a [[Remote Tzimisce Castle]].
- [[Dragan Premysl]] and [[Sabina Premysl]] are direct witnesses to Radomir's household and research.
- [[Radomir's Missing Ritual Book]] appears central to the murder.
- [[Tremere-Tzimisce War]] correspondence makes Tremere involvement plausible but unconfirmed.
- A [[Mysterious Crusader Agent]] or less-than-human crusader presence may be connected.

## Theories

- The ritual package may have been bait sent to Radomir by an enemy who knew his interests.
- The Tremere may be involved, but the transcript preserves this as suspicion rather than proof.
- The gargoyle attack suggests the conspirators may be monitoring attempts to contact the spirit witness.

title: Radomir Pavlovich's Haven
summary: Radomir's attacked haven and first investigation scene.
text:
# Radomir Pavlovich's Haven

## Overview

The haven of [[Radomir Pavlovich]], found after his destruction. A sale notice is nailed to the door, and the property is later purchased by Dalibor through retainers.

## Important events

- The coterie searches the house in [[Game 1]].
- They find revenant blood, ritual markings, missing books, correspondence, [[Stone of Hearts Wandering]], and the [[Bronze Fleshcrafted Vampire Statue]].
- The house is later targeted by arson; a suspect is taken to [[Castle Jail]].
- [[Istvan Vlaszy]] was the second-highest bidder at auction.

## Appears in

- [[Game 1]]
- [[Game 2]]

title: Radomir's Cainite Sales to the Tremere
summary: Radomir sold Cainites or bodies to the Tremere in exchange for magical secrets and war knowledge.
text:
# Radomir's Cainite Sales to the Tremere

## Overview

[[Dragan Premysl]] reveals that [[Radomir Pavlovich]] secretly traded Cainites to the Tremere for magical texts, war secrets, and other esoteric knowledge. [[Mira Vlaszy]] later confirms that she helped deliver bodies to the [[Golden Lane Shop]] under Radomir's arrangements.

## Confirmed Details

- Radomir was receiving Tremere magical knowledge, including war-related or warding knowledge.
- The trade involved Cainites or Cainite bodies that Radomir could not openly dispose of.
- Mira served as one of Radomir's communication and delivery agents inside Prague.
- The Prince may not have known the full source of Radomir's knowledge.

## Open Questions

- Exactly how many victims were delivered is not stated.
- Whether Radomir believed the ends justified the means is clear from the testimony, but not a defense of his actions.

## Appears In

- [[Game 3]]
- [[Game 4]]

title: Radomir's Missing Ritual Book
summary: The missing locked ritual book.
text:
# Radomir's Missing Ritual Book

## Overview

A missing book from [[Radomir Pavlovich's Haven]]. The investigators find a space behind a bookcase or cabinet where a book had been chained or locked, but the book itself is gone.

## Later connection

In [[Game 2]], [[Sabina Premysl]] says Radomir received a book/package containing rituals and runes that he used for spirit communion. This is likely connected to the missing book, but the transcript does not prove whether it is the same object.

## Related clues

- [[Spirit Communion Ritual]]
- [[Banu Haqim Desert Runes]]
- [[Gargoyle Attack at Sabina's Cottage]]

text:
# Radomir's Tremere Correspondence

## Overview

Correspondence found at [[Radomir Pavlovich's Haven]] documents Radomir's hostility toward Clan Tremere and his desire to expel or purge them from Prague.

## Extracted facts

- Radomir chronicled Tremere movement and activity in the city.
- He searched for the Tremere primary chantry.
- Replies from [[Prince Josef Zvi]] refused to facilitate attacks on Tremere domain.

## Related clues

title: Remote Tzimisce Castle
summary: Castle four hours north tied to Istvan's courier route.
text:
# Remote Tzimisce Castle

## Overview

A castle four hours north of Prague connected to [[Istvan Vlaszy]]'s courier work for [[Radomir Pavlovich]]. Istvan met a page outside town and rode there as part of a package/message route.

## Open questions

- Who controls the castle?
- What was carried there?
- Whether the route is connected to Radomir's murder remains uncertain.

## Game 4 notes

- Istvan reaches the castle during the day to deliver Radomir's package.
- Four Hussite-style war wagons with red-capped troops attack shortly afterward.
- Tzimisce at the castle tell Istvan that Tremere agents have manipulated some Hussite bands into attacking Tzimisce holdings.

text:
# Rudolph the Clockwork Alchemist

## Overview

Rudolph is a wanted alchemist and engineering-minded contact of [[Katherine Karalova]]. Guards at the [[Night Market]] seek a young alchemist they consider a heretic to God's order. Katherine later finds Rudolph wounded in a Nosferatu oubliette.

## Appears In

- [[Game 9]]
- [[Game 10]]

## Details

- He survives in darkness by drawing engineering layouts and planning escape.
- He has clockwork engineering visible under or within his ribcage.
- Katherine ghouls him and he understands that she is undead.
- He asks for help reaching a safe place against his opposition.

## Relationships

- [[Katherine Karalova]] - rescuer, patron, and domitor after she ghouls him.

## Related

- [[Night Market]]

title: Sabina as Tremere Leverage
summary: The plan to use Sabina's new Tremere status to push the weapon against Shagra.
text:
# Sabina as Tremere Leverage

## Overview

The group reasons that [[Sabina Premysl]], Embraced by the Tremere off screen, may be able to convince the Tremere to use the [[Tremere Weapon Against Shagra]].

## Logic

- Sabina is Tremere now, but her old revenant body and Tzimisce-crafted nature may still transmit Shagra's control.
- The Tremere may flee Prague unless convinced Shagra can track them anywhere.
- Sabina could frame weapon use as necessary survival rather than a favor to the Anarchs.

## Uncertain

- The transcript does not show this plan being carried out.

## Related

- [[Sabina Premysl]]
- [[Tremere Weapon Against Shagra]]
- [[Game 11]]

title: Sabina Premysl
summary: Sabina Premysl imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Revenants, Mortal). Mortal.
text:
# Sabina Premysl

![Sabina Premysl](/wiki/media/Sabina-Premysl.jpg)

## Overview

Sabina Premysl imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Revenants, Mortal). Mortal.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Mortal
- **Foundry folder:** NPC's / Reventants
- **Advantages:** Blood Pool 0/10

## Appearance

Sabina is an emaciated, waifish figure with elongated limbs and an otherworldly appearance. Her skin is ashen, stretched tightly over her bony frame, and her eyes are milky white, appearing blind. Despite her apparent frailty, she moves with an unsettling grace and wields a small, elegantly curved dagger. Her aura is one of dark knowledge and unnerving calm.

## Background

Sabina was born with a psychic connection to the slumbering Methuselah of Vysehrad. Since childhood, she has been plagued by dreams and visions, fragments of the Methuselah's ancient memories that seep into her consciousness. The Premysl elders recognized this ability and honed her prophetic talents, hoping to gain insights into the Methuselah's will. Over the years, she has acted as a seer for the family, offering cryptic warnings and guiding them through her disturbing visions.

## Roleplaying notes

Sabina believes she is meant to serve as the Methuselah's voice in Prague, interpreting its will and guiding her family in preparation for its reawakening. While she appears passive, she harbors a quiet ambition, wanting to ensure her role as the Methuselah's chosen oracle. She resents those who dismiss her visions and has a cold hatred for Christof, whose disruption brought chaos to her family. Sabina also secretly envies Kindred, longing for a more permanent power that would free her from her revenant fate.

## Attributes

| Trait | Rating |
|---|---:|
| Strength | 1 |
| Dexterity | 1 |
| Stamina | 1 |
| Charisma | 1 |
| Manipulation | 1 |
| Appearance | 1 |
| Perception | 1 |
| Intelligence | 1 |
| Wits | 1 |

## Disciplines and powers

- **Potence** (0); V20 192
  Each dot that the vampire has in Potence adds one die to all Strength-related dice rolls. Further, the player can spend one blood point and change his Potence dice into an equal number of automatic successes to all Strength-related rolls for the turn. In melee and brawling combat, successes from Potence (either rolled or automatic) are applied to the damage roll results.
- **●●●●● Potence** (0); V20 192
  Each dot that the vampire has in Potence adds one die to all Strength-related dice rolls. Further, the player can spend one blood point and change his Potence dice into an equal number of automatic successes to all Strength-related rolls for the turn. In melee and brawling combat, successes from Potence (either rolled or automatic) are applied to the damage roll results.
- **Auspex** (0); V20 134
  Auspex gives the vampire uncanny sensory abilities. She starts with the capacity to heighten her natural senses significantly, but as she grows in power, she can perceive psychic auras and read the thoughts of another being. Auspex can also pierce through mental illusions such as those created by Obfuscate -- see the sidebar "Seeing the Unseen" on p. 142 for more.
  
  However, a vampire with Auspex needs to be careful. Her increased sensory sensitivity can cause her to be drawn in by beautiful things or stunned by loud noises or pungent smells. Sudden or dynamic events can disorient an Auspex-using character unless her player makes a Willpower roll to block them out (difficulty of at least 4, although the more potent the source of distraction, the higher the difficulty). Failure overwhelms the character's senses, making her oblivious to her surroundings for a turn or two. While the Malkavians and Toreador are more prone to these kinds of distractions, the Tremere and Tzimisce aren't immune.
  
  Dots in Perception are very useful for using Auspex powers, as more successes help the character gain more sensory information.
- **○ Seeing the Unseen** (0); V20 142
  **• Obfuscate:** When a vampire tries to use her heightened perceptions to notice a Kindred hidden with Obfuscate, she detects the subject's presence if her Auspex rating is higher than his Obfuscate, and she succeeds at a Perception + Awareness roll (difficulty equals 7 minus the number of dots by which her Auspex exceeds his Obfuscate). Conversely, if the target's Obfuscate outranks her Auspex, he remains undiscovered. If the two ratings are equal, both characters make a resisted roll of Perception + Awareness (Auspex user) against Manipulation + Subterfuge (Obfuscate user). The difficulty for both rolls is 7, and the character with the most successes wins.
  
  **• Chimerstry: **Likewise, vampires with Auspex may seek to penetrate illusions created with Chimerstry. The Auspex-wielder must actively seek to pierce the illusion (i.e., the player must tell the Storyteller that his character is trying to detect an illusion). The Auspex-user and Chimerstry-wielder then compare relative ratings, per Obfuscate, above. The process is otherwise identical to piercing Obfuscate.
  
  **• Other Powers: **Since the powers of beings like magi and wraiths function differently from vampiric Disciplines, a simple comparison of relative ratings isn't applicable. To keep things simple, both characters make a resisted roll. The vampire rolls Perception + Awareness, while the subject rolls Manipulation + Subterfuge. Again, the difficulty is 7, and the character with the most successes wins.
- **● Heightened Senses** (1); V20 134
  It takes a reflexive action to activate this ability, but no roll or other cost is required. In certain circumstances, dice rolls associated with using the character's sense (such as Perception + Alertness) decrease in difficulty by a number equal to the character's Auspex rating when the power is engaged.
  
  The Storyteller may also use this power to see if the character perceives a threat. In this case, the Storyteller privately rolls the character's unmodified Auspex rating, applying whatever difficulty he feels best suits the circumstances. For example, sensing that a pistol is pointed at the back of the character's head may require a roll of difficulty 5, while the sudden realization that a rival for Primogen is planning her assassination may require a 9. Note that even this "precognition" comes only as a result of interpreting details the Kindred is able to notice. It's not an all-purpose insight or miraculous revelation.
  
  At the character's discretion, she may selectively heighten one specific sense, rather than leaving them all on. In these cases, the difficulty to perceive stimuli using that sense drops by one, but the difficulty to avoid distraction or temporary bedazzlement increases by one.
  
  This power does not let characters see in pitch darkness, as does Eyes of the Beast (p. 199), but it does reduce difficulty penalties to act in such darkness from +2 to +1, and the character may make ranged attacks in pitch darkness if she can hear, smell, or otherwise detect her foe.
- **●● Aura Perception** (2); V20 135
  After the character stares at the subject for at least a few seconds, the player rolls Perception + Empathy (difficulty 8); each success indicates how much of the subject's aura the character sees and understands (see the table below). A failure indicates that the play of colors and patterns yields no prevailing impression. A botch indicates a false or erroneous interpretation. The Storyteller may wish to make this roll, thus keeping the player in the dark as to the veracity of the character's interpretation.
  
  **Successes**
  **Result**
  
  1 Success
  Can distinguish only the shade (pale or bright).
  
  2 Successes
  Can distinguish the main color.
  
  3 Successes
  Can recognize the color patterns.
  
  4 Successes
  Can detect subtle shifts.
  
  5 Successes
  Can identify mixtures of color and pattern.
  
  The Aura Colors chart offers some example ideas of common colors and the emotions they reflect that Storytellers can use. Note that it is nearly impossible to determine with certainty if a particular character is lying or not with this power - vampires are inherently deceitful by nature, but even mortals might react with anxiety to questions while still being truthful. It is, however, helpful in determine the target's emotional state, which might lead the vampire to decide that a particular target is suspicious.
  
  A character may choose to perform a very cursory aura scan of a large area like a nightclub's dance floor or the audience in a gallery. In this case, the player decides which characteristic of auras she's looking for, and that's the only information she's able to glean if the roll is successful. (At the Storyteller's discretion, on this general scan roll, more successes on the roll may more quickly yield what the character seeks.) For example, the player may specify, "Who's the most nervous person in attendance?" or "Are there any vampirically pale auras among the CEO's entourage?" Thereafter, the player may narrow down her scrutiny of a single individual, with an additional roll as normal.
  
  The character may focus in on a particular subject's aura only once per scene with any degree of clarity. Any subsequent attempts that result in failure should be considered botches. It is very easy for the character to imagine seeing what she wants to see when judging someone's intentions. After 24 hours, the character may try again at no penalty.
  
  It is possible, though difficult, to sense the aura of a being who is otherwise invisible to normal sight. Refer to "Seeing the Unseen," p. 142, for more information.

## Traits

- **Legerdemain** (0)
- **Archery** (0)
- **Commerce** (0)
- **Ride** (0)
- **Hearth Wisdom** (0)
- **Seneschal** (0)
- **Theology** (0)

## Source

- Foundry world archive: aoe.zip
- Actor ID: QoM3WIU1ql3jHkRu
- Actor folder: NPC's / Reventants

## Session notes

- [[Game 1]] - appears in visions connected to Radomir's haven and the Premysl revenants.
- [[Game 2]] - explains that Radomir received a mysterious ritual package and used her as a vessel for spirit communion. She begins a ritual to contact the spirit witness before a gargoyle breaks the ward.

## Game 3-4 notes

- In [[Game 3]], Sabina is seized during the gargoyle attack at her cottage.
- After the gargoyle is destroyed, she is lifted through the ruined roof and carried north by an unidentified floating Cainite.
- The coterie suspects a Tremere Embrace, but the transcript does not confirm it.

Related: [[Sabina's Abduction]]

## Game 5-8 notes

- Sabina's Embrace by the Tremere is confirmed as having happened off screen.
- [[Dragan Premysl]] renews his hatred of the Tremere after learning this.

## Chronicle 1 finale notes

- The finale treats Sabina's Tremere Embrace as already complete but off screen.
- The group theorizes in [[Sabina as Tremere Leverage]] that she may still carry Tzimisce/revenant influence and could be used to pressure the Tremere into deploying the [[Tremere Weapon Against Shagra]].
playerText:
# Sabina Premysl

![Sabina Premysl](/wiki/media/Sabina-Premysl.jpg)

## Overview

Sabina Premysl imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Revenants, Mortal). Mortal.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Mortal
- **Foundry folder:** NPC's / Reventants
- **Advantages:** Blood Pool 0/10

## Appearance

Sabina is an emaciated, waifish figure with elongated limbs and an otherworldly appearance. Her skin is ashen, stretched tightly over her bony frame, and her eyes are milky white, appearing blind. Despite her apparent frailty, she moves with an unsettling grace and wields a small, elegantly curved dagger. Her aura is one of dark knowledge and unnerving calm.

## Background

Sabina was born with a psychic connection to the slumbering Methuselah of Vysehrad. Since childhood, she has been plagued by dreams and visions, fragments of the Methuselah's ancient memories that seep into her consciousness. The Premysl elders recognized this ability and honed her prophetic talents, hoping to gain insights into the Methuselah's will. Over the years, she has acted as a seer for the family, offering cryptic warnings and guiding them through her disturbing visions.

## Roleplaying notes

Sabina believes she is meant to serve as the Methuselah's voice in Prague, interpreting its will and guiding her family in preparation for its reawakening. While she appears passive, she harbors a quiet ambition, wanting to ensure her role as the Methuselah's chosen oracle. She resents those who dismiss her visions and has a cold hatred for Christof, whose disruption brought chaos to her family. Sabina also secretly envies Kindred, longing for a more permanent power that would free her from her revenant fate.

## Attributes

| Trait | Rating |
|---|---:|
| Strength | 1 |
| Dexterity | 1 |
| Stamina | 1 |
| Charisma | 1 |
| Manipulation | 1 |
| Appearance | 1 |
| Perception | 1 |
| Intelligence | 1 |
| Wits | 1 |

title: Sabina Premysl's Cottage
summary: Spirit-thin cottage and Game 2 cliffhanger location.
text:
# Sabina Premysl's Cottage

## Overview

A small one-story cottage with a thatched roof, frosted poor-quality glass, and a rope-knot knocker. The Gauntlet is extremely thin here.

## Important events

- Theodosia is overwhelmed by nature spirits.
- Eliska experiences sorrow and graveyard impressions through synesthesia.
- The coterie sees many awakened or spirit-bound household objects.
- [[Sabina Premysl]] explains Radomir's ritual research.
- A gargoyle crashes through the roof and breaks the ritual ward.

## Appears in

- [[Game 2]]

title: Sabina's Abduction
summary: Sabina is pulled through the ruined roof after the gargoyle battle and carried north by a floating Cainite.
text:
# Sabina's Abduction

## Overview

After the gargoyle is destroyed at [[Sabina Premysl's Cottage]], the ritual does not simply end. [[Sabina Premysl]] rises toward the hole in the roof, apparently drawn by a force beyond her own control.

[[Jardani Jovonovich]] follows upward and sees a floating man or Cainite embrace Sabina in midair and carry her north.

## Confirmed Details

- Sabina survives the immediate gargoyle attack.
- She is taken away through the air after the ritual erupts.
- The abductor is not identified by name in the transcript.
- The coterie suspects Tremere involvement because of the gargoyle, blood magic, and a later Tremere request for retroactive permission for an Embrace. The Embrace is later confirmed as having happened off screen.

## Uncertain

- The identity of the floating abductor is unknown.

## Appears In

- [[Game 3]]

title: Salubri Chantry Raid
summary: Dragan smuggles Salubri warriors into Prague to attack the Tremere Chantry.
text:
# Salubri Chantry Raid

## Overview

[[Dragan Premysl]] reveals in [[Game 7]] that he helped smuggle six Salubri warriors into Prague to strike the Tremere. The attack damages Tremere retainers but goes badly.

## Confirmed Details

- One Salubri appears to have been compelled or controlled into daylight and died.
- Dragan is wounded by electricity and by a wooden heart-seeking creature.
- The Tremere later approach the coterie because Dragan was associated with them during Radomir's investigation.

## Appears In

- [[Game 7]]
- [[Game 8]]

title: Salubri War Names
summary: The Salubri practice of concealing names during the War of Names.
text:
# Salubri War Names

## Overview

The hidden Salubri refuse to give names freely, explaining that their people hide names as sacred and strategic things during a "War of Names."

## Details

- The speaker's third eye glows during the meeting.
- The Salubri reject insulting or propagandized language used by Tremere.
- They agree to help against [[Adrastos von Orlok]] because he hunts and enslaves Salubri.
- They frame aid as requiring payment: life for life.

## Related

- [[Hidden Salubri Cave]]
- [[Orlok Assassination]]
- [[Game 9]]

title: Sebík Pražský
summary: Sebík Pražský imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Elders, Vampire). Vampire.
text:
# Sebík Pražský

![Sebík Pražský](/wiki/media/Sebik-Prazsky.jpg)

## Overview

Sebík Pražský imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Elders, Vampire). Vampire.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Vampire
- **Foundry folder:** NPC's / Elders
- **Foundry name note:** Nos Prim
- **Original Foundry name:** Sebík Pražský (Nos Prim)
- **Advantages:** Willpower 4; Blood Pool 0/15

## Background

Sebík Pražský, originally named Sadb, was born in Caledonia in 185 AD, part of a Celtic tribe deeply rooted in mysticism and nature worship. Before his embrace, he was known among his people as a faith healer, someone who communed with the spirits and offered solace to the sick and wounded. His life, however, took a drastic turn during a nighttime raid on a Roman legion encampment. It was during this raid that Sadb encountered a powerful Nosferatu elder, a Kindred who had been traveling with the Roman legions in search of ancient lore lost to their kind. The elder, impressed by Sadb's fearlessness and mystical knowledge, saw potential in him and decided to embrace him, transforming him into one of the undead. With the embrace, Sadb became Sebík, shedding his mortal name and beginning his journey into the dark world of eternal life. After his embrace, Sebík traveled back to Rome with his sire, who belonged to a sect of Nosferatu scholars devoted to recovering lost knowledge. In Rome, he was commissioned by the local Nosferatu clan to embark on a perilous mission: to gather forgotten lore and lost secrets from the farthest reaches of the world. This mission took Sebík across continents, as he ventured through Persia, India, and eventually to the great city of Yanjing in China. There, he encountered the enigmatic Wan Kuei, the Eastern vampires, and managed to steal ancient scrolls containing powerful Yin magics. His journeys broadened his knowledge and understanding of the supernatural world, and he deposited many of these secrets with his childer in Prague, creating an early foundation for his hidden library. Yet, it wasn't until centuries later that the true cost of this knowledge would reveal itself. Returning to Prague in 1346, Sebík was part of a caravan that brought the Black Plague to Europe. To his horror, he realized that the plague was not a mere mortal disease, but the result of a terrible curse tied to the scrolls of Yin magic he had taken from the Wan Kuei. The Yin energies had tainted the caravan's members and spread through the city like wildfire, leaving death and destruction in its wake. Realizing the dangerous nature of the knowledge he had uncovered, Sebík became paranoid about the potential for this cursed knowledge to bring further calamities. It was then that he and his childer created the Giftschrank, a hidden repository beneath the University of Prague, where he would store all forbidden texts and artifacts that held the potential to unleash great harm on the world. Only Sebík and his most trusted brood were permitted to access this vault, safeguarding humanity from the darker aspects of his discoveries. As the centuries passed, Sebík adapted to life in Prague, and his influence grew in the shadows. His greatest accomplishment was his role in the creation of the University of Prague in 1348, one of the first universities in Central Europe. Sebík had a direct hand in guiding the development of its curriculum, ensuring that the institution became a hub for intellectuals, scholars, and philosophers. By embedding himself in the academic elite, Sebík could easily gather new knowledge while maintaining his secrecy. His role as "The Librarian" grew out of this era, as both mortals and Kindred who sought ancient wisdom often found themselves indebted to him for his unparalleled knowledge of history, theology, and the occult. In time, Sebík's scholarly interests led him to discover Klára Karálová, a noblewoman with a thirst for knowledge who had disguised herself as a man to attend classes at the university. Sebík admired her boldness and her disregard for societal norms, which mirrored his own insatiable curiosity for forbidden knowledge. When Klára was discovered and relegated to the role of a maid, Sebík continued to watch her nocturnal studies from the shadows. Impressed by her determination, Sebík embraced her, believing that her intellect and ambition made her a worthy addition to his growing network of scholars. Together, they expanded the Giftschrank, turning it into a secretive library filled with arcane texts, artifacts of dark power, and knowledge that could tip the balance of power in the hands of those who understood it. Despite his vast collection of knowledge and his role as an elder Nosferatu, Sebík's existence has been marked by a deep sense of paranoia. His experiences with cursed knowledge, especially the Black Plague and the Yin magic scrolls, have left him wary of what lies beyond the surface of his understanding. His brood, though loyal, have witnessed his increasing isolation and obsessive behavior regarding the Giftschrank's contents. Sebík now rarely ventures beyond his subterranean sanctum beneath Prague, where he spends his nights studying the ancient texts, always searching for some hidden truth that has eluded him for centuries. His paranoia about the potential dangers of knowledge only deepens, driving him to lock away ever more dangerous secrets. By 1433, Prague had become a city of turmoil, with the rise of the Hussite movement and the schism between Catholics and reformists shaking the city's foundations. Sebík has watched these developments from the shadows, recognizing that the city is teetering on the edge of chaos. While he cares little for the religious upheaval on a mortal level, he is acutely aware that such instability could lead to a power vacuum among the Kindred as well. More than ever, Sebík believes that the knowledge he and his brood have hidden away is the key to surviving the storms ahead. With Klára and a select few of his childer by his side, Sebík continues to operate from the darkness, content to let others fight for power while he guards the secrets that could shape the future of both mortals and Kindred alike.Sebík Pražský, originally named Sadb, was born in Caledonia in 185 AD, part of a Celtic tribe deeply rooted in mysticism and nature worship. Before his embrace, he was known among his people as a faith healer, someone who communed with the spirits and offered solace to the sick and wounded. His life, however, took a drastic turn during a nighttime raid on a Roman legion encampment. It was during this raid that Sadb encountered a powerful Nosferatu elder, a Kindred who had been traveling with the Roman legions in search of ancient lore lost to their kind. The elder, impressed by Sadb's fearlessness and mystical knowledge, saw potential in him and decided to embrace him, transforming him into one of the undead. With the embrace, Sadb became Sebík, shedding his mortal name and beginning his journey into the dark world of eternal life. After his embrace, Sebík traveled back to Rome with his sire, who belonged to a sect of Nosferatu scholars devoted to recovering lost knowledge. In Rome, he was commissioned by the local Nosferatu clan to embark on a perilous mission: to gather forgotten lore and lost secrets from the farthest reaches of the world. This mission took Sebík across continents, as he ventured through Persia, India, and eventually to the great city of Yanjing in China. There, he encountered the enigmatic Wan Kuei, the Eastern vampires, and managed to steal ancient scrolls containing powerful Yin magics. His journeys broadened his knowledge and understanding of the supernatural world, and he deposited many of these secrets with his childer in Prague, creating an early foundation for his hidden library. Yet, it wasn't until centuries later that the true cost of this knowledge would reveal itself. Returning to Prague in 1346, Sebík was part of a caravan that brought the Black Plague to Europe. To his horror, he realized that the plague was not a mere mortal disease, but the result of a terrible curse tied to the scrolls of Yin magic he had taken from the Wan Kuei. The Yin energies had tainted the caravan's members and spread through the city like wildfire, leaving death and destruction in its wake. Realizing the dangerous nature of the knowledge he had uncovered, Sebík became paranoid about the potential for this cursed knowledge to bring further calamities. It was then that he and his childer created the Giftschrank, a hidden repository beneath the University of Prague, where he would store all forbidden texts and artifacts that held the potential to unleash great harm on the world. Only Sebík and his most trusted brood were permitted to access this vault, safeguarding humanity from the darker aspects of his discoveries. As the centuries passed, Sebík adapted to life in Prague, and his influence grew in the shadows. His greatest accomplishment was his role in the creation of the University of Prague in 1348, one of the first universities in Central Europe. Sebík had a direct hand in guiding the development of its curriculum, ensuring that the institution became a hub for intellectuals, scholars, and philosophers. By embedding himself in the academic elite, Sebík could easily gather new knowledge while maintaining his secrecy. His role as "The Librarian" grew out of this era, as both mortals and Kindred who sought ancient wisdom often found themselves indebted to him for his unparalleled knowledge of history, theology, and the occult. In time, Sebík's scholarly interests led him to discover Klára Karálová, a noblewoman with a thirst for knowledge who had disguised herself as a man to attend classes at the university. Sebík admired her boldness and her disregard for societal norms, which mirrored his own insatiable curiosity for forbidden knowledge. When Klára was discovered and relegated to the role of a maid, Sebík continued to watch her nocturnal studies from the shadows. Impressed by her determination, Sebík embraced her, believing that her intellect and ambition made her a worthy addition to his growing network of scholars. Together, they expanded the Giftschrank, turning it into a secretive library filled with arcane texts, artifacts of dark power, and knowledge that could tip the balance of power in the hands of those who understood it. Despite his vast collection of knowledge and his role as an elder Nosferatu, Sebík's existence has been marked by a deep sense of paranoia. His experiences with cursed knowledge, especially the Black Plague and the Yin magic scrolls, have left him wary of what lies beyond the surface of his understanding. His brood, though loyal, have witnessed his increasing isolation and obsessive behavior regarding the Giftschrank's contents. Sebík now rarely ventures beyond his subterranean sanctum beneath Prague, where he spends his nights studying the ancient texts, always searching for some hidden truth that has eluded him for centuries. His paranoia about the potential dangers of knowledge only deepens, driving him to lock away ever more dangerous secrets. By 1433, Prague had become a city of turmoil, with the rise of the Hussite movement and the schism between Catholics and reformists shaking the city's foundations. Sebík has watched these developments from the shadows, recognizing that the city is teetering on the edge of chaos. While he cares little for the religious upheaval on a mortal level, he is acutely aware that such instability could lead to a power vacuum among the Kindred as well. More than ever, Sebík believes that the knowledge he and his brood have hidden away is the key to surviving the storms ahead. With Klára and a select few of his childer by his side, Sebík continues to operate from the darkness, content to let others fight for power while he guards the secrets that could shape the future of both mortals and Kindred alike.

## Attributes

| Trait | Rating |
|---|---:|
| Strength | 2 |
| Dexterity | 1 |
| Stamina | 3 |
| Charisma | 2 |
| Manipulation | 5 |
| Appearance | 1 |
| Perception | 2 |
| Intelligence | 5 |
| Wits | 3 |

## Abilities

**Talents:** Alertness 2, Awareness 2, Empathy 1, Subterfuge 2

**Skills:** Animal Ken 1, Etiquette 2, Stealth 3, Survival 2

**Knowledges:** Academics 3, Enigmas 2, Investigation 3, Occult 3

## Disciplines and powers

- **Background: Status 2** (0)
- **Background: Generation 3** (0)
- **Background: Domain 2** (0)
- **Animalism** (0)
- **Potence** (0)
- **Obfuscate** (0)
- **The Silence of Death** (1)
  There is no roll. The power creates a zone of absolute silence around the character, with a maximum radius of 7 meters. No sound occurs inside this zone, though sounds originating outside the area of effect maybe heard by anyone in it. Additionally, if the player spends two blood points and the character spends five turns inconcentration in a room or enclosure, the silence remainsin it after the character leaves the room and lasts until she wills it to end. A character can't create more than one zone of silence, so if she wishes to use Silence of Death on herself (or curse another room), the zone in the first room fades automatically.
- **Unseen Presence** (2)
  There is no roll necessary to conjure an Unseen Presence. Should a Cainite draw attention to herself by sneak attack, blunder, or speech, accidental attention will call for a Wits + Stealth roll, difficulty from 5 to 9, based on the amount of noise or calamity the Cainite has caused. Willful attempts to violate the secrecy of Unseen Presence while still maintaining it may call for a high difficulty and multiple successes. For example, whispering to an enemy without giving away her location would require three or so successes at a difficulty of 7. Even when seen or unseen, a secondary use of this power allows a Cainite to conceal small items already on her person, or with a successful Manipulation + Subterfuge from 5 to 9, based on the amount of observation she can grasp and conceal an easily moved item.
- **Mask of a Thousand Faces** (3)
  To create this guise, the player rolls Manipulation + Performance at a difficulty 7. To mimic the face of another, the vampire must have a good look at them. The Storyteller might raise the difficulty on the roll if the vampire has only briefly seen the person they wish to mimic. How successful the disguise is depends on the chart at the top of the page. Should a vampire wish to create a face much more appealing than her own, she must spend a point of blood for every dot of difference from her Appearance to that of the disguise. While fooling acquaintances may require only a look at the target and clever crafting of the mask, fooling someone intimately connected to the target is another matter. That requires actual familiarity with the target, and perhaps other rolls to keep the illusion going.
  
  1 success  The vampire's mass remains the same. She cannot be taller or shorter or much bulkier, but she may change enough features so as to be hard to recognize (though she might be mistaken for a blood relative). A Nosferatu, for example, can appear as a perfectly believable, albeit tragically hideous human.
  
  2 successes  Her appearance is now a complete mask, and she resembles herself in no way, should she wish. She could not be mistaken for her own blood relative even in strong lantern light. She can now change how others perceive her gender.
  
  3 successes  Her intention is made complete, and she wears the mask she intends to wear. Her face is her own canvas.
  
  4 successes  The illusion is now so complete that the victims will imagine the vampire to sound exactly as the mask suggests. If it is a specific person, the illusion forces the victims to imagine the vampire is performing the correctt witches and physical mannerisms of the person they are mimicking.
  
  5 successes  The transformation is complete. Even age and size no longer hold the vampire back. She can make her victims  see her as anyone she wants, just by willing it so.
- **Vanish From The Mind’s Eye** (4)
  The player rolls her character's Intelligence + Stealth against their victim's Wits + Alertness. If there is more than one victim, use the highest pool to resist. If the player rolls one to three successes, the vampire fades but does not vanish, appearing ghost-like and translucent. With more than three successes, the vampire vanishes from her victim's senses entirely. If the player achieves more successes than the victim, the victim forgets that the vampire was there to begin with, and tends to not reflecton any conflict in memory that might cause.
  
  It is possible to interact with a ghosted vampire, though difficult. A player tracking such a vampire rolls Perception + Alertness at a difficulty of 8. On a successful roll, the character can interact normally with the ghostlike vampire, though they are likely to be disturbed by hisethereal presence. On a failure, the character loses two dice on any roll to interact with the ghostlike vampire, from attacking them to following them to seducing them.
  
  After witnessing a vampire vanish before their eyes, the player of any other character present must roll Wits + Courage. For humans, this is difficulty 9; for Cainites, difficulty 5. If the player succeeds, the character may act as expected in the situation. A failure on this roll means that the character is beside themselves and unable to act for two turns, unless that action is to flee.
- **Cloak The Gathering** (5)
  For every dot of Stealth the vampire has, she may add one additional person to her cloak. The cloak extends any single Obfuscate power the vampire already has to any character in the cloak. Large objects such as horses or houses may be added at the "cost" of an additional dot of Stealth. All members of the cloak must behave under the limitations of the power extended to them, and will drop out of the cloak should they breach the power's requirement. If the vampire creating the cloak fails to uphold the power's requirements, the whole cloak falls
- **Mask The Devil** (6)
  The vampire wills herself to become nothing, and her soul responds. Furthermore, because the Beast appears to be gone, any and all abilities, vampiric or otherwise, that might target the Beast such as the Animalism power Song of Serenity(see p. 192) fail, regardless of rolls.
- **Merit: Medium 2** (0)
  Ghosts, spirits, and shades speak to you. You may have had this talent in your mortal life,or it may be a new ability granted by your Embrace. By spending a moment in meditation, you may ask the spirits who visit you for their aid. They are ready and willing to help you in whatever way they can (most often by providing information), but they always ask a favor in return.The severity of this favor should be in line with the task given to the spirits
- **Merit: Lingua Franca 1** (0)
  You are able to speak a mixed language made of common tongues that most people you encounter will understand. You may not be able to hold philosophical discussions, but you can navigate your way through conversations in most places and will be able to communicate sufficiently in dire situations.
- **Merit: Literacy 1** (0)
  You are a learned scholar, able to both read and write. The Merit assumes that you can read and write with fluency in your native tongue, as well as Latin and any languages you may have gained through the Multi-Lingual Merit. In some chronicles, literacy can be assumed. In these cases, Illiteracy becomes a 1-point Flaw.
- **Merit: Multi-Lingual 2** (0)
  Through patience, study, and/or immersion, you are able to speak another language or languages with native fluency. This represents a full command over the language. For each dot of Intelligence your character possesses, you may know one other language. You may also use this Merit to reflect secret codes, cants, jargon, or sign language as well. Additional languages increase the cost of this Merit by one point per language.
- **Fortress of Silence** (7)
  Spend one blood point. Upon activation, this power inverts the effects of Silence of Death, simultaneously cutting a character off from external distractions while inwardly quieting peripheral subconscious background noise and chatter. The difficulty of all Perception-based rolls for characters meditating through Fortress of Silence is increased by two. This focused control over his own thoughts, however, allows him to reinvigorate his will, rejuvenating one lost Willpower point every 15 minutes.After a character ceases meditating and emerges from Fortress of Silence, the difficulty of all rolls involving Mental Attributes and Willpower is decreased by one for the remainder of the evening.
  
  To target an opponent with this power, the player spends one blood point and rolls Stamina + Occult (difficulty equal to the opponent's Willpower). The target acquires a Derangement if the character achieves more successes on the roll than the target has permanent Willpower. With 3 or more successes, this Derangement is permanent; otherwise it lasts for the duration of the night. For the duration of the scene, the amount of successes equal the number of Dexterity and Mental Attribute dice the target is prevented from accessing for any rolls. A target may spend Willpower on a one-for-one basis to regain dice lost through use of this power.

## Traits

- **Legerdemain** (0)
- **Archery** (0)
- **Commerce** (0)
- **Ride** (0)
- **Hearth Wisdom** (0)
- **Seneschal** (0)
- **Theology** (0)

## Source

- Foundry world archive: aoe.zip
- Actor ID: GSJUnJLFSN0WsCfV
- Actor folder: NPC's / Elders
