# Sebík Pražský

![Sebík Pražský](/wiki/media/Sebik-Prazsky.jpg)

## Overview

Sebík Pražský imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Elders, Vampire). Vampire.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Vampire
- **Foundry folder:** NPC's / Elders
- **Foundry name note:** Nos Prim
- **Original Foundry name:** Sebík Pražský (Nos Prim)
- **Advantages:** Willpower 4; Blood Pool 0/15

## Background

Sebík Pražský, originally named Sadb, was born in Caledonia in 185 AD, part of a Celtic tribe deeply rooted in mysticism and nature worship. Before his embrace, he was known among his people as a faith healer, someone who communed with the spirits and offered solace to the sick and wounded. His life, however, took a drastic turn during a nighttime raid on a Roman legion encampment. It was during this raid that Sadb encountered a powerful Nosferatu elder, a Kindred who had been traveling with the Roman legions in search of ancient lore lost to their kind. The elder, impressed by Sadb's fearlessness and mystical knowledge, saw potential in him and decided to embrace him, transforming him into one of the undead. With the embrace, Sadb became Sebík, shedding his mortal name and beginning his journey into the dark world of eternal life. After his embrace, Sebík traveled back to Rome with his sire, who belonged to a sect of Nosferatu scholars devoted to recovering lost knowledge. In Rome, he was commissioned by the local Nosferatu clan to embark on a perilous mission: to gather forgotten lore and lost secrets from the farthest reaches of the world. This mission took Sebík across continents, as he ventured through Persia, India, and eventually to the great city of Yanjing in China. There, he encountered the enigmatic Wan Kuei, the Eastern vampires, and managed to steal ancient scrolls containing powerful Yin magics. His journeys broadened his knowledge and understanding of the supernatural world, and he deposited many of these secrets with his childer in Prague, creating an early foundation for his hidden library. Yet, it wasn't until centuries later that the true cost of this knowledge would reveal itself. Returning to Prague in 1346, Sebík was part of a caravan that brought the Black Plague to Europe. To his horror, he realized that the plague was not a mere mortal disease, but the result of a terrible curse tied to the scrolls of Yin magic he had taken from the Wan Kuei. The Yin energies had tainted the caravan's members and spread through the city like wildfire, leaving death and destruction in its wake. Realizing the dangerous nature of the knowledge he had uncovered, Sebík became paranoid about the potential for this cursed knowledge to bring further calamities. It was then that he and his childer created the Giftschrank, a hidden repository beneath the University of Prague, where he would store all forbidden texts and artifacts that held the potential to unleash great harm on the world. Only Sebík and his most trusted brood were permitted to access this vault, safeguarding humanity from the darker aspects of his discoveries. As the centuries passed, Sebík adapted to life in Prague, and his influence grew in the shadows. His greatest accomplishment was his role in the creation of the University of Prague in 1348, one of the first universities in Central Europe. Sebík had a direct hand in guiding the development of its curriculum, ensuring that the institution became a hub for intellectuals, scholars, and philosophers. By embedding himself in the academic elite, Sebík could easily gather new knowledge while maintaining his secrecy. His role as "The Librarian" grew out of this era, as both mortals and Kindred who sought ancient wisdom often found themselves indebted to him for his unparalleled knowledge of history, theology, and the occult. In time, Sebík's scholarly interests led him to discover Klára Karálová, a noblewoman with a thirst for knowledge who had disguised herself as a man to attend classes at the university. His experiences with cursed knowledge, especially the Black Plague and the Yin magic scrolls, have left him wary of what lies beyond the surface of his understanding. His brood, though loyal, have witnessed his increasing isolation and obsessive behavior regarding the Giftschrank's contents. Sebík now rarely ventures beyond his subterranean sanctum beneath Prague, where he spends his nights studying the ancient texts, always searching for some hidden truth that has eluded him for centuries. His paranoia about the potential dangers of knowledge only deepens, driving him to lock away ever more dangerous secrets. By 1433, Prague had become a city of turmoil, with the rise of the Hussite movement and the schism between Catholics and reformists shaking the city's foundations. Sebík has watched these developments from the shadows, recognizing that the city is teetering on the edge of chaos. While he cares little for the religious upheaval on a mortal level, he is acutely aware that such instability could lead to a power vacuum among the Kindred as well. More than ever, Sebík believes that the knowledge he and his brood have hidden away is the key to surviving the storms ahead. Large objects such as horses or houses may be added at the "cost" of an additional dot of Stealth. All members of the cloak must behave under the limitations of the power extended to them, and will drop out of the cloak should they breach the power's requirement. If the vampire creating the cloak fails to uphold the power's requirements, the whole cloak falls
- **Mask The Devil** (6)
  The vampire wills herself to become nothing, and her soul responds. Furthermore, because the Beast appears to be gone, any and all abilities, vampiric or otherwise, that might target the Beast such as the Animalism power Song of Serenity(see p. 192) fail, regardless of rolls.
- **Merit: Medium 2** (0)
  Ghosts, spirits, and shades speak to you. You may have had this talent in your mortal life,or it may be a new ability granted by your Embrace. By spending a moment in meditation, you may ask the spirits who visit you for their aid. They are ready and willing to help you in whatever way they can (most often by providing information), but they always ask a favor in return.The severity of this favor should be in line with the task given to the spirits
- **Merit: Lingua Franca 1** (0)
  You are able to speak a mixed language made of common tongues that most people you encounter will understand. You may not be able to hold philosophical discussions, but you can navigate your way through conversations in most places and will be able to communicate sufficiently in dire situations.
- **Merit: Literacy 1** (0)
  You are a learned scholar, able to both read and write. The Merit assumes that you can read and write with fluency in your native tongue, as well as Latin and any languages you may have gained through the Multi-Lingual Merit. In some chronicles, literacy can be assumed. In these cases, Illiteracy becomes a 1-point Flaw.
- **Merit: Multi-Lingual 2** (0)
  Through patience, study, and/or immersion, you are able to speak another language or languages with native fluency. This represents a full command over the language. For each dot of Intelligence your character possesses, you may know one other language. You may also use this Merit to reflect secret codes, cants, jargon, or sign language as well. Additional languages increase the cost of this Merit by one point per language.
- **Fortress of Silence** (7)
  Spend one blood point. Upon activation, this power inverts the effects of Silence of Death, simultaneously cutting a character off from external distractions while inwardly quieting peripheral subconscious background noise and chatter. The difficulty of all Perception-based rolls for characters meditating through Fortress of Silence is increased by two. This focused control over his own thoughts, however, allows him to reinvigorate his will, rejuvenating one lost Willpower point every 15 minutes.After a character ceases meditating and emerges from Fortress of Silence, the difficulty of all rolls involving Mental Attributes and Willpower is decreased by one for the remainder of the evening.
  
  To target an opponent with this power, the player spends one blood point and rolls Stamina + Occult (difficulty equal to the opponent's Willpower). The target acquires a Derangement if the character achieves more successes on the roll than the target has permanent Willpower. With 3 or more successes, this Derangement is permanent; otherwise it lasts for the duration of the night. For the duration of the scene, the amount of successes equal the number of Dexterity and Mental Attribute dice the target is prevented from accessing for any rolls. A target may spend Willpower on a one-for-one basis to regain dice lost through use of this power.

## Traits

- **Legerdemain** (0)
- **Archery** (0)
- **Commerce** (0)
- **Ride** (0)
- **Hearth Wisdom** (0)
- **Seneschal** (0)
- **Theology** (0)

## Source

- Foundry world archive: aoe.zip
- Actor ID: GSJUnJLFSN0WsCfV
- Actor folder: NPC's / Elders

title: Setite Ship North
summary: The vessel used by the northern Setites to flee Prague and travel toward Lithuania.
text:
# Setite Ship North

## Overview

The northern Setites have a ship crewed by those who must serve them. They offer it as passage north after the [[Night of Chaos]].

## Route

- Prague escape route
- Lithuania and Teutonic lands
- Later northern domains and Scandinavia

## Important Events

- The coterie chooses the ship instead of returning deeply into Prague.
- In [[Game 14]], the boat must be portaged after [[Greg the Tremere]] leaves with the horses.

## Related

- [[Norse Setite Anteaters]]
- [[Land-Spirit Crossing]]

title: Shadow Creature at Sabina's Cottage
summary: A shadow entity answers questions after the failed or co-opted ritual at Sabina's cottage.
text:
# Shadow Creature at Sabina's Cottage

## Overview

After [[Father Zemislav]]'s abyssal power and [[Sabina Premysl]]'s ritual interact, a shadow-like creature forms in the cottage. It answers several questions before the scene ends.

## Answers Given

- Radomir was killed by "darkness."
- The killer is not in the room.
- The killer is connected to "the ones who kill."
- The speaker frames the connection as analogous to enslavement: as the coterie is bound to those seeking the killer, the killer is connected to those who kill.

## Interpretations

- [[Istvan Vlaszy]] takes the answer as pointing toward crusaders or crusader-linked agents.
- Later events at the [[Red Calf]] connect the crossbow shooter to liberation rhetoric rather than a straightforward crusader command structure.

## Appears In

- [[Game 3]]

title: Shagra's Bloodline
summary: The Tzimisce Methuselah influence tied to Radomir, Istvan, Theodosia's crisis, and Prague itself.
text:
# Shagra's Bloodline

## Overview

Shagra is the name used for the Tzimisce Methuselah influence tied to [[Radomir Pavlovich]], [[Istvan Vlaszy]], and later [[Theodosia Byzantios]]. Theodosia perceives the influence as spread through Prague itself, present in blood, skin, mist, plants, and people.

## Confirmed Details

- Radomir's stored blood carries a tangible Methuselah presence.
- Theodosia experiences the influence as invasive and nearly soul-consuming.
- Istvan believes Prince Josef's arm ritual exposed his true ancient connection.
- The group theorizes that Radomir may have been trying to help the Prince resist or understand this influence.

## Related

- [[Theodosia's Tzimisce Infection]]
- [[Prince Josef's Arm Ritual]]
- [[Prince Joseph and Antediluvian Influence]]

## Chronicle 1 finale notes

- The Anarch leaders describe a Shagra-linked ancient presence feeding on or controlling land magic.
- [[Micah Vykos]] says the Tremere have a [[Tremere Weapon Against Shagra]] that can strike the ancient's psyche.
- [[Land-Spirit Crossing]] later clarifies that Tzimisce-style land control may be a corrupted or enslaving version of older land-spirit communion.

title: Spirit Communion Ritual
summary: The ritual that occupied Radomir and Sabina.
text:
# Spirit Communion Ritual

## Confirmed facts

- [[Sabina Premysl]] says [[Radomir Pavlovich]] received a package containing arcane rituals and runes.
- The rituals excited Radomir and warmed his blood.
- Radomir used Sabina as a vessel, filling her with spirits so he could interrogate them.
- Sabina describes the spirits as older than Christianity.
- She believes the ritual occupied Radomir and left his defenses lowered.
- The ritual circle Sabina later draws resembles the markings found at Radomir's haven.
- The ritual is interrupted by a gargoyle attack before the spirit witness can be questioned.

## Risks

- Spirit activity is explicitly dangerous.
- Istvan says a spirit's behavior is shaped by observation and awareness; more eyes may help, but antagonizing spirits remains dangerous.

## Game 3 notes

- The recreated ritual at Sabina's cottage is interrupted by the gargoyle and then overtaken by darker forces.
- The resulting shadow creature answers questions about Radomir's killer, but in cryptic terms.

title: Spirit-Protective Bedding Runes
summary: Runes on bedding used to resist possession.
text:
# Spirit-Protective Bedding Runes

## Overview

Bedding at [[Radomir Pavlovich's Haven]] carries runes distinct from the blood/Arabic markings elsewhere. Research indicates the sheets are meant to stop spiritual possession, especially during sleep or unconsciousness.

## Related clues

- [[Spirit Communion Ritual]]
- [[Banu Haqim Desert Runes]]

title: St. Petersburg Sabbat Work
summary: The pack's time converting Brujah and manipulating Russian nobility.
text:
# St. Petersburg Sabbat Work

## Overview

In the fast epilogue of [[Game 14]], the pack reaches the court of St. Petersburg during the height of Brujah influence.

## Details

- Brujah there view absolute nobility and serfdom as a reflection of Cain's command over mortals.
- Blood flows freely because mortal lives are treated cheaply.
- The pack converts large groups of Brujah to its cause.
- They encourage or manipulate ghouling among Russian nobles, leading toward violent wars and attacks on neighbors.

## Reward / Growth

- The group receives a free out-of-clan discipline opportunity from this period.

## Related

- [[Chronicle 1 Epilogue]]

title: Stake the Librarian Offer
summary: A stranger asks Katherine to preserve her sire rather than destroy him.
text:
# Stake the Librarian Offer

## Overview

After [[Katherine Karalova]] hides her books, a stranger approaches and asks her to stake [[The Librarian]] instead of killing him.

## Details

- The request is framed as preserving knowledge for later.
- The stranger's identity and allegiance are not confirmed in the transcript.
- The offer matters because Katherine has just acted against her sire's library and may be outside his trust.

## Related

- [[Katherine's Hidden Trunk]]
- [[The Librarian]]
- [[Game 10]]

title: Stockholm
summary: The city where the pack lands at the end of Chronicle 1 and where Chronicle 2 begins.
text:
# Stockholm

## Overview

Stockholm is the endpoint of Chronicle 1. After roughly a century of travel, survival, and Sabbat formation, the pack lands there and begins inserting itself into the local court.

## Important Events

- The pack arrives after the [[Unconquered Banu Haqim]] incident at sea.
- Stockholm becomes the launch point for Chronicle 2.
- The characters receive a fifty-experience-point time jump and are now solidly ancillae.

## Related

- [[Chronicle 1 Epilogue]]
- [[Pack Roles After Prague]]

title: Svigar Derkinson
summary: Leader or speaker of the northern Setite Anteater band.
text:
# Svigar Derkinson

## Overview

Svigar Derkinson is the tall, thin speaker of the [[Norse Setite Anteaters]] at the [[Fireflower Anarch Camp]].

## Appears In

- [[Game 13]]

## Details

- His aura is interwoven with spirits and sorceries that guard him.
- He speaks of the Fireflower as stolen Promethean flame and a weapon against elder control.
- He describes northern darkness, sleeping dragons, and a tomb that may be Cain's.
- He invites the coterie north on the [[Setite Ship North]].

## Relationships

- [[Ursa]], [[Colleen Norgobane]], and [[Thor Skydre]] - companions.

## Uncertain

- The transcript initially sounds like "Sphereguard"; "Svigar Derkinson" is the cleaned-up page title used here.

title: The Fireflower
summary: Reader-facing hub for the Fireflower, proto-Vauldere, and the pack's founding rite.
text:
# The Fireflower

## What It Is

The Fireflower is the revolutionary rite that breaks elder blood bonds and replaces them with a new mutual bond among participants. It begins as rumor and promise in [[Graziano's Revolution]] and becomes the pack's lived foundation in the [[First Fireflower Valderie]].

## Key Pages

- [[Fireflower and Proto-Vauldere]]
- [[First Fireflower Valderie]]
- [[Silver Fireflower Chalice]]
- [[Blood Bond and the Crown]]
- [[Graziano's Revolution]]
- [[Pack Roles After Prague]]

## Why It Matters

- It gives younger vampires a way to act against elders.
- It turns political revolt into a spiritual and blood-bound community.
- It helps [[Istvan Vlaszy]] survive the later Banu Haqim curse as one of the [[Unconquered Banu Haqim]].
- It marks the coterie's transformation into a Sabbat pack.
playerText:
# The Fireflower

## What It Is

The Fireflower is the revolutionary rite that breaks elder blood bonds and replaces them with a new mutual bond among participants. It begins as rumor and promise in [[Graziano's Revolution]] and becomes the pack's lived foundation in the [[First Fireflower Valderie]].

## Key Pages

- [[Fireflower and Proto-Vauldere]]
- [[First Fireflower Valderie]]
- [[Silver Fireflower Chalice]]
- [[Blood Bond and the Crown]]
- [[Graziano's Revolution]]
- [[Pack Roles After Prague]]

## Why It Matters

- It gives younger vampires a way to act against elders.
- It turns political revolt into a spiritual and blood-bound community.
- It helps [[Istvan Vlaszy]] survive the later Banu Haqim curse as one of the [[Unconquered Banu Haqim]].
- It marks the coterie's transformation into a Sabbat pack.

title: The Librarian
summary: A Nosferatu elder or information broker tied to Katherine and Prague's hidden archives.
text:
# The Librarian

## Overview

The Librarian is a Nosferatu information broker or elder associated with Prague's hidden archives. The transcript connects him to [[Katherine Karalova]] as her sire or patron, though exact wording should be treated cautiously.

## Appears In

- [[Game 3]] - approached through Elysium circles after Sabina's abduction.
- [[Game 4]] - meets the coterie in the library and explains Prince Josef's history.

## Relationships

- [[Katherine Karalova]] - sire or close Nosferatu patron, based on table phrasing.
- [[Prince Josef Zvi]] - knows important history and can arrange a meeting.

## Associated Locations

- [[The Librarian's Library]]
- [[Castle Catacombs]]

## Notes

- He says the Prince may listen if shown that his agents are escalating Prague toward war.
- He accepts the [[Book of Koldun Exorcery]] as payment for arranging the audience.

# The Librarian's Library

## Overview

The Librarian's Library is a public-facing research space where the coterie studies dangerous or obscure topics.

## Important Events

- [[Istvan Vlaszy]] researches [[Necromancer Countermeasures]].
- [[The Librarian]] explains [[Prince Joseph and Antediluvian Influence]] and arranges the [[Castle Catacombs Audience]].

## Appears In

text:
# The Ones Who Kill

## Overview

The shadow creature at [[Sabina Premysl's Cottage]] says Radomir's killer is connected to "the ones who kill." The phrase becomes a clue rather than a solved identity.

## Uses In Play

- [[Istvan Vlaszy]] links it to the crusader-like crossbow bolt.
- The trail leads to Jacob at the [[Red Calf]].
- The phrase may point to Assamite/Banu Haqim assassins, crusader-linked agents, or another network; the transcript does not settle this.

## Appears In

title: Theodosia Byzantios
summary: Theodosia Byzantios imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Player Characters, Vampire). Vampire | Nature: Fanatic | Demeanor: Penitent.
text:
# Theodosia Byzantios

![Theodosia Byzantios](/wiki/media/Theodosia-Byzantios.png)

## Overview

Theodosia Byzantios imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Player Characters, Vampire). Vampire | Nature: Fanatic | Demeanor: Penitent.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Vampire
- **Foundry folder:** Players
- **Foundry name note:** Jenn
- **Original Foundry name:** Theodosia Byzantios (Jenn)
- **Nature:** Fanatic
- **Demeanor:** Penitent
- **Advantages:** Willpower 4; Blood Pool 12/15; The Path of Christ 4

## Appearance

A young woman of obviously Mediterranean ancestry - dark hair, large, dark, unblinking eyes, and pale olive skin. She dresses as simply but nicely as she can, not anything of the noble class, more of what passes for the middle class in the medieval period. She wears a cross at her throat and a medallion for Saint Theodosia.

## Background

**Theodosia History**

As Theodosia's surname indicated, her roots are deeply tied to the fortunes of Constantinople, the great imperial city of the East. Her ancestors were not great nobles or generals, however, they were the artisans who made the jewel of the Roman Empire shine. For generations, they had worked in royal palaces and grand churches, funded by the gifts of the nobility seeking to show off the prestige and wealth of the only true city in Europe at the time. It meant that Theodosia grew up in relative comfort compared to many but with the anonymity of being nothing more than a well-off artisan's daughter.

She was still a young girl when the Frankish brutes calling themselves Crusaders arrived in her city. Most thought they were there to pass on to the lands of Jerusalem further to the south, and none knew the thieves in Venice contracted them to rape and plunder a vulnerable Constantinople. Theodosia and her family hid as the devils ransacked the city, carrying off its treasures to the hated Doge. She has never forgotten the sound of her father weeping as they stood in the _Hagia Theodosia, _the very church her grandfather and great-grandfather had worked to make so beautiful, stripped of its glory by those who violated that sacred place. Whether it was the stress and terror of those horrible days, or the anguish of seeing the work of generations of her family destroyed, it was that moment she had her first vision, of Saint Theodosia, for whom she was named, shining brightly upon the city that she held as one of her sacred places, restoring it with as a gift to God. When she awoke, she had sketched out the sacred image of the blessed saint upon one of the ruined walls, much to the astonishment of her father and the priests with them. It was determined that what Theodosia experienced was a divine gift and that her patron saint touched her to see into the divine world, and given visions of God and his saints, which she dually painted into icons. She never questioned such visions, as she understood that God was unknowable and that the mind of the divine was not for mortals to understand. Her visions were mere glimpses of God's divine plan, pieces that only she could see and translate into something that other human beings could understand. She was merely a vessel by which such divine communications could be shared and disseminated in an understandable and digestible form for those who sought God, and her place was never to attempt to understand God's purpose in them. For many years, Theodosia created such icons, seeing it as her sacred duty to help rebuild what was lost, tapping into such visions to help restore the lost glory of God's kingdom on earth.

Theodosia gained a reputation among sacred quarters of the city, and while some urged her to dedicate her gift to God in sacred orders, Theodosia never sought the monastic life. She felt her purpose was to do what she could to restore the glory of Constantinople and not the spiritual life. Her visions, however, attracted the attention of a monk all the same. Yahyeh came to her to speak about what she saw of the visions that guided her hand to create the sacred images, and the pair had many long conversations about it. Given the sacred vows he took, Theodosia never questioned the fact that she only ever saw him in the darkness of night, nor the fact that he seemed strangely obsessed with her gift. It was only when he decided to pass on his dark powers to her that she came to fully understand the madness of Yahyeh, but by then, it was too late, she was now drawn into that same madness.

Theodosia had gained some notoriety in Constantinople for her gifts, which was why Yahya had been allowed to bring her across into the ranks of the undead, granted permission by the divine patriarch, Michael. Theodosia's sire promised the ancient and powerful Toreador that Theodosia would be a perfect tool to use in the furthering of Michael's Dream, the vision he had for the utopia he was continuously shaping Constantinople into. As someone whose who family had been shaped by Michael's vision, Theodosia readily agreed, seeing in Michael's Dream a hint of the greater possibilities of which she had only glimpsed briefly as a mortal. She threw herself into the effort, eager to aid the patriarch. But her sire, a restless soul, was seeking something else. He began to wander again, and eventually left Constantinople all together decades ago, in search of new religious perspectives by which to understand the questions that troubled him. Theodosia was left behind in the city, with the patriarch and his madness, attempting to see the road that lay ahead for Michael's Dream. But increasingly her visions simply led nowhere, or to darkness, and incensed, Michael lost all patience with her and banished her from her beloved home. For the first time in two centuries, she turned her back on the shining city, into an uncertain future.

Since she last saw him, her sire has settled in Prague, with its religious fervor, and settled into his madness. He has begun to recall all of his childer to him, convinced that he nearly has the pieces of what he's been searching for, that God is speaking directly to him. While others of her kin are circumspect in answering his call, Theodosia, left without a home and with nowhere else to go, has decided to follow his summons. She does this with great trepidation as she fears what Yahyeh is attempting to do. Theodosia does not believe that one should attempt to read into divine prophecy, as humans - mortal or otherwise - are creations, unable to fully comprehend the full scope of the divine. Her sire is playing with holy fire, and it worries Theodosia, even as she makes her way to Prague to do his bidding.

## Derangement

Visions

## Attributes

| Trait | Rating |
|---|---:|
| Strength | 2 |
| Dexterity | 2 |
| Stamina | 2 |
| Charisma | 3 |
| Manipulation (Inspiring) | 4 |
| Appearance | 2 |
| Perception (Uncanny insight) | 4 |
| Intelligence | 3 |
| Wits (Calm focus) | 4 |

## Abilities

**Talents:** Alertness 2, Awareness 3, Empathy 3, Expression (Conversation) 2, Subterfuge 1

**Skills:** Animal Ken 1, craft (Art) 4, Etiquette 2, Melee 2, Stealth 3, Survival 1

**Knowledges:** Academics (Arts) 1, Enigmas 1, Investigation 1, Law (Contracts) 1, Occult 1, Politics 1

## Experiences

- **Gained experience**
  Game 1
- **Gained experience**
  Game 2
- **Game 3**
  Game 3
- **Gained experience**
  Character Write up with laura
- **Spent experience**
  Bought Obfuscate 2
- **Spent experience**
  Bought Auspex 2
- **Gained experience**

## Features, backgrounds, merits, and flaws

- **Generation** (4)
- **Multi-Lingua** (2)
  Languages: Czech, Byzantine Greek, Latin.
- **Literacy** (1); DA 20
  Literacy (1 point): You are a learned scholar, able to both read and write. The Merit assumes that you can read and write with fluency in your native tongue, as well as Latin and any languages you may have gained through the Multi-Lingual Merit. In some chronicles, literacy can be assumed. In these cases, Illiteracy becomes a 1-point Flaw.
- **Prey Exclusion** (1)
  Prey Exclusion (1 point): For whatever reason you choose, you refuse to feed upon a certain type of person. If, by some unfortunate circumstance, you prey upon such a person, you frenzy immediately and must make a Degeneration check. Ventrue may only take this Flaw for a subset of their particular feeding stock. For example, a Ventrue might feed from "virgin men," but will not feed from Jewish virgin men.
- **Outsider** (1)
  The difficulty to applicable Social rolls increases by one.
- **Resources** (1)
  Money she is gifted from icons.
- **Allies** (1)
  A local monk who knows of my icon work. (Need to think of a name for this fellow.)
- **Fear of the Devil** (1)
  Your religious beliefs may be particularly strong or all but nonexistent, and your experience and intellect may offer all kinds of reassurances - but still, you harbor a deep-rooted, irrational fear that someday, for some reason, you will be dragged down to an eternity of torment at the hands of the Devil. This panic may be triggered by anything you associate with "black magic" or by anything violating the teachings of the Church and obviously will make your interactions with vampires considerably more complex. Although the degree to which you keep your feelings under control may vary, note that this terror is not a conscious choice on your character's part and cannot be "reasoned" away over time. Like many Flaws, Fear of the Devil is presented as more of an opportunity for roleplaying than min-maxing a particular character.
- **Unblinking Vigil** (1)
  +1 to the difficulty of friendly social interactions with anyone whose player makes a successful Perception + Alertness roll at difficulty 8 to notice.
- **Infectious** (3)
  Madness flows within your blood, but your bite carries a taint as well. Mortals take a temporary derangement for every three points of blood you take from them. The derangement stays until the mortal restores the lost blood.
- **Blood Connoisseur** (3)
  With a single whiff or tasted drop, you can learn much about a given sample of blood. Your refined palette can even detect the presence of foreign substances or taint. Whenever you analyze a sample, roll Perception + Alertness (diff 6). Each success allows you to glean one fact about the blood, such as its potency, freshness, species, or whether is has been poisoned or contaminated with disease. If multiple successes are spent learning a single fact, additional details may be unveiled at the Storyteller's discretion. Note that this Merit does not confer a preternatural awareness like the Rego Vitae power "A Taste for Blood." Characters who have never tasted arsenic or Lupine blood cannot recognize either - although they may discern such samples as poisoned or non-human. In no case may characters discern supernatural properties like clan or generation. As always, the Storyteller determines what information a character may learn.
- **New boon**
- **Yahya al-Qamar** (3)

## Melee Weapons

- **Bite**; V20 276
  This maneuver is available only to vampires (or other supernatural creatures with sharp teeth, such as werewolves). A bite maneuver is a “combat” bite, intended to cause damage rather than drain blood. Bite damage is aggravated. To use a bite attack, the vampire must first perform a successful clinch, hold, or tackle maneuver (see below). On the turn following the successful attack, the player may declare the bite attempt and make a roll using the modifiers below.
  
  Alternatively, a player can declare her vampire’s bite to be a “Kiss” attack. A Kiss is resolved in the same way as a normal bite, but inflicts no health levels of damage. Upon connecting with a Kiss, the vampire may begin to drain the victim’s blood at the normal rate, and the victim is typically helpless to resist (see p. 269 for specifics). Following the Kiss, a vampire may, if she chooses, lick the puncture wound of the Kiss closed, thereby removing any evidence that she has fed.
- **Bite (Kiss)**; V20 276
  This maneuver is available only to vampires (or other supernatural creatures with sharp teeth, such as werewolves). A bite maneuver is a “combat” bite, intended to cause damage rather than drain blood. Bite damage is aggravated. To use a bite attack, the vampire must first perform a successful clinch, hold, or tackle maneuver (see below). On the turn following the successful attack, the player may declare the bite attempt and make a roll using the modifiers below.
  
  Alternatively, a player can declare her vampire’s bite to be a “Kiss” attack. A Kiss is resolved in the same way as a normal bite, but inflicts no health levels of damage. Upon connecting with a Kiss, the vampire may begin to drain the victim’s blood at the normal rate, and the victim is typically helpless to resist (see p. 269 for specifics). Following the Kiss, a vampire may, if she chooses, lick the puncture wound of the Kiss closed, thereby removing any evidence that she has fed.
- **Clinch**; V20 276
  Clinch: On a successful attack roll, the attacker goes into a clinch with the target. In the first turn, the attacker may roll Strength damage. In each subsequent turn, combatants act on their orders in the initiative. A combatant can inflict Strength damage automatically or attempt to escape the clinch. No other actions are allowed until one combatant breaks free. To escape a clinch, make a resisted Strength + Brawl roll against the opponent. If the escaping character has more successes, she breaks free; if not, the characters continue to grapple in the next turn.
  
  Traits: Strength + Brawl
  Difficulty: Normal
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: Strength
- **Hold**; V20 276
  Hold: This attack inflicts no damage, as the intent is to immobilize rather than injure the subject. On a successful roll, the attacker holds the target until the subject’s next action. At that time, both combatants roll resisted Strength + Brawl actions; the subject remains immobilized (able to take no other action) until she rolls more successes than the attacker does.
  
  Traits: Strength + Brawl
  Difficulty: Normal
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: None
- **Kick**; V20 276
  Kicks range from simple front kicks to aerial spins. The base attack is at +1 difficulty and inflicts the attacker’s Strength +1 in damage. These ratings may be modified further at the Storyteller’s discretion, increasing in damage and/or difficulty as the maneuver increases in complexity.
  
  Ability: Dexterity + Brawl
  Difficulty: +1
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: Strength +1
- **Strike**; V20 276
  The attacker lashes out with a fist. The base attack is a standard action and inflicts the character’s Strength in damage. The Storyteller may adjust the difficulty and/or damage depending on the type of punch: hook, jab, haymaker, karate strike.
  
  Traits: Dexterity + Brawl
  Difficulty: Normal
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: Strength
- **Sweep**; V20 276
  Sweep: The attacker uses her own legs to knock the legs out from under her opponent. The target takes Strength damage and must roll Dexterity + Athletics (difficulty 8) or suffer a knockdown (see “Maneuver
  Complications,” p. 279).
  
  The attacker can also use a staff, chain, or similar implement to perform a sweep. The effect is the same, although the target takes damage per the weapon type.
  
  Traits: Dexterity + Brawl/Melee
  Difficulty: +1
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: Str; knockdown

## Disciplines and powers

- **○ Seeing the Unseen** (0); V20 142
  **• Obfuscate:** When a vampire tries to use her heightened perceptions to notice a Kindred hidden with Obfuscate, she detects the subject's presence if her Auspex rating is higher than his Obfuscate, and she succeeds at a Perception + Awareness roll (difficulty equals 7 minus the number of dots by which her Auspex exceeds his Obfuscate). Conversely, if the target's Obfuscate outranks her Auspex, he remains undiscovered. If the two ratings are equal, both characters make a resisted roll of Perception + Awareness (Auspex user) against Manipulation + Subterfuge (Obfuscate user). The difficulty for both rolls is 7, and the character with the most successes wins.
  
  **• Chimerstry: **Likewise, vampires with Auspex may seek to penetrate illusions created with Chimerstry. The Auspex-wielder must actively seek to pierce the illusion (i.e., the player must tell the Storyteller that his character is trying to detect an illusion). The Auspex-user and Chimerstry-wielder then compare relative ratings, per Obfuscate, above. The process is otherwise identical to piercing Obfuscate.
  
  **• Other Powers: **Since the powers of beings like magi and wraiths function differently from vampiric Disciplines, a simple comparison of relative ratings isn't applicable. To keep things simple, both characters make a resisted roll. The vampire rolls Perception + Awareness, while the subject rolls Manipulation + Subterfuge. Again, the difficulty is 7, and the character with the most successes wins.
- **Auspex** (0); V20 134
  Auspex gives the vampire uncanny sensory abilities. She starts with the capacity to heighten her natural senses significantly, but as she grows in power, she can perceive psychic auras and read the thoughts of another being. Auspex can also pierce through mental illusions such as those created by Obfuscate -- see the sidebar "Seeing the Unseen" on p. 142 for more.
  
  However, a vampire with Auspex needs to be careful. Her increased sensory sensitivity can cause her to be drawn in by beautiful things or stunned by loud noises or pungent smells. Sudden or dynamic events can disorient an Auspex-using character unless her player makes a Willpower roll to block them out (difficulty of at least 4, although the more potent the source of distraction, the higher the difficulty). Failure overwhelms the character's senses, making her oblivious to her surroundings for a turn or two. While the Malkavians and Toreador are more prone to these kinds of distractions, the Tremere and Tzimisce aren't immune.
  
  Dots in Perception are very useful for using Auspex powers, as more successes help the character gain more sensory information.
- **● Heightened Senses** (1); V20 134
  It takes a reflexive action to activate this ability, but no roll or other cost is required. In certain circumstances, dice rolls associated with using the character's sense (such as Perception + Alertness) decrease in difficulty by a number equal to the character's Auspex rating when the power is engaged.
  
  The Storyteller may also use this power to see if the character perceives a threat. In this case, the Storyteller privately rolls the character's unmodified Auspex rating, applying whatever difficulty he feels best suits the circumstances. For example, sensing that a pistol is pointed at the back of the character's head may require a roll of difficulty 5, while the sudden realization that a rival for Primogen is planning her assassination may require a 9. Note that even this "precognition" comes only as a result of interpreting details the Kindred is able to notice. It's not an all-purpose insight or miraculous revelation.
  
  At the character's discretion, she may selectively heighten one specific sense, rather than leaving them all on. In these cases, the difficulty to perceive stimuli using that sense drops by one, but the difficulty to avoid distraction or temporary bedazzlement increases by one.
  
  This power does not let characters see in pitch darkness, as does Eyes of the Beast (p. 199), but it does reduce difficulty penalties to act in such darkness from +2 to +1, and the character may make ranged attacks in pitch darkness if she can hear, smell, or otherwise detect her foe.
- **Dementation**; V20 148
  Dementation is the Discipline that allows a vampire to focus and channel madness into the minds of those around him. Though it's the natural legacy of the Malkavians, practitioners of Dementation need not actually be mad to use the Discipline... but it helps.
  
  Disturbingly, Dementation doesn't actually make their victims mad, but rather it seems to break down the doors to the hidden darkness of the target's mind, releasing into the open whatever is found there. The Malkavians claim that this is because insanity is the next logical step in mental evolution, a transhumanist advancement of what modern people consider consciousness. Other Kindred scoff that this reasoning is an outright justification for the chaos that Dementation brings. They don't scoff too loudly, however, lest the Malkavian advance their consciousness next.
- **● Passion** (1); V20 148
  The character talks to her victim, and the vampire's player rolls Charisma + Empathy (difficulty equals the victim's Humanity or Path rating). The number of successes determines the duration of the altered state of feeling. Effects of this power might include one- or two-point additions or subtractions to difficulties of frenzy rolls, Virtue rolls, rolls to resist Presence powers, etc.
  
  **Successes**
  **Result**
  
  1 Success
  One turn
  
  2 Successes
  One hour
  
  3 Successes
  One night
  
  4 Successes
  One week
  
  5 Successes
  One month
  
  6+ Successes
  Three months
- **●● The Haunting** (2); V20 148
  After the vampire speaks to the victim, the player spends a blood point and rolls Manipulation + Subterfuge (difficulty of his victim's Perception + Self-Control/Instinct). The number of successes determines the length of the sensory "visitations." The precise effects are up to the Storyteller, though particularly eerie or harrowing apparitions can certainly reduce dice pools for a turn or two after the manifestation.
  
  1 Success
  One night
  
  2 Successes
  Two nights
  
  3 Successes
  One week
  
  4 Successes
  One month
  
  5 Successes
  Three months
  
  6+ Successes
  One year
- **Obfuscate** (0); VDA 224
  To many common men and women living in the 13th century, the Devil lurks in every shadow and hides behind every corner. Evil is ever-present and a threat that can rarely be seen with your eyes.
  In the case of those Cainites who have mastered Obfuscate, this paranoia is justified. While evil may be debatable, Obfuscate makes it so that a gifted vampire could be lurking in the darkness ready to strike or flee at any time. The monster must be close at hand to accomplish this, no more than five meters per dots of Wits + Stealth from their would-be victims.
  Most mortals, even those trained in Awareness, cannot pierce the monster's deception. Some unique individuals may be able to see straight through these lies; children, horses, dogs, some cats, and very few religious visionaries are unaffected by Obfuscate, but this is all at Storyteller discretion.
  Cainite with the Discipline of Auspex may be able to pierce a Cainite's Obfuscate. For more, see Auspex on p. 195.
  Most Obfuscate powers last a scene once activated,unless willed away by the Cainite. They take no particular concentration to uphold once raised.
- **● The Silence of Death** (1); VDA 225
  There is no roll. The power creates a zone of absolute silence around the character, with a maximum radius of 7 meters. No sound occurs inside this zone, though sounds originating outside the area of effect may be heard by anyone in it. Additionally, if the player spends two blood points and the character spends five turns in concentration in a room or enclosure, the silence remains in it after the character leaves the room and lasts until she wills it to end. A character can't create more than one zone of silence, so if she wishes to use Silence of Death on herself (or curse another room), the zone in the first room fades automatically.
- **●● Aura Perception** (2); V20 135
  After the character stares at the subject for at least a few seconds, the player rolls Perception + Empathy (difficulty 8); each success indicates how much of the subject's aura the character sees and understands (see the table below). A failure indicates that the play of colors and patterns yields no prevailing impression. A botch indicates a false or erroneous interpretation. The Storyteller may wish to make this roll, thus keeping the player in the dark as to the veracity of the character's interpretation.
  
  **Successes**
  **Result**
  
  1 Success
  Can distinguish only the shade (pale or bright).
  
  2 Successes
  Can distinguish the main color.
  
  3 Successes
  Can recognize the color patterns.
  
  4 Successes
  Can detect subtle shifts.
  
  5 Successes
  Can identify mixtures of color and pattern.
  
  The Aura Colors chart offers some example ideas of common colors and the emotions they reflect that Storytellers can use. Note that it is nearly impossible to determine with certainty if a particular character is lying or not with this power - vampires are inherently deceitful by nature, but even mortals might react with anxiety to questions while still being truthful. It is, however, helpful in determine the target's emotional state, which might lead the vampire to decide that a particular target is suspicious.
  
  A character may choose to perform a very cursory aura scan of a large area like a nightclub's dance floor or the audience in a gallery. In this case, the player decides which characteristic of auras she's looking for, and that's the only information she's able to glean if the roll is successful. (At the Storyteller's discretion, on this general scan roll, more successes on the roll may more quickly yield what the character seeks.) For example, the player may specify, "Who's the most nervous person in attendance?" or "Are there any vampirically pale auras among the CEO's entourage?" Thereafter, the player may narrow down her scrutiny of a single individual, with an additional roll as normal.
  
  The character may focus in on a particular subject's aura only once per scene with any degree of clarity. Any subsequent attempts that result in failure should be considered botches. It is very easy for the character to imagine seeing what she wants to see when judging someone's intentions. After 24 hours, the character may try again at no penalty.
  
  It is possible, though difficult, to sense the aura of a being who is otherwise invisible to normal sight. Refer to "Seeing the Unseen," p. 142, for more information.
- **●● Unseen Presence** (2); VDA 225
  There is no roll necessary to conjure an Unseen Presence. Should a Cainite draw attention to herself by sneak attack, blunder, or speech, accidental attention will call for a Wits + Stealth roll, difficulty from 5 to 9, based on the amount of noise or calamity the Cainite has caused. Willful attempts to violate the secrecy of Unseen Presence while still maintaining it may call for a high difficulty and multiple successes. For example, whispering to an enemy without giving away her location would require three or so successes at a difficulty of 7.
  Even when seen or unseen, a secondary use of this power allows a Cainite to conceal small items already on her person, or with a successful Manipulation + Subterfuge from 5 to 9, based on the amount of observation she can grasp and conceal an easily moved item.
- **● Malleable Visage** (1); V20 241
  The player must spend a blood point for each body part to be changed, then roll Intelligence + Medicine (difficulty 6). To duplicate another person or voice requires a Perception + Medicine roll (difficulty 8), and five successes are required for a flawless copy; fewer successes leave minute (or not-so-minute) flaws. Increasing one's Appearance Trait has a difficulty of 9, and the vampire must spend an additional blood point for each dot of Appearance increased beyond their natural total. A botch permanently reduces the Attribute by one.
- **Vicissitude** (0); V20 241
  **Note:** Nosferatu _always_ "heal" Vicissitude alterations, at least the ones that make them better-looking. The ancient curse of the Clan may not be circumvented through Vicissitude. The same applies to physical deformities from the Gangrel Clan weakness.
  
  Body Crafts
  Vampires who wish to use Vicissitude well often specialize their knowledge of Medicine in an art known as Body Crafts. This specialization enables its possessor to make all manner of alterations to living and dead flesh and bone. It also gives insight into more mundane techniques; many Tzimisce are skilled at flaying, bone-carving, embalming, taxidermy, tattooing, and piercing.
- **Eyes of Chaos** (3); V20 - Dark Ages
  Fleeting clarity hides within insanity; a shattered mirror reflects more than one unbroken one. This level of Dementation is responsible for the Malkavians' reputation of gleaning hidden truths, and their tendency to speak these truths to power. She may scrutinize the "patterns" of personal interaction or even random events in nature itself to reveal hidden truths about the situation she's in. The character need not as a question - the pattern is devious at even a moment's glance.
  
  System: This power may only be used when scrutnizing a complex pattern or event, such as the interactions of a vampiric court or the clash of an army. Analysing the blood spatter flicked from one's fingers or the spill of entrails works just as well, however. Roll Perception + Enigmas, the difficulty varied based on available information and the campire's familiarity on available information and the vampire's familiarity with the situation. A court in the vampire's home culture and domain might be difficutly 5, while a foreign court (or the clash of two freign armies), to difficulty 7. A completely alien encounter (encountering mysterious Cathayan vampires) or just meeting an Anda would be difficulty 9.
  
  The players ask the Storytellers direct questions about the situation at hand. This can virtually be anything related to the hidden truth. The answers are related to the player directly and to the character via and to the characters via metaphors and imagery, with more successes providing a clearer and more complete answer. Failure produces imagery unrelated to the matter at hand, while a botch on the roll relates a completely untrue but nevertheless believable, which may explain some of the more radical Malkavian behavior. Viable questions may include, but not limited to:
  
  - A persons Nature and Demeanor.
  
  - What is the worst choice I could make?
  
  - What is the safest choice I could make?
  
  - Is this person being controlled by another?
  
  - What the underlying or hidden message?
  
  - How relevant are our overarching goals to this scene?
- **Pick a new out of clan discipline.**
  P

- Actor folder: Players

## Session notes

- [[Game 1]] - uses visions and supernatural perception during the haven investigation.
- [[Game 2]] - is overwhelmed by nature spirits at [[Sabina Premysl's Cottage]] where the Gauntlet is extremely thin.

## Game 3-4 notes

- In [[Game 3]], Theodosia uses Dementation or Passion to deaden the gargoyle's emotional state.
- At [[Public Elysium]], she speaks with [[Khalid Mansur]].
- In [[Game 4]], she uses Passion to calm the Cappadocian negotiator long enough for the coterie to exit safely.

## Game 5-8 notes

- In [[Game 6]], Theodosia suffers [[Theodosia's Tzimisce Infection]] after praying over Radomir's stored blood.
- [[Ecaterina the Wise]] calms her and shares visions of the [[Promethean Dream]].
- In [[Game 7]], Theodosia's voice has changed and she can use Vicissitude; [[Dragan Premysl]] resets her voice.
- In [[Game 8]], she helps investigate the [[Impossible River]] and the Tremere parley aftermath.

## Chronicle 1 finale notes

- Looks into the [[Labyrinth Chasm]] and suffers spiritual damage, leaving her eyes black-on-black.
- Her tainted blood recoils during the [[First Fireflower Valderie]].
- In [[Game 14]], she interprets the land-spirits through a Christian theological lens rather than simply calling them pagan.
- Discussed as a possible abbot or seer figure in [[Pack Roles After Prague]], though her Tzimisce taint complicates leadership.
playerText:
# Theodosia Byzantios

![Theodosia Byzantios](/wiki/media/Theodosia-Byzantios.png)

## Overview

Theodosia Byzantios imported from the Foundry Ashes of Ages world (Player Characters, Vampire). Vampire | Nature: Fanatic | Demeanor: Penitent.

## Foundry details

- **Actor type:** Vampire
- **Foundry folder:** Players
- **Foundry name note:** Jenn
- **Original Foundry name:** Theodosia Byzantios (Jenn)
- **Nature:** Fanatic
- **Demeanor:** Penitent
- **Advantages:** Willpower 4; Blood Pool 12/15; The Path of Christ 4

## Appearance

A young woman of obviously Mediterranean ancestry - dark hair, large, dark, unblinking eyes, and pale olive skin. She dresses as simply but nicely as she can, not anything of the noble class, more of what passes for the middle class in the medieval period. She wears a cross at her throat and a medallion for Saint Theodosia.

## Background

**Theodosia History**

As Theodosia's surname indicated, her roots are deeply tied to the fortunes of Constantinople, the great imperial city of the East. Her ancestors were not great nobles or generals, however, they were the artisans who made the jewel of the Roman Empire shine. For generations, they had worked in royal palaces and grand churches, funded by the gifts of the nobility seeking to show off the prestige and wealth of the only true city in Europe at the time. It meant that Theodosia grew up in relative comfort compared to many but with the anonymity of being nothing more than a well-off artisan's daughter.

She was still a young girl when the Frankish brutes calling themselves Crusaders arrived in her city. Most thought they were there to pass on to the lands of Jerusalem further to the south, and none knew the thieves in Venice contracted them to rape and plunder a vulnerable Constantinople. Theodosia and her family hid as the devils ransacked the city, carrying off its treasures to the hated Doge. She has never forgotten the sound of her father weeping as they stood in the _Hagia Theodosia, _the very church her grandfather and great-grandfather had worked to make so beautiful, stripped of its glory by those who violated that sacred place. Whether it was the stress and terror of those horrible days, or the anguish of seeing the work of generations of her family destroyed, it was that moment she had her first vision, of Saint Theodosia, for whom she was named, shining brightly upon the city that she held as one of her sacred places, restoring it with as a gift to God. When she awoke, she had sketched out the sacred image of the blessed saint upon one of the ruined walls, much to the astonishment of her father and the priests with them. It was determined that what Theodosia experienced was a divine gift and that her patron saint touched her to see into the divine world, and given visions of God and his saints, which she dually painted into icons. She never questioned such visions, as she understood that God was unknowable and that the mind of the divine was not for mortals to understand. Her visions were mere glimpses of God's divine plan, pieces that only she could see and translate into something that other human beings could understand. She was merely a vessel by which such divine communications could be shared and disseminated in an understandable and digestible form for those who sought God, and her place was never to attempt to understand God's purpose in them. For many years, Theodosia created such icons, seeing it as her sacred duty to help rebuild what was lost, tapping into such visions to help restore the lost glory of God's kingdom on earth.

Theodosia gained a reputation among sacred quarters of the city, and while some urged her to dedicate her gift to God in sacred orders, Theodosia never sought the monastic life. She felt her purpose was to do what she could to restore the glory of Constantinople and not the spiritual life. Her visions, however, attracted the attention of a monk all the same. Yahyeh came to her to speak about what she saw of the visions that guided her hand to create the sacred images, and the pair had many long conversations about it. Given the sacred vows he took, Theodosia never questioned the fact that she only ever saw him in the darkness of night, nor the fact that he seemed strangely obsessed with her gift. It was only when he decided to pass on his dark powers to her that she came to fully understand the madness of Yahyeh, but by then, it was too late, she was now drawn into that same madness.

Theodosia had gained some notoriety in Constantinople for her gifts, which was why Yahya had been allowed to bring her across into the ranks of the undead, granted permission by the divine patriarch, Michael. Theodosia's sire promised the ancient and powerful Toreador that Theodosia would be a perfect tool to use in the furthering of Michael's Dream, the vision he had for the utopia he was continuously shaping Constantinople into. As someone whose who family had been shaped by Michael's vision, Theodosia readily agreed, seeing in Michael's Dream a hint of the greater possibilities of which she had only glimpsed briefly as a mortal. She threw herself into the effort, eager to aid the patriarch. But her sire, a restless soul, was seeking something else. He began to wander again, and eventually left Constantinople all together decades ago, in search of new religious perspectives by which to understand the questions that troubled him. Theodosia was left behind in the city, with the patriarch and his madness, attempting to see the road that lay ahead for Michael's Dream. But increasingly her visions simply led nowhere, or to darkness, and incensed, Michael lost all patience with her and banished her from her beloved home. For the first time in two centuries, she turned her back on the shining city, into an uncertain future.

Since she last saw him, her sire has settled in Prague, with its religious fervor, and settled into his madness. He has begun to recall all of his childer to him, convinced that he nearly has the pieces of what he's been searching for, that God is speaking directly to him. While others of her kin are circumspect in answering his call, Theodosia, left without a home and with nowhere else to go, has decided to follow his summons. She does this with great trepidation as she fears what Yahyeh is attempting to do. Theodosia does not believe that one should attempt to read into divine prophecy, as humans - mortal or otherwise - are creations, unable to fully comprehend the full scope of the divine. Her sire is playing with holy fire, and it worries Theodosia, even as she makes her way to Prague to do his bidding.

## Derangement

Visions

## Features, backgrounds, merits, and flaws

- **Generation** (4)
- **Multi-Lingua** (2)
  Languages: Czech, Byzantine Greek, Latin.
- **Literacy** (1); DA 20
  Literacy (1 point): You are a learned scholar, able to both read and write. The Merit assumes that you can read and write with fluency in your native tongue, as well as Latin and any languages you may have gained through the Multi-Lingual Merit. In some chronicles, literacy can be assumed. In these cases, Illiteracy becomes a 1-point Flaw.
- **Prey Exclusion** (1)
  Prey Exclusion (1 point): For whatever reason you choose, you refuse to feed upon a certain type of person. If, by some unfortunate circumstance, you prey upon such a person, you frenzy immediately and must make a Degeneration check. Ventrue may only take this Flaw for a subset of their particular feeding stock. For example, a Ventrue might feed from "virgin men," but will not feed from Jewish virgin men.
- **Outsider** (1)
  The difficulty to applicable Social rolls increases by one.
- **Resources** (1)
  Money she is gifted from icons.
- **Allies** (1)
  A local monk who knows of my icon work. (Need to think of a name for this fellow.)
- **Fear of the Devil** (1)
  Your religious beliefs may be particularly strong or all but nonexistent, and your experience and intellect may offer all kinds of reassurances - but still, you harbor a deep-rooted, irrational fear that someday, for some reason, you will be dragged down to an eternity of torment at the hands of the Devil. This panic may be triggered by anything you associate with "black magic" or by anything violating the teachings of the Church and obviously will make your interactions with vampires considerably more complex. Although the degree to which you keep your feelings under control may vary, note that this terror is not a conscious choice on your character's part and cannot be "reasoned" away over time. Like many Flaws, Fear of the Devil is presented as more of an opportunity for roleplaying than min-maxing a particular character.
- **Unblinking Vigil** (1)
  +1 to the difficulty of friendly social interactions with anyone whose player makes a successful Perception + Alertness roll at difficulty 8 to notice.
- **Infectious** (3)
  Madness flows within your blood, but your bite carries a taint as well. Mortals take a temporary derangement for every three points of blood you take from them. The derangement stays until the mortal restores the lost blood.
- **Blood Connoisseur** (3)
  With a single whiff or tasted drop, you can learn much about a given sample of blood. Your refined palette can even detect the presence of foreign substances or taint. Whenever you analyze a sample, roll Perception + Alertness (diff 6). Each success allows you to glean one fact about the blood, such as its potency, freshness, species, or whether is has been poisoned or contaminated with disease. If multiple successes are spent learning a single fact, additional details may be unveiled at the Storyteller's discretion. Note that this Merit does not confer a preternatural awareness like the Rego Vitae power "A Taste for Blood." Characters who have never tasted arsenic or Lupine blood cannot recognize either - although they may discern such samples as poisoned or non-human. In no case may characters discern supernatural properties like clan or generation. As always, the Storyteller determines what information a character may learn.
- **New boon**
- **Yahya al-Qamar** (3)

## Melee Weapons

- **Bite**; V20 276
  This maneuver is available only to vampires (or other supernatural creatures with sharp teeth, such as werewolves). A bite maneuver is a “combat” bite, intended to cause damage rather than drain blood. Bite damage is aggravated. To use a bite attack, the vampire must first perform a successful clinch, hold, or tackle maneuver (see below). On the turn following the successful attack, the player may declare the bite attempt and make a roll using the modifiers below.
  
  Alternatively, a player can declare her vampire’s bite to be a “Kiss” attack. A Kiss is resolved in the same way as a normal bite, but inflicts no health levels of damage. Upon connecting with a Kiss, the vampire may begin to drain the victim’s blood at the normal rate, and the victim is typically helpless to resist (see p. 269 for specifics). Following the Kiss, a vampire may, if she chooses, lick the puncture wound of the Kiss closed, thereby removing any evidence that she has fed.
- **Bite (Kiss)**; V20 276
  This maneuver is available only to vampires (or other supernatural creatures with sharp teeth, such as werewolves). A bite maneuver is a “combat” bite, intended to cause damage rather than drain blood. Bite damage is aggravated. To use a bite attack, the vampire must first perform a successful clinch, hold, or tackle maneuver (see below). On the turn following the successful attack, the player may declare the bite attempt and make a roll using the modifiers below.
  
  Alternatively, a player can declare her vampire’s bite to be a “Kiss” attack. A Kiss is resolved in the same way as a normal bite, but inflicts no health levels of damage. Upon connecting with a Kiss, the vampire may begin to drain the victim’s blood at the normal rate, and the victim is typically helpless to resist (see p. 269 for specifics). Following the Kiss, a vampire may, if she chooses, lick the puncture wound of the Kiss closed, thereby removing any evidence that she has fed.
- **Clinch**; V20 276
  Clinch: On a successful attack roll, the attacker goes into a clinch with the target. In the first turn, the attacker may roll Strength damage. In each subsequent turn, combatants act on their orders in the initiative. A combatant can inflict Strength damage automatically or attempt to escape the clinch. No other actions are allowed until one combatant breaks free. To escape a clinch, make a resisted Strength + Brawl roll against the opponent. If the escaping character has more successes, she breaks free; if not, the characters continue to grapple in the next turn.
  
  Traits: Strength + Brawl
  Difficulty: Normal
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: Strength
- **Hold**; V20 276
  Hold: This attack inflicts no damage, as the intent is to immobilize rather than injure the subject. On a successful roll, the attacker holds the target until the subject’s next action. At that time, both combatants roll resisted Strength + Brawl actions; the subject remains immobilized (able to take no other action) until she rolls more successes than the attacker does.
  
  Traits: Strength + Brawl
  Difficulty: Normal
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: None
- **Kick**; V20 276
  Kicks range from simple front kicks to aerial spins. The base attack is at +1 difficulty and inflicts the attacker’s Strength +1 in damage. These ratings may be modified further at the Storyteller’s discretion, increasing in damage and/or difficulty as the maneuver increases in complexity.
  
  Ability: Dexterity + Brawl
  Difficulty: +1
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: Strength +1
- **Strike**; V20 276
  The attacker lashes out with a fist. The base attack is a standard action and inflicts the character’s Strength in damage. The Storyteller may adjust the difficulty and/or damage depending on the type of punch: hook, jab, haymaker, karate strike.
  
  Traits: Dexterity + Brawl
  Difficulty: Normal
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: Strength
- **Sweep**; V20 276
  Sweep: The attacker uses her own legs to knock the legs out from under her opponent. The target takes Strength damage and must roll Dexterity + Athletics (difficulty 8) or suffer a knockdown (see “Maneuver
  Complications,” p. 279).
  
  The attacker can also use a staff, chain, or similar implement to perform a sweep. The effect is the same, although the target takes damage per the weapon type.
  
  Traits: Dexterity + Brawl/Melee
  Difficulty: +1
  Accuracy: Normal
  Damage: Str; knockdown

## Disciplines and powers

- **○ Seeing the Unseen** (0); V20 142
  **• Obfuscate:** When a vampire tries to use her heightened perceptions to notice a Kindred hidden with Obfuscate, she detects the subject's presence if her Auspex rating is higher than his Obfuscate, and she succeeds at a Perception + Awareness roll (difficulty equals 7 minus the number of dots by which her Auspex exceeds his Obfuscate). Conversely, if the target's Obfuscate outranks her Auspex, he remains undiscovered. If the two ratings are equal, both characters make a resisted roll of Perception + Awareness (Auspex user) against Manipulation + Subterfuge (Obfuscate user). The difficulty for both rolls is 7, and the character with the most successes wins.
  
  **• Chimerstry: **Likewise, vampires with Auspex may seek to penetrate illusions created with Chimerstry. The Auspex-wielder must actively seek to pierce the illusion (i.e., the player must tell the Storyteller that his character is trying to detect an illusion). The Auspex-user and Chimerstry-wielder then compare relative ratings, per Obfuscate, above. The process is otherwise identical to piercing Obfuscate.
  
  **• Other Powers: **Since the powers of beings like magi and wraiths function differently from vampiric Disciplines, a simple comparison of relative ratings isn't applicable. To keep things simple, both characters make a resisted roll. The vampire rolls Perception + Awareness, while the subject rolls Manipulation + Subterfuge. Again, the difficulty is 7, and the character with the most successes wins.
- **Auspex** (0); V20 134
  Auspex gives the vampire uncanny sensory abilities. She starts with the capacity to heighten her natural senses significantly, but as she grows in power, she can perceive psychic auras and read the thoughts of another being. Auspex can also pierce through mental illusions such as those created by Obfuscate -- see the sidebar "Seeing the Unseen" on p. 142 for more.
  
  However, a vampire with Auspex needs to be careful. Her increased sensory sensitivity can cause her to be drawn in by beautiful things or stunned by loud noises or pungent smells. Sudden or dynamic events can disorient an Auspex-using character unless her player makes a Willpower roll to block them out (difficulty of at least 4, although the more potent the source of distraction, the higher the difficulty). Failure overwhelms the character's senses, making her oblivious to her surroundings for a turn or two. While the Malkavians and Toreador are more prone to these kinds of distractions, the Tremere and Tzimisce aren't immune.
  
  Dots in Perception are very useful for using Auspex powers, as more successes help the character gain more sensory information.
- **● Heightened Senses** (1); V20 134
  It takes a reflexive action to activate this ability, but no roll or other cost is required. In certain circumstances, dice rolls associated with using the character's sense (such as Perception + Alertness) decrease in difficulty by a number equal to the character's Auspex rating when the power is engaged.
  
  The Storyteller may also use this power to see if the character perceives a threat. In this case, the Storyteller privately rolls the character's unmodified Auspex rating, applying whatever difficulty he feels best suits the circumstances. For example, sensing that a pistol is pointed at the back of the character's head may require a roll of difficulty 5, while the sudden realization that a rival for Primogen is planning her assassination may require a 9. Note that even this "precognition" comes only as a result of interpreting details the Kindred is able to notice. It's not an all-purpose insight or miraculous revelation.
  
  At the character's discretion, she may selectively heighten one specific sense, rather than leaving them all on. In these cases, the difficulty to perceive stimuli using that sense drops by one, but the difficulty to avoid distraction or temporary bedazzlement increases by one.
  
  This power does not let characters see in pitch darkness, as does Eyes of the Beast (p. 199), but it does reduce difficulty penalties to act in such darkness from +2 to +1, and the character may make ranged attacks in pitch darkness if she can hear, smell, or otherwise detect her foe.
- **Dementation**; V20 148
  Dementation is the Discipline that allows a vampire to focus and channel madness into the minds of those around him. Though it's the natural legacy of the Malkavians, practitioners of Dementation need not actually be mad to use the Discipline... but it helps.
  
  Disturbingly, Dementation doesn't actually make their victims mad, but rather it seems to break down the doors to the hidden darkness of the target's mind, releasing into the open whatever is found there. The Malkavians claim that this is because insanity is the next logical step in mental evolution, a transhumanist advancement of what modern people consider consciousness. Other Kindred scoff that this reasoning is an outright justification for the chaos that Dementation brings. They don't scoff too loudly, however, lest the Malkavian advance their consciousness next.
- **● Passion** (1); V20 148
  The character talks to her victim, and the vampire's player rolls Charisma + Empathy (difficulty equals the victim's Humanity or Path rating). The number of successes determines the duration of the altered state of feeling. Effects of this power might include one- or two-point additions or subtractions to difficulties of frenzy rolls, Virtue rolls, rolls to resist Presence powers, etc.
  
  **Successes**
  **Result**
  
  1 Success
  One turn
  
  2 Successes
  One hour
  
  3 Successes
  One night
  
  4 Successes
  One week
  
  5 Successes
  One month
  
  6+ Successes
  Three months
- **●● The Haunting** (2); V20 148
  After the vampire speaks to the victim, the player spends a blood point and rolls Manipulation + Subterfuge (difficulty of his victim's Perception + Self-Control/Instinct). The number of successes determines the length of the sensory "visitations." The precise effects are up to the Storyteller, though particularly eerie or harrowing apparitions can certainly reduce dice pools for a turn or two after the manifestation.
  
  1 Success

  Two nights
  
  3 Successes

  One month
